entries.title: VIPER time / time-zone discrepancy breaks batches & processing
entries.answer: When VIPER and the POS or Site Controller disagree on time or time zone, the site stops behaving: batches won't balance, totals don't collect, SiteOverSite won't send, and the network can refuse to come online. The clocks have to match. On a V910 or V920, set the time zone with the IPConfig tool and correct it if it was wrong at install. Then make sure the Site Controller and VIPER share the same time and zone, because the Site Controller's setting will override VIPER if they differ.
entries.problem: When VIPER's time or time-zone differs from the POS / Site Controller, batch balances and daily processing fail — the site can't send SiteOverSite data, collect totals, or send alarms, and the network may not come online.
entries.context: Topaz, Sapphire, Commander, VIPER. NewPro must run with the same time/time-zone as VIPER; a Site Controller time setting can override VIPER's. (VASC Field Service Bulletin 0315_003.)
entries.solution: Align the time-zone settings. On V910/V920 set the time zone with the IPConfig tool (correct it if it was wrong at initial install). Make the Site Controller and VIPER share the same time/zone so the Site Controller doesn't override VIPER.
entries.keywords: VIPER, time zone, NewPro, batch balance, SiteOverSite, IPConfig, V910, V920, network online

entries.problem: Reference list of register / pos messages/codes and their corrective actions.

﻿

Sales
E1101 - E1293
Numbered Messages Related To Sales
# 	Message 	Description/Action
E1101 	INVALID CASHIER # 	The cashier number entered is incorrect. Re-key the number.
E1102 	INVALID PASSWORD 	The password entered is incorrect. Re-key the information,
E1103 	INVALID DRAWER # 	Entered a drawer number that has not been set up. Key the drawer number again.
E1104 	MOP INVALID/NOT ALLOWED 	Used an MOP soft key that no longer exists. Choose another MOP.
E1105 	LAN UNAVAILABLE, CHECK CABLE 	The Ruby terminal cannot process a network transaction. Check for loose connections on the back of the Ruby terminal.
E1106 	USE VOID LINE/VOID TICKET 	Attempted to use [ERROR CORR] to remove more than the last item from the current transaction. Use [VOID LINE] to remove additional items from the transaction or [VOID TICKET] to remove the entire ticket.
E1107 	INVALID KEY PRESSED 	The wrong key was pressed during a transaction. Wait for the error to clear and press the correct key.
E1108 	INVALID QUANTITY 	The quantity entered is less than minimum or greater than the maximum allowed.
E1109 	PRESET NOT USED 	The soft key has not been defined or the PLU no longer exists.
E1110 	PLU TOO LONG 	PLU entered has too many digits. Key the correct PLU.
E1111 	INVALID PLU 	PLU entered does not exist. Key the correct PLU.
E1112 	ART. NOT FOR SALE 	Article not for sale.
E1113 	NO REFUND ALLOWED 	No refund is allowed on this PLU or in this department.
E1114 	INVALID BARCODE 	Barcode is not recognized for this transaction.
E1115 	INVALID DEPARTMENT 	The department entered in Department Setup function in Car Wash Manager mode does not exist. Define the department in the Department File or choose another department.
E1116 	AMOUNT TOO LARGE 	The dollar amount entered is larger than the maximum allowed for this department or MOP.
E1118 	NO SPLIT TENDER ON FUEL 	Only one MOP allowed for fuel purchases.
E1119 	OPER. NOT ALLOWED 	The attempted operation is not allowed.
E1120 	DO NOT OVERRIDE MOP AMOUNT 	Cannot override the minimum or maximum allowable amount for an MOP.
E1121 	DISCOUNT TOO SMALL 	The discount entered is less than the set minimum amount.
E1122 	DISCOUNT TOO LARGE 	The discount entered is more than the set maximum amount.
E1123 	INVALID NUMBER OF GALLONS (LITERS) 	Fuel discount is not valid for the gallons (liters) entered.
E1124 	PAYROLL RECORD FULL 	Print a Payroll Report.
E1125 	INVALID CHOICE, TRY AGAIN 	The item number entered is not available on the menu.
E1126 	MUST CLOCK OUT FIRST 	Employee attempted to clock in, but has not clocked out yet.
E1127 	MUST CLOCK IN FIRST 	Employee attempted to clock out, but had not clocked in yet.
E1128 	FINISH TRANSACTION 	The sale MUST BE completed because no more line items can be added to the transaction.
E1129 	NO FUEL DEPARTMENT 	Fuel department needs to be set up.
E1130 	MOP CONFLICT 	Customer dispensed fuel at one MOP price level and attempted to pay with an MOP associated with a different price level when the "Ignore MOP conflict" parameter is set to `N' in Fuel Manager > DCR Configuration.
E1131 	AMOUNT TOO SMALL 	The dollar amount entered is less than the minimum set up for the department or MOP.
E1132 	SUB-TOTAL REQUIRED 	Must press [TOTAL] before choosing an MOP.
E1134 	PENDING TRANSACTION 	Cannot complete function because a transaction is pending.
E1135 	DROP MONEY FIRST 	The amount of money in the drawer exceeds the limit set in the MOP File function. Perform a Safe Drop.
E1136 	INVALID CATEGORY NR 	`NR' = number - The item entered does not have a valid category. Enter a valid category number.
E1137 	NO FEE/CHARGES DEPT. 	Fee/Charges department must be set up.
E1138 	INVALID MOP AMOUNT 	The dollar amount entered is not allowed by the selected MOP. This can occur if a dollar amount is entered and credit selected since credit only takes the balance of the sale.
E1139 	INVALID AMOUNT ENTERED 	MOP may not require an amount or the amount entered is less than the minimum or more than the maximum amount allowed for the MOP.
E1140 	AMOUNT REQUIRED 	A dollar amount must be entered.
E1141 	PURCH. NOT ALLOWED 	The customer may not purchase this item because either (1) the date of birth entered for the ID check does not meet the age requirements or (2) a Blue Law restriction has been set up for this item or department at this time on this day.
E1142 	INVALID ENTRY 	An invalid selection is made when a fuel tax exemption is attempted. The fuel tax menu redisplays and the user can re-enter the selection.
E1143 	INVALID DATE, CHECK FORMAT 	Dates MUST BE entered as six digits in the format MMDDYY (month/day/year).
E1144 	INVALID MODIFIER 	The modifier entered is incorrect. Select another modifier.
E1145 	CASH ONLY ALLOWED 	Only cash is allowed in this transaction.
E1150 	REFUND NOT ALLOWED ON CREDIT SALE 	A credit card purchase cannot be refunded in cash.
E1151 	TICKET TOTALS ZERO - Press CASH 	Although the total on the ticket is zero, press [CASH] to complete the transaction.
E1152 	NEGATIVE CHECK AMOUNT NOT ALLOWED 	A check cannot have a negative amount.
E1153 	TOTAL OVER LIMIT 	The transaction amount is more than the network allows on this card.
E1155 	NO PENDING TRANSACTIONS 	Appears if the View Pending Transactions function is selected and there are no suspended transactions.
E1156 	INVALID RECEIPT INFO 	Attempted to do a ticket reprint or ticket recall on a DCR/fuel transaction and the Ruby terminal found the ticket data invalid. Call servicer for assistance.
E1157 	UNABLE TO SUSPEND 	Sale cannot be suspended. Perhaps the maximum number of suspended sales have been exceeded.
E1158 	REPRINT NOT POSSIBLE 	The DCR has a stacked sale and the cashier attempted to reprint the receipt for sale #1. Since the Ruby only keeps the last DCR transaction, a receipt for the first sale cannot be reprinted.
E1159 	INVALID RECEIPT NUMBER 	No fuel receipts available for printing at this pump.
E1160 	MAXIMUM PENDING TRANS. EXCEEDED 	Too many transactions are suspended. Only one credit

E2011 	NO RESPONSE FROM TANK MONITOR 	Selected Tank Monitor Report for printing but the Ruby terminal did not receive a response from the TLS. A servicer should check the communications between the TLS and the Ruby terminal.
E2012 	ERR CLSING MO PRD 	The period could not be closed on the money order device. See the money order terminal documentation for instructions.
E2013 	TANKBANK END OF DAY FAILED 	The end of day process to the third-party host failed. Contact TankBank vendor.

E3018 	NON-COMPATIBLE LDM 	Attempted to start a load module and the LDM file specified was not compatible with other LDMs in the system. This message also displays when either the Start Debugger or Execute Load Module function is chosen. The servicer should investigate.
E3019 	READ-ONLY FILE SYSTEM 	Attempted to start a load module and the medium which holds the LDM file is read-only.
E3022 	OUT OF MEMORY 	Attempted to start a load module and the medium which holds the LDM file does not have enough memory. If this continues to happen call the servicer.
E3023 	RESOURCE IS BUSY 	Attempted to start a load module that is already in use.
E3024 	NOT ENOUGH MEMORY 	Attempted to start a load module and the medium which holds the LDM file does not have enough memory. If this continues to happen call the servicer.
E3025 	CHECKSUM ERROR 	Attempted to start a load module and a checksum test fails. The servicer should investigate.
E3026 	INVALID LDM HEADER 	Attempted to start a load module and its header is invalid. The servicer should investigate.
E3027 	NO LOAD MODULES FOUND ON THIS FILE SYSTEM 	Attempted to start a load module and the medium specified does not contain any LDMs. The servicer should investigate and reinstall the application, if necessary.
E3028 	FILE NOT FOUND 	Attempted to start a load module but it cannot be found on the medium specified. The servicer should investigate.
E3029 	*** Failed!! Error Number <error #> *** 	Attempted to start a load module and an unknown error was encountered. The servicer should investigate.
E3030 	SOFT KEY FILE RESET ERROR 	The Reset Soft Key File function was chosen and the soft key file could not be reset. Try the action again. If this continues to happen call the servicer.
E3031 	EXPANDED PLUS REQUIRES A RUBY CARD 	Attempted to edit the PLU File with expanded PLUs and no Ruby Card is in the Ruby terminal. Place the Ruby Card in the Ruby terminal, reboot, and re-enter the data.
E3032 	MODEM RESET FAILED 	The Reset Modem function could not be reset. Check for loose connections on the back of the Ruby terminal. Call the servicer if connections are good.
E3033 	ERROR IN CARWASH SETUP 	The car wash parameters have not been set up correctly and the Discount Type prompt displays in the PLU Promotion File function. Check the car wash parameters.
E3034 	PLU FILE CHANGE: FILE SYSTEM ERROR 	A major error was encountered while changing the PLU File size. Make sure the proper Ruby Card is installed. If the correct card is installed, have the servicer investigate further.
E3035 	PLU FILE CHANGE FAILED 	An error was encountered while changing the PLU File size. Make sure the proper Ruby Card is installed. If the correct card is installed, have the servicer investigate further.
E3036 	NOT ENOUGH MEM. TO CHANGE PLU FILE 	There is not enough memory to change the PLU File size. Make sure the proper Ruby Card is installed. If the correct card is installed, have the servicer investigate further.
E3037 	INVALID KEY PRESS 	The original Cprintf file could not be accessed. Have the servicer investigate.
E3048 	COULD NOT COPY ALL OF THE FILE 	The Copy Cprintf File function was chosen and the entire file could not be copied. Have the servicer investigate.
E3049 	COULD NOT OPEN CPRINTF FILE COPY 	The Copy Cprintf File function was chosen but the destination file could not be opened

E4005 	INVALID INPUT 	Entered as invalid value.  Wait for the prompt to clear and enter the correct information.
E4005 – cont.  Either may appear. 	ERROR DISABLING CAR WASH 	An attempt was made to disable the car wash but was unsuccessful.  Check the connections.  Call the servicer if the connections are good.
E4006 	ERROR ENABLING CAR WASH 	An attempt was made to enable the car wash but was unsuccessful.  Check the connections.  Call the servicer if the connections are good.
E4007 	PLU ALREADY USED 	Attempted to assign a PLU to a car wash but has already been assigned.  Assign another PLU.
E4008 	NO OPEN PLUS ALLOWED 	Attempted to assign an open PLU to a car wash.  This is not allowed.  Assign another PLU.
E4009 	UNABLE TO READ CAR WASH FILE 	Attempted to assign a PLU to a car wash but the car wash key file cannot be read.  Have the servicer investigate.
E4010 	ONLY CAR WASH ITEMS ALLOWED 	Attempted to assign a PLU to a car wash when the PLU has not been assigned to a car wash department.  Assign the PLU to a car wash department then assign the PLU to a car wash.
E4011 	C/W PORT CONFLICT 	Alarm Line Only:  Another device has been assigned to the car wash port.  Check for correct port assignments.
E4012	CAR WASH ERROR	Alarm Line Only:  The Ruby terminal was unable to communicate wit the car wash device.  The servicer should investigate.

0

DISABLE PRINTER? (Y/N) 	At startup, the Ruby terminal could not access the specified printer. Check for loose connections on the back of the Ruby terminal.

DISABLE PRINTER? (Y/N) 	At startup, the Ruby terminal could not access the specified printer. Check for loose connections on the back of the Ruby terminal.

E7004 	PRINTER COMMS ERROR DISABLE PRINTER? (Y/N) 	At startup, the Ruby terminal could not communicate with the specified printer. Check the connections. If they are good, have the servicer investigate the error. If this stops sales from being run, see "Troubleshooting."

E7006 	INVALID INPUT 	An invalid value was entered. Wait for the prompt to clear then enter the correct information.
E7007 	PLU TOT FILE FULL - RECORD LOST 	Journal Only: Prints when an item is sold and the PLU total file does not have room to store the information. Close the current period. This makes room in the PLU File.
E7008 	DEP TOT FILE FULL - RECORD LOST 	Journal Only: Printed when an item is sold and the department total file no longer has room to store the information about the sale of the item. Close the current period. This makes room in the department file.
E7009 	CAT TOT FILE FULL - RECORD LOST 	Journal Only: Printed when an item is sold and the category total file no longer has room to store the information about the sale of the item. Close the current period. This makes room in the category file.
E7010 	PLU EXCEPT FILE FULL - RECORD LOST 	Journal Only: Printed when an item is sold using price override, PLU not found, or is not scannable and the PLU exception file no longer has room to store the information about the sale of the item. Close the current period. This makes room in the PLU exception file.
E7011 	SAFE DROP FILE FULL - RECORD LOST 	The safe drop prints on the journal, but cannot be saved because it exceeds the maximum safe drops that can be stored. To make room, close the current period.
E7012 	ACCESS DENIED 	User does not have a high enough security level to access a menu item or a VeriFone password is needed. Have someone with a higher security level access these menu items.
E7013 	INVALID SECURITY LEVEL 	A security level which is less than 0 or greater than 9 was entered. Wait for the prompt to clear then enter the proper security level.
E7014 	LAN COMMS ERROR
UNABLE TO DOWNLD <filename> 	The controller was trying to download a file to a workstation and the LAN cable was not connected. Check for loose connections on the back of the Ruby terminal.
E7015 	<file name> DOESN'T EXIST
UNABLE TO RUN SALES
SALES ABORTED 	Journal Only: The controller was trying to download a file to a workstation and the file could not be found.
E7016 	RUBY CARD IS WRITE PROTECTED 	The Ruby terminal tried to write a change to the Ruby Card when an attempt was made to change the number of PLUs. The Ruby Card is write-protected. To locate the write-protect switch, hold the card so that the edge with the holes faces down and the one-sided notch is to the right. The switch is on the top right side of the card. This switch is recessed and the `On' position is towards the right. This switch should always be in the `On' position. If the switch is on, contact the VeriFone Technical Support Center.
E7017 	COULD NOT PUT FILE ON NEW MEDIA 	The Ruby terminal tried to write a change to the Ruby Card when an attempt was made to change the number of PLUs, but there is a problem with the Ruby Card. Check that the card is inserted correctly, reboot the Ruby terminal. Re-enter the data.
E7018 	<file name> MAY HAVE OLD DATA 	Journal Only: This occurs when downloading a file from the controller to a workstation and the file being downloaded may be older than the one already on the workstation. Find the file with the most current information.
E7019 	ERR DOWNLDING <file name> 	Journal Only: An error occurred downloading a file from the controller to the workstation. See the site's procedures for handling this situation.
E7020 	Continue Sales [Y/N] ?
UNABLE TO DOWNLD <file name> 	An error occurred downloading a file from the controller to the workstation. Check the journal for more detail and see the site's procedures for handling this situation.
E7021 	DOWNLD <file name> FAILED
<file name> DOESN'T EXIST
SALES ABORTED 	The controller was trying to download a file to a workstation and the file could not be found. See the site's procedures for handling this situation.
E7022 	TOO MANY PLUS TO BE PUT IN RAM 	Attempted to copy too many PLUs to RAM. A maximum of 5000 is allowed. Insert a Ruby Card with expanded PLUs. Reboot the Ruby terminal.
E7023 	LAN COMMS DOWN 	Journal Only: Attempted to clock in or out or tried to reprint a clock in or out ticket and the LAN was down. See the site's procedures for handling this situation.
E7024 	INVALID INPUT 	Entered an invalid date or time. Enter the information in the proper format.
E7025 	FILE CREATE ERROR: <file index> 	Attempted to create a file using the Create Files function and the file could not be created. See the site's procedures for handling this situation.
E7026 	BAD MONTH 	A number less than 1 or greater than 12 was entered for a month. Enter the correct information.
E7027 	BAD YEAR 	An invalid year was entered. Re-key the year.
E7028 	BAD DAY 	A number less than 1 or greater than the maximum for the specified m

entries.title: Pump "Preset Not Allowed" — pump-side fault, not POS
entries.answer: One pump showing "Preset Not Allowed" while the others are fine, with Verifone already confirming the configuration is clean, points to the dispenser rather than the POS. This is a pump-side fault. There is nothing to change on the Verifone end, so send the pump servicer to that fueling point.
entries.problem: A fueling point (pump 5) shows "Preset Not Allowed."
entries.context: Shell, 4500 Monroe Hwy, Watkinsville GA. Verifone reviewed configuration — correct, no errors on the Verifone side (case 160419-37658).
entries.solution: The fault is on that individual pump (pump side), not the Verifone POS or its configuration. Have the pump/dispenser servicer address that pump; POS config is fine.
entries.keywords: preset not allowed, pump 5, dispenser, Shell, pump-side, configuration correct

entries.title: RubyCI stuck on "CI" status
entries.answer: A RubyCI stuck on "CI" has stalled partway through its load. Drop it into Diagnostics and run the load from there; that is what gets it past the hang.
entries.problem: RubyCI hangs on "CI" status and won't finish loading.
entries.context: 10/23/24, Blake, 310 Peachtree Industrial. Commander/RubyCI.
entries.solution: Load the unit in Diagnostics (diag mode) to recover and reload.
entries.keywords: RubyCI, stuck on CI, diagnostics, load, A.2

entries.title: Ruby2/Topaz 410 stuck at A5 — can't load with Petro Suite Installer
entries.answer: A Ruby2 or Topaz 410 sitting at A5 can't pull its load, and the usual reason is that the Petro Suite Installer isn't reaching it. Put the register in diagnostics (flip the diag switch down and reboot), then connect your laptop to the router and to the register's serial console port. Run the Petro Suite Installer and point it at the default device IP, 192.168.31.193. When it finishes and asks to reboot, flip the diag switch back up first; you may have to power-cycle by hand. If it then parks on A8, go back into diagnostics and work the A8 procedure.
entries.problem: Ruby2/Topaz 410 stuck at status A5; unable to load with the Petro Suite Installer.
entries.context: Commander/Ruby2 and Commander/Topaz 410 (Verifone article 000006131).
entries.solution: 1) Flip the diag switch down and reboot the register. 2) In diag mode, connect a laptop to the router and to the register's serial console port. 3) Start Petro Suite Installer; for Device IP use the default Ruby2/Topaz 410 address **192.168.31.193**. 4) When prompted, put the diag switch back up and reboot (may need a manual reboot). 5) If it then sticks on A8 after the initial reboot, go back into diag mode and continue the A8 procedure.
entries.keywords: A5, Ruby2, Topaz 410, Petro Suite Installer, diag switch, 192.168.31.193, serial console

entries.title: NewPro/Topaz won't load past A8 ("Erase F2" prompt)
entries.answer: A NewPro or Topaz that won't climb past A8, showing the "A8 Erase F2" prompt, is almost always a Compact Flash problem, and a hard reset won't clear it. Start by reformatting the CF and reloading. If it still stops at A8, replace the CF. If a fresh card doesn't move it, the board is next.
entries.problem: NewPro/Topaz won't go past A8 when reloading ("A8 Erase F2 prompt"); persists after a hard reset and reload.
entries.context: Shell rebranding to Texaco, 455 S. Marietta Pkwy (case 151210-18857).
entries.solution: Reformat the Compact Flash (CF) first; if no change, replace the Compact Flash; if still stuck at A8, replace the board.
entries.keywords: A8, NewPro, Topaz, Erase F2, compact flash, CF, reformat, board replace

entries.title: POS alarm: "Locked DB cannot be synchronized"
entries.answer: The "Locked DB cannot be synchronized" alarm comes from two registers colliding on the same address. It shows up when a unit gets loaded in diagnostics on .193, the load address, instead of its own. Move that unit to .101 and reboot, and the databases sync.
entries.problem: POS alarm — "Locked DB cannot be synchronized."
entries.context: 8/27/21, Shiraz, 6083 Norcross-Tucker Citgo. Unit had been loaded in Diagnostics on IP .193.
entries.solution: Change the unit's IP from .193 to .101 and reboot (a second register left on .193 instead of .101 caused the DB sync conflict).
entries.keywords: locked DB, cannot be synchronized, IP 193 101, reboot, RubyCI sync
entries.source: ALARM ON POS LOCKED DB CANNOT BE SYNCRONIZED.xlsx

entries.title: Unwanted "Is this the Correct Amount Y/N" prompt on MX915 after chip removal
entries.answer: That extra "Is this the Correct Amount Y/N" after the customer pulls the chip is driven by the pin pad's base software, not anything you set at the POS; the network owns those prompts. Older bases bring it back (Exxon and Chevron 1.14.35) and newer ones clear it (Chevron 1.14.41). Update the pad to a base that resolves it. If the brand still forces the prompt, that's an ExxonMobil call.
entries.problem: An extra "Is this the Correct Amount Y/N" prompt appears after the customer removes the chip card on MX915 pin pads.
entries.context: Exxon, 925 Loganville Hwy, Bethlehem GA. Lower base versions (Exxon/Chevron 1.14.35) cause it; newer base (Chevron 1.14.41) addressed it. Prompts are controlled by the network/base, not the POS.
entries.solution: Update the pin pad base software to a version that resolves the prompt (e.g., Chevron 1.14.41 or newer). For brand-specific prompt behavior, escalate to ExxonMobil.
entries.keywords: correct amount prompt, MX915, chip removal, base version, 1.14.35, 1.14.41, Exxon, Chevron

entries.title: Apple Pay / contactless (tap) inoperative
entries.answer: When Apple Pay and tap go dead, the pad is missing its contactless software and table. Load ctls1.16.05A4, call Citgo for a table download, and reinitialize the pad. Don't trust the diagnostics test card here; it can fail to read no matter what software is loaded, so confirm with a live tap.
entries.problem: Apple Pay and tap (contactless) not working on the pin pad.
entries.context: Bells Ferry Citgo.
entries.solution: Load contactless software **ctls1.16.05A4**, call Citgo for a table download, and reinitialize the pin pad. Note: a test card may not read in diagnostics regardless of software — verify with a live transaction.
entries.keywords: Apple Pay, contactless, tap, inop, ctls1.16.05A4, table download, reinitialize, Citgo

entries.title: "Bad Grade Spec" — Gilbarco blender configuration
entries.answer: "Bad Grade Spec" on Gilbarco pumps almost always traces to fuel configuration, and it tends to appear right after someone reconfigures fuel. If a servicer was just on site, the fastest fix is to call them back to correct what they changed. Otherwise check product config yourself: every product set up, and the unused "virtual" products pointed at tanks you aren't actually dispensing from (TANK04, TANK05, and so on). Fix the grade-to-tank mapping and the error clears.
entries.problem: "Bad Grade Spec" error when configuring Gilbarco pumps with blending.
entries.context: Gilbarco pumps; Ruby, Sapphire, Topaz. Often appears right after a servicer reconfigures fuel (article 4134).
entries.solution: If a servicer/VASC was onsite just before the error, have them re-check the fuel config. Otherwise verify product config: all products set up, and unused ("virtual") products assigned to tanks NOT used for actual dispensed products (e.g., TANK04, TANK05). Correct the blend/grade-to-tank assignments.
entries.keywords: bad grade spec, Gilbarco, blending, virtual product, tank assignment, fuel config

entries.title: Gilbarco Graphics (VFG) CRIND error codes & setup
entries.problem: Setup information and error codes for Gilbarco graphic CRINDs (DCRs). Verify graphics are installed via Maintenance > Print Installation Information before troubleshooting graphical errors.
entries.solution: Look up the specific code or message in the table below for its description and corrective action.
entries.table:
Error Codes and Information - Gilbarco Graphics                                                Page 1 of 4

  Add to Favorites

                                            Article 1673 Details

                                              Article Title
  Error Codes and Information - Gilbarco Graphics
                                             Problem/Task
      • Setup information
      • Error Codes
                                              Environment
      • Gilbarco pumps
      • Graphic CRINDs (DCRs)
                                                  Cause
  Insufficient User Rights
                                       Customer Solution/Action

  *****************************************************************

  PLEASE VERIFY IN MAINTENACE -> PRINT INSTALLATION INFORMATION TO VALIDATE
  THAT GRAPHICS HAVE BEEN INSTALLED AT THIS LOCATION WHEN DEALING WITH
  GRAPHICAL ERRORS

  VFG Install Script (Choices):

      • DCR Prompt
        Regardless of whether you select a prompt, it will be over-ridden with the default
        application-specific idle graphic.

      • Once the animation sequence is supported, this may change so that it alternates
        between the default graphic and a selected Idle prompt.

      •    DCR Keypad
           Regardless of whether you select a keypad, it will be overridden with the Graphics
          keypad.

      •   Dcr_Graphics
          Select this option to install the Graphics binary and data files

      •    Dcr_Keypad_Graphics
           Select this option if the CRIND has the graphics keypad. There are variations of this.
          The one thing that identifies it is the presence of keys to the left or right of the display
          itself.

  Configuration Choices:

  DCR Attributes:

http://clientservices.veriorder.com/index.cfm?fuseaction=view.articledetail&id=1673             12/4/2015
 Error Codes and Information - Gilbarco Graphics                                           Page 2 of 4

  Graphics Enabled
  Y

  Primary Side
   01
   For each dispenser, one side must be selected as the “Primary” side. The “primary” side
  will be the side to display the messages while it is downloading. It does not seem to
  matter which side is chosen. Example: site has 4 dispensers; 1-2, 3-4, 5-6 and 7-8.
  Primary side for pumps 1 and 2 = 01; for 3 and 4 = 03; for pumps 5 and 6 = 05; for pumps 7
  and 8 = 7.

  Graphics Keypad
  Y
  Y

  Graphics Display
  M
  For Monochrome (only type supported)

  Graphics Printer
  N
  Not yet supported

  DCR Site Parameters:

  Graphic Animation Delay: not used yet (leave at 00)

  Downloading:

      • Cold Start the CRINDs
        Any time you go from software that does not support Graphics to software that does,
        you MUST cold-start the CRINDS. If this is not done, the CRINDS will not download
        properly. Other wierdnesses have been noted as well. It may be necessary if the
        proces is reversed as well (removing graphics flags when it was set up in s/w that
        included the flags but did not support graphics).

      • Initializing Fuel - Messages
        As Fuel is being initialized, a message will display on the primary side of the Graphics
        display “loading 100” through “loading 650”

      • Downloading to the DCRs
        While the download is occurring the “stage” will display on the CRIND. It starts at 50
        and will complete at 650 (depending on how far application goes). This will display
        on CRINDs that have been designated as a PRIMARY CRIND. Both sides will display
        “Loading”. But only the primary side will display a number that indicates where it is
        in the loading process.

  On the demo, it counted from 100 to 650 in increments of 50.

http://clientservices.veriorder.com/index.cfm?fuseaction=view.articledetail&id=1673        12/4/2015
 Error Codes and Information - Gilbarco Graphics                                         Page 3 of 4

      • Broadcasting
        DCRs are downloaded using “Broadcast” method. This means that they are all
        downloaded at the same time. A typical download, even for 1 single displenser) takes
        approximately 12-15 minutes. Each additional dispenser would add something to that
        time. However, because we are broadcasting, the additional time is not significant.

      • Note**: Do not interrupt the download (by making other choices in Fuel Manager,
        rebooting Ruby, etc). It may finish its original download before initiating ANOTHER
        download in order to do it cleanly.

      • Effect of Ruby Reboot on DCRs
        Because the time needed to download graphics is so significant, we now check to see
        if the Graphics information is still in tact when the Ruby comes back from a reboot. If
        the graphics informatin is still there (which would be normal), then the DCRs are not
        downloaded again. The site needs only to wait for normal fuel initialization to
        complete before being able to sell fuel.

  Displaying Car Wash Options with their Prices

      • All features and options of our carwash feature are supported with Gilbarco Graphics.

      • Most applications display the carwash options (menu presentation or single line;
        depending on the option configured). One application (Strpak) will even include the
        price in the item display for each carwash being offered. Please be aware that this is
        NOT CONFIGURABLE; it is software dependent.

      • Ask customer to examine pump display for numeric error code by saying something
        like, “Can you take a look at your pumps outside, and tell me exactly what you see on
        the display?”
      • See Below For Bad Graphics Config Error messages

  Gilbarco Graphics Error Codes

http://clientservices.veriorder.com/index.cfm?fuseaction=view.articledetail&id=1673       12/4/2015
 Error Codes and Information - Gilbarco Graphics

entries.title: Bennett DCT poll errors at all fueling points
entries.answer: Poll errors on every Bennett fueling point usually mean the wrong edit was chosen in Gemstall. Bennett DCTs talk through Wayne files, so the D_DCT_Bennett edit has to load the Wayne drivers (dcrdrv.ldm, catsys.ldm) with the right IoCfg on the COM port. Confirm the VASC picked that edit. Bennett units can hang off Wayne, Gilbarco, Schlumberger, or Tokheim systems, so the file selection is what makes or breaks communication.
entries.problem: DCT poll errors at all fueling points on Bennett DCRs.
entries.context: Bennett DCTs; Ruby, Sapphire. Bennett DCTs use Wayne files to communicate (article 1628).
entries.solution: Verify the VASC chose the correct edit in Gemstall (item D_DCT_Bennett — Wayne dcrdrv.ldm / catsys.ldm files with proper IoCfg on the COM port). Bennett DCTs can attach to other fueling systems (Wayne, Gilbarco, Schlumberger, Tokheim), so the correct file/edit selection is vital.
entries.keywords: Bennett, DCT, poll errors, Gemstall, Wayne files, dcrdrv.ldm, fueling points

entries.title: Bennett DCRs unable to load due to logo
entries.answer: Bennett DCRs don't support a logo, so on any branded app (P66, Shell, anything carrying one) they stall at load step 6 because the logo file blocks them. The fix isn't on your end: call the Helpdesk and have them strip the logo file, and the DCRs load.
entries.problem: Bennett pump DCRs (CI) unable to communicate / stuck loading at "6" during install because they don't support a logo.
entries.context: Ruby CI, Commander; P66/Shell or any app with a logo (article 15358).
entries.solution: Call the Helpdesk to have the logo file removed (Bennett DCRs don't support a logo).
entries.keywords: Bennett, DCR, unable to load, logo, stuck on 6, RubyCI, Commander

entries.title: "BLUE" / $0.00 stuck on dispenser — Commander
entries.answer: A dispenser stuck on BLUE at $0.00 on a Commander needs the fuel side reset. In Fuel Manager, clear pumps on hold, reset the controller, then initialize fuel. Back out to the splash screen and log into Sales again. Run a $1 prepay on that dispenser and cash it out, then cancel the prepay and cash that out too; that clears the stuck state.
entries.problem: Dispenser shows BLUE / $0.00 and is stuck (Commander).
entries.context: Commander; advice from Pat, 6/7/2017.
entries.solution: In Fuel Manager: Clear Pumps on Hold → Reset Controller → Initialize Fuel. Exit all the way to the Splash screen and log back into Sales. Prepay the dispenser for $1 and cash it out; then cancel the prepay and cash it out.
entries.keywords: blue, 0.00, dispenser, Commander, Fuel Manager, clear pumps on hold, reset controller, initialize fuel, prepay

entries.title: Topaz stuck on Diagnostic "Burn In" screen during reload
entries.answer: A Topaz that drops to the diagnostic "Burn In" screen during a reload is held there by its internal battery. Unplug the internal battery and briefly short the terminals to drain it, then load, and it goes through. This is a hardware step, so do it carefully.
entries.problem: Re-loading a Topaz goes to the Diagnostic "Burn In" screen and won't proceed.
entries.context: 4500 Monroe Hwy, Watkinsville Shell, 4/14/2016 (Mark).
entries.solution: Unplug the internal battery and briefly short across the terminals with a wire to clear it, then reload — this allowed the Topaz to load.
entries.keywords: burn in, Topaz, diagnostic screen, internal battery, short terminals, reload

entries.title: Does a Buypass site need a network interconnect (Prysm/Cybera)?
entries.answer: A Buypass site running the standard app does not need a Prysm, a Cybera, or any dedicated network interconnect, which is a common point of confusion on RubyCI installs. Set the site up the way bulletin 1115_002 lays out and it works without the extra box.
entries.problem: Installing a RubyCI at a Buypass site — is a network interconnect box (Prysm or Cybera) required?
entries.context: Buypass site, 4852 Canton Rd, Marietta GA; generic/latest Buypass application (case 160216-8587).
entries.solution: No dedicated network device is required. Set the site up per Verifone bulletin **1115_002**.
entries.keywords: Buypass, network interconnect, Prysm, Cybera, RubyCI, bulletin 1115_002

entries.title: Cabling Connections reference — ENCRYPTED (could not open)
entries.answer: This one is a gap. The cabling-connections workbook is password-protected, so its contents aren't in the database yet. Send the password and the cable-by-cable reference goes in.
entries.problem: A reference workbook of cabling connections by cable number. The file is password-encrypted and could not be extracted.
entries.solution: Provide the file password to include this content. (Flagged as a data gap.)
entries.keywords: cabling, connections, cable number, encrypted, gap, password

entries.title: Reaching a live Verifone agent as a VASC (chat path, F-02)
entries.answer: To get past the "Val" chat bot to a real person, tell it you're a VASC, choose Yes when it offers a transfer, and give your Service ID. That drops you into the queue for a live agent.
entries.problem: Getting transferred from the automated "Val" chat bot to a live agent.
entries.context: Verifone web chat ("Val" assistant), 2/18/2026.
entries.solution: When the bot can't answer, state you are a VASC, choose "Yes" to be transferred, and provide your Service ID to be queued for an agent.
entries.keywords: help desk, VASC, Val bot, agent, service ID, contact Verifone

entries.title: Reaching a live Verifone agent (chat path, P-01)
entries.answer: Same path through the ContactUs/Val bot: say you're a VASC, pick Yes to transfer, and hand over your Service ID. The bot won't answer VASC questions itself, so don't spend time in its menus; just get to the queue.
entries.problem: Same as F-02 — escalating from the "ContactUs/Val" bot to a live agent.
entries.context: Verifone web chat, 3/16/2021. Bot menu: VHQ, Payware Connect, Register/POS, PIN Pads, Portals, Kiosk.
entries.solution: Reply "I am a VASC", choose Yes to transfer, and provide your Service ID; you're placed in queue for the next agent.
entries.keywords: help desk, VASC, ContactUs, agent, service ID, queue

entries.title: CAPK expired — re-download EMV public key (BP)
entries.answer: An expired CAPK (the Certificate Authority Public Key behind EMV) stops chip processing until you pull a fresh one. In Network Manager, open EPS Network Functions and run number 14, Download Public Key, then run EMV Init twice. That reloads the key and clears the expiry.
entries.problem: CAPK (Certificate Authority Public Key) expired.
entries.context: BP software; per JR, 10/4/2019.
entries.solution: In Network Manager → EPS Network Functions → **#14 Download Public Key**, then run **EMV Init twice**.
entries.keywords: CAPK, certificate authority public key, expired, EMV init, download public key, network manager, EPS, BP

entries.title: CAPK warning won't clear — full EMV re-init (Buypass/Valero)
entries.answer: A CAPK warning that survives a normal key download usually needs the whole EMV config rebuilt, not just another download. On a Buypass site (this was Valero 3.10.53): CSR Functions → Network → EPS Network Functions → 13, Perform EMV CA Public Key File Download, then run 3, Table Download — expect it to fail, that's normal here. Now rebuild the config in Configuration Manager → Payment Controller → EPS Config → EPS Global: run EMV Init, Add All, Save. Initialize every DCR from Forecourt → Initialize → DCRs All. Finally log out of all registers and back in; the PIN pads reboot and the warning drops off.
entries.problem: CAPK warning keeps coming back after a standard public-key download.
entries.context: Buypass, Valero version 3.10.53; per KAL 6/2/22.
entries.solution: EPS Network Functions #13 → Table Download (#3, will fail). Then EPS Global → EMV Init → Add All → Save. Forecourt → Initialize → DCRs All. Log registers out and back in (PIN pads reboot).
entries.keywords: CAPK, EMV CA public key, won't clear, EMV init, table download, Buypass, Valero, DCR initialize, PIN pad reboot

entries.title: Cash/credit pricing won't engage — set default MOP to cash
entries.answer: Cash/credit pricing splits the fuel price by how the customer pays, and it simply won't kick in if the pumps default to credit. Set each fueling position's default MOP to cash under CSR Functions → Forecourt → Fuel Config Pending → Fueling Positions. Then, so debit still rings at the credit price, go to CSR Functions → Payment Controller → EPS Configuration → Buypass Config and switch debit from cash to credit. That debit-to-credit step is easy to forget and is usually why it still looks wrong.
entries.problem: Cash/credit fuel pricing would not work until the default MOP was changed to cash.
entries.context: ExxonMobil AutoUpgrade base 51; per Damian 2/25/20. Related POP note (Shell, 4/29/20): default MOP was credit and "Ignore MOP Conflict" was checked.
entries.solution: Forecourt → Fuel Config Pending → Fueling Positions: default MOP = cash for each position. Then Payment Controller → EPS Configuration → Buypass Config: set debit from cash to credit.
entries.keywords: cash credit pricing, default MOP, fueling position, Buypass config, debit to credit, ExxonMobil AutoUpgrade
entries.source: CASH CREDIT PRICING CHANGE DEFAULT MOP TO CASH.xlsx

entries.title: Cash/credit pricing — uncheck "Ignore MOP Conflict"
entries.answer: For a site to charge different prices for cash and credit fuel, "Ignore MOP Conflict" has to be turned off. Leave it checked and the system lets any tender clear a sale at its original price level, which quietly defeats split pricing. Uncheck it under Fuel Configuration → Site Params. The price level for each card type is set in Payment Controller → EPS Configuration → (primary FEP) → Cash Credit Pricing by Card Type; out of the box, debit and prepaid follow the cash price level and every other card type follows credit.
entries.problem: Different cash vs. credit fuel prices not applying; MOP Conflict prompts on prepay/postpay.
entries.context: Cash/credit pricing overview (text recovered via OCR). Used so credit fuel can be priced higher than cash to offset card fees.
entries.solution: Fuel Configuration → Site Params: uncheck "Ignore MOP Conflict." Set per-card-type price levels in Payment Controller → EPS Configuration → FEP Card → Cash Credit Pricing by Card Type.
entries.keywords: cash credit pricing, ignore MOP conflict, site params, price level, card type, MOP conflict prompt
entries.source: CASH CREDIT PRICING UNCHECK IGNORE MOP CONFLICT.xlsx

entries.title: Clear a stuck "reserved" fuel sale on a RubyCI
entries.answer: A fuel sale stuck in "reserved" on a RubyCI is awkward because the Ruby2 register can't run the Commander's Pump# → Prepay → Stop sequence the way a Topaz can. The reliable fix is to initialize fuel, which clears it. If you'd rather not init, there's a screen-button workaround: build standalone PREPAY and STOP buttons (separate from the pump-icon fuel buttons), pick the pump using the on-screen number pad — not a pump icon — then press the standalone PREPAY and STOP. It works some of the time; a full fuel init is the sure thing.
entries.problem: Fuel sale stuck in "reserved" and won't clear on a RubyCI (Commander Ci paired with a Ruby2 register).
entries.context: Verifone chat with Matthew Thumbtzen; the Topaz/Commander "Pump# Prepay Stop" method does not work on the Ruby2.
entries.solution: Initialize fuel to clear it. Alternative: add isolated PREPAY and STOP buttons on screen, select the pump via the on-screen number pad, then press the separate PREPAY then STOP (works intermittently).
entries.keywords: reserved sale, stuck prepay, RubyCI, Ruby2, init fuel, prepay stop button, clear reserved

entries.title: Commander E9040 "Unable to store fuel prepay trans"
entries.answer: E9040 almost always appears right after a load, reload, or upgrade, and it points to registers that don't agree with each other. Check that every register is on the same application version and shares the same time and date — line those up and the error clears. If it persists with everything matched, it's a Verifone Helpdesk call.
entries.problem: Error "Unable to store fuel prepay trans" (E9040) when prepaying on a Commander.
entries.context: Commander / RubyCI; Verifone KB article 000005903. Typically seen after a load/reload/upgrade.
entries.solution: Confirm all registers are on the same application version and the same time/date. If unresolved, contact the Verifone Helpdesk.
entries.keywords: E9040, unable to store fuel prepay, Commander, RubyCI, version mismatch, time date, after upgrade
entries.source: Commander - E9040 Unable To Store Fuel Prepay Trans - Error Message.pdf

entries.title: Report Navigator won't pull reports on a new laptop (IE)
entries.answer: Report Navigator leans on Internet Explorer, so it breaks on newer Windows — on Windows 8 it just opens IE to the home page instead of the report. The fixes that work: drop IE11 to IE10 or lower, set the Report Navigator shortcut to "Run as administrator" (right-click → Properties → Advanced), and add the Commander's address to IE's trusted/compatibility sites. That same combination is what got it working on Windows 7 with IE11.
entries.problem: Report Navigator on a site laptop opens IE to the home page instead of pulling the configured report.
entries.context: PEI forum thread; Commander with ConfigClient/Report Navigator on a new Windows 8 laptop. A saved report file opened fine, so the break is in IE's live launch.
entries.solution: Downgrade IE11 → IE10 (or lower); run the Report Navigator icon as administrator; add the Commander to IE trusted/compatibility sites.
entries.keywords: Report Navigator, Commander, laptop, Windows 8, Internet Explorer, IE11, run as administrator, compatibility view

entries.title: Telling the real Commander/Ruby2/CI lockup from look-alikes
entries.answer: Verifone stopped pulling logs on the classic Commander/Ruby2/CI lockup, so the job now is separating the real fault from things that mimic it. The tell is the heartbeat light: on the genuine lockup it's solid or fully off, never flashing. A flashing heartbeat points elsewhere — sunlight on the screen, the screensaver bug, mismatched software versions, or loyalty auth problems — so still walk the template questions. Two more rule-outs: if you can remote in while it's locked, or a soft reset recovers it without pulling power, it's almost certainly not the real lockup. Cases still escalate to ESG when the heartbeat is solid/off and the unit isn't in direct sunlight. (When you pull the serial for the ERS step, run status on the RubyCI, not the Ruby2 — the serial belongs to the unit.)
entries.problem: Distinguishing the known Commander/Ruby2/CI lockup from other causes of a frozen terminal.
entries.context: Verifone alert; logs/debugging no longer collected for this issue. References article 15207, step 7 (serial number).
entries.solution: Genuine lockup = heartbeat solid or off (not flashing). Flashing → check sunlight, screensaver, version mismatch, loyalty auth. Remote access while locked, or recovery via soft reset, means it's a different issue. Escalate to ESG when heartbeat is solid/off and not in sunlight; get the serial from the RubyCI via status.
entries.keywords: Commander lockup, Ruby2 lockup, RubyCI, heartbeat light, screensaver, version mismatch, loyalty auth, ESG escalation, soft reset

entries.title: Commander "EC / Please check system WDT" (watchdog)
entries.answer: "EC / Please check system WDT" is a watchdog fault on the Commander site controller. Power it down, pull the Ethernet, and reseat the three parts that tend to work loose — the SATA DOM, the USB DOM, and the RAM on the SBC — then power back up. If it's still stuck, bring it up in diag mode (dd) and reload with the installer, diag switch returned to the up position. If none of that takes, the unit has to go in for repair.
entries.problem: Commander displays "EC / Please check system WDT."
entries.context: Site controller watchdog (WDT) error.
entries.solution: Power down, remove Ethernet, reseat SATA DOM, USB DOM, and RAM on the SBC. If unresolved, load from diag mode (dd) using the installer with the diag switch up. If it persists, return for repair.
entries.keywords: EC1, check system WDT, watchdog, Commander, SATA DOM, USB DOM, RAM, SBC, diag mode, repair

entries.title: Commander "EC / Please check system WDT" (Word version)
entries.answer: Same procedure as the WDT note above: power down, pull the Ethernet, and reseat the SATA DOM, USB DOM, and SBC RAM; if it's still showing EC, reload from diag mode; if it still won't clear, send the unit in for repair. This is the Word copy of the same fix.
entries.problem: Commander displays "EC / Please check system WDT" (duplicate of [038]).
entries.context: Same content as [038], in .docx form.
entries.solution: Reseat SATA DOM, USB DOM, and SBC RAM after powering down and removing Ethernet; reload from diag mode if needed; return for repair if it persists.
entries.keywords: EC1, check system WDT, watchdog, Commander, reseat DOM, RAM, diag mode, duplicate

entries.title: Topaz "Available" message = a requested upgrade
entries.answer: When the Topaz status line reads something like "exxonmobil 2.01.01 Available," it's an upgrade the site itself requested — that request is exactly what triggers the prompt. The site applies it when convenient: close the day, the month, and the year, then run the upgrade from the Maintenance menu. It takes a while to finish. Worth knowing: Verifone won't walk the site through it, because the site initiated the request in the first place.
entries.problem: Topaz status line shows "exxonmobil 2.01.01 Available" — what is it and what should the site do?
entries.context: Verifone chat, 5/8/2015 (Mobil, Smyrna GA). The prompt appears because the site requested the upgrade.
entries.solution: Close day/month/year, then apply the upgrade from the Maintenance menu (takes time). Verifone will not walk the site through it.
entries.keywords: upgrade available, Topaz status line, ExxonMobil, close day month year, maintenance menu, requested upgrade

entries.title: Card clamped at outdoor EMV — Wayne DCRs on Buypass 3.10.28
entries.answer: After a site moves to Buypass 3.10.28 with Wayne dispensers and outdoor EMV turned on, a card can get clamped — physically held — in the DCR reader. It happens when EMV lookup can't match the card to a known type and the mag-stripe fallback doesn't match either, so the Wayne iXPay holds the card until it's rebooted. The fix is to power-cycle that dispenser to release the card. Because Wayne is the site's own equipment, Verifone can explain the cause but can't walk them through the reboot — that part goes to the pump servicer.
entries.problem: Card "locked" / "clamped" in the outdoor EMV reader after upgrading to Buypass 3.10.28 with Wayne DCRs.
entries.context: Commander/RubyCI, ExxonMobil/Buypass 3.10.28, Wayne iXPay DCRs; Verifone KB article 000007961.
entries.solution: Power-cycle the affected dispenser to release the card. Verifone can advise but cannot walk the site through rebooting non-Verifone (Wayne) equipment — refer to the pump servicer.
entries.keywords: card clamped, card locked, outdoor EMV, Wayne, iXPay, Buypass 3.10.28, dispenser power cycle, DCR

entries.title: "Permission denied for the function – 5111" on Cashier Trans Indicators report
entries.answer: That 5111 error pulling the Cashier Trans Indicators report is a role-permission gap, not a broken report. The function it needs is vtransset; if that's unchecked on every role the user holds, the report comes back empty. Don't dump all functions onto a role — that opens up security the site doesn't want. Enable just the one: Configuration Manager → Security → Manage Users → Configure Roles, pick the user's role, scroll the function list on the right, and check vtransset.
entries.problem: Site Report Navigator throws "permission denied for the function - 5111" and the Cashier Trans Indicators report won't populate.
entries.context: Commander / RubyCI; Verifone KB article 000007254. Adding all functions to a role is discouraged for security.
entries.solution: Configuration Manager → Security → Manage Users → Configure Roles → select the user's role → check the "vtransset" function.
entries.keywords: error 5111, permission denied, Cashier Trans Indicators, vtransset, Report Navigator, configure roles, manage users

entries.title: Commander clicking or beeping (three beeps)
entries.answer: A Commander beeping in groups of three — sometimes more — is an annoyance, not a performance problem. A reboot of the Commander clears it. If it returns after that, call the Helpdesk.
entries.problem: Commander makes clicking or beeping noises, often in sets of three beeps.
entries.context: Commander / RubyCI; Verifone KB article 000007272. Does not affect performance.
entries.solution: Reboot the Commander. If it persists, contact the Helpdesk.
entries.keywords: Commander beeping, three beeps, clicking noise, reboot, annoyance

entries.title: Block credit/debit on lottery — Disable MOP via Rule Manager
entries.answer: To stop customers paying for lottery with credit or debit, build a rule in Rule Manager instead of touching the department. First note two numbers: the lottery department number and the MOP number you want to block (the MOP number, not the MOP code). Then Tools → Rule Manager → Add: scope Transaction, trigger type Department/Payment. Add a condition Department = (lottery dept), then "add AND" a second condition MOP = (the MOP to restrict). Save it, and that tender is refused on lottery sales.
entries.problem: Site wants to restrict a method of payment (credit/debit) on lottery sales.
entries.context: Commander, RubyCI, Ruby2, Topaz; Verifone KB article 000006634.
entries.solution: Tools → Rule Manager → Add (Scope: Transaction; Trigger: Department/Payment). Condition Department = lottery dept, AND condition MOP = the MOP to block. Save. Use the MOP *number*, not the MOP code.
entries.keywords: disable MOP, lottery, Rule Manager, department, MOP number, restrict credit debit, condition

entries.title: Commander "EC / Check System WDT" — power-cycle first (KB version)
entries.answer: Same "EC / Please check system WDT" fault, but the first-line fix is gentler than opening the box: fully power-cycle the Commander. Pull the power, wait for every light to go out, leave it off a full two minutes, then reconnect. If the message is still there, do exactly one more power cycle (two total). A VASC can go further and reload the unit from diag mode. If EC still won't clear, it's almost certainly a loose or damaged internal component — reseat the DOMs and RAM (see [038]) or send it in for repair.
entries.problem: Commander won't boot — stuck on "Starting Application," EC in status, "Please Check System WDT!!" on the green display.
entries.context: Commander Site Controller; Verifone KB article 000005899. VASC-only instructions are in the article attachment.
entries.solution: Power-cycle (power off until lights die, wait 2 min, power on). Repeat once if needed (2 total). VASC: reload from diag mode. If still present, suspect a loose/damaged internal component (reseat DOMs/RAM per [038], or return for repair).
entries.keywords: EC, check system WDT, won't boot, starting application, power cycle, diag mode reload, Commander, watchdog

entries.title: Commander status code F-03 (fuel code after install/upgrade)
entries.answer: F-03 on the Commander status line is a fuel code, and like its sibling F-02 it usually shows up right after an install or upgrade. The fix is to reinitialize the fuel chain: init fuel, the fuel driver, the DCR driver, and the DCRs. That re-establishes the link and the code clears.
entries.problem: Status code F-03 on a Commander.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 12/9/2015 (Shell install, Doraville GA). Tony (VASC) — a fuel code that normally appears after an installation or upgrade. Case #151209.
entries.solution: Initialize fuel, the fuel driver, the DCR driver, and the DCRs.
entries.keywords: F-03, F03, status code, fuel code, init fuel, fuel driver, DCR driver, after install, after upgrade

entries.title: Outside fuel sales "coming due inside" — turn off auto-approval settings
entries.answer: When outside fuel sales keep coming due inside, an approval setting is forcing the cashier to OK them. Check three places. In Fuel Site Parameters (Forecourt → Fuel → Fuel Config-Pending), uncheck Approve All and uncheck Auto Approval on each enabled fueling position, and make sure every fuel product — even unused ones — has a unique product code. In DCR Site Parameters (Forecourt → DCR), uncheck Approve All. In Touch Screen Configuration (Store Operations → Touch Screen), uncheck Allow One-touch Approval for every register. After any change, make sure no one is fueling and run Initialize Fuel so it takes.
entries.problem: Inside/outside fuel sales come due inside after the customer dispenses fuel.
entries.context: Commander / RubyCi; Verifone KB article 000006405.
entries.solution: Uncheck Approve All + per-position Auto Approval (Fuel Config-Pending); ensure unique product codes for all fuel products; uncheck Approve All (DCR params); uncheck Allow One-touch Approval for all registers (Touch Screen). Then Initialize Fuel with no one fueling.
entries.keywords: sales coming due inside, auto approval, approve all, fueling position, unique product code, one-touch approval, initialize fuel

                                                        Article 13431 Details

                                                            Article Title
  Commander Site Controller Fuel and DCR Configuration and Troubleshooting Guide
                                                          Problem/Task
     • Commander Site Controller Fuel and DCR Configuration and Troubleshooting Guide
                                                           Environment
     • Commander
     • Spa/Spi
                                                               Cause
  Insufficient User Rights
                                                   Customer Solution/Action
                                         Common Fuel Issues with Steps to Resolve

  Pumps Offline

     • Offline pumps mean that commander is not able to communicate with the dispenser, this could be the
       result of the dispenser not being powered on or started correctly or a loose cable or connection either at
       commander or at the FCI box.
     • Are all pumps effected? If so check the Fourcourt Interface (see picture) and verify that each board has
       led lights that are lit and what activity they are performing; blinking, solid, no light activity. If there are
       no LED’s lighted, then verify the power cable is connected to an outlet that has power, and move it to a
       new power source if possible. If all looks ok unplug it for 30 seconds and plug it back in to power cycle
       it.
     • Once connection to the pumps is back up it may be required to initialize them to return them to full
       operation.
            ◦ Csr Functions -> Fuel Manager -> Initialize Fuel Contact the helpdesk with this information.

  Fueling/Calling/Authorizing/Credit Card Activity

     • A status of fueling means when no one at the pump is actually fueling can mean the dispenser is having a
       communication issue. This is usually accompanied by a message on the Dispenser Card Reader that is
       different from the normal idle message. “Thank you for shopping”, “Please see cashier inside”, "E01
       error" are some examples.
     • If no response at the pump, customer can try to lift the handle or press a payment key or swipe a card to
       try to "kickstart" the pump out of the mode it is in.
     • If a pump is authorized then there is a prepay on it and this needs to be stopped. Pump # -> Stop to
       unauthorized it.
     • If there are no pending fuel transactions and the pumps are clear, attempt a fuel initialization and dcr
       initialization.
            ◦ Config Client -> Forecourt -> Initialize -> Initialize Fuel Driver
            ◦ Config Client -> Forecourt -> Initialize -> Initialize Fuel
            ◦ Config Client -> Forecourt -> Initialize -> Initialize DCR
                  ◾ Choose DCR # if isolated to one DCR or press 0 for All DCRS if all are effected
            ◦ If the issue persists contact the helpdesk for additional troubleshooting

  Pump Stuck in Reserve

     • Reserves can be cleared by hitting the following key/softkey sequence. Pump # -> Prepay -> Stop
           ◦ If you do not have the Prepay and Stop keys programmed they can programmed Config Manager ->
             Store Operations -> Touch Screen
     • Reserves can also be cleared by initializing fuel.
     • Verify nobody is fueling and pumps are clear and all fuel sales are completed.
           ◦ CSR Functions -> Fuel Menu -> Initialize Fuel

                 troubleshooting.

     • This can be caused by the fuel site pause being enabled. Verify that it is currently set to false, if it is not
       then change it to false
     • CSR Functions -> Fuel Menu -> Toggle Fuel Site Pause

     • If there are any messages indicating offline pumps, or you cannot p

entries.title: Packing list — Conoco/Windy Hill (Alpharetta Food Mart) Commander install
entries.answer: This one isn't a troubleshooting note — it's a Verifone packing list recording the hardware shipped for the Conoco/Windy Hill (Alpharetta Food Mart) install on 2 April 2015. The order included a Commander standard site controller, Topaz XL registers, a Forecourt Fuel Controller (FCI), a Cybera zone router, an SPP current-loop kit, Topaz 2x20 customer displays, Petro media drawers, and a run of Sapphire Ethernet cables. Useful as a reference for what a full Commander/Topaz fuel install actually ships with.
entries.problem: Equipment manifest (not a fault) for a specific site install.
entries.context: Verifone packing list 37308630, shipped to Atlanta Petroleum Equipment Co. for end customer Alpharetta Food Mart, 6415 Atlanta Hwy. Text recovered via OCR.
entries.solution: Reference only — itemized bill of materials for a Commander + Topaz + FCI + Cybera router fuel install.
entries.keywords: packing list, bill of materials, Commander site controller, Topaz XL, FCI, Cybera zone router, SPP current loop, customer display, media drawer, install

entries.title: Don't check "Contactless" (Chevron greys it out)
entries.answer: A standing note from Ms. Peggy: leave the Contactless box unchecked. On a Chevron site where both Contactless and Contact were checked, Contactless ended up greyed out anyway — so checking it gains nothing and can confuse the config. Keep Contactless off.
entries.problem: Confusion over whether to enable the Contactless setting.
entries.context: Note dated 3/12/20 — Michael, Chevron: both Contactless and Contact were checked and Contactless showed greyed out.
entries.solution: Do not check Contactless.
entries.keywords: contactless, never check, Chevron, greyed out, contact, card settings

entries.title: Commander Fuel/DCR Troubleshooting Guide (03-06-16)
entries.problem: Step-by-step fuel/DCR troubleshooting matrix: offline pumps, clearing pumps on hold, initializing/reset fuel, FCI power/LED checks, patch-list and ESG escalation notes.
entries.solution: Look up the specific code or message in the table below for its description and corrective action.
entries.table:
### Sheet: Fuel Troubleshooting
Fuel Troubleshooting
* Note: There have been many Commander patches created for fuel and DCR issues. For undocumented, or unresolved issues the alert PATCH LIST FOR PETRO HELPDESK – COMMANDER should be checked to see if there has been a patch created for the issue. Checked previous cases to see if the site has been patched previously, or has had a reload since a patch has been installed.
** Note:  Currently, if a reboot/resetfuel is needed in any of the below steps then logs need to be pulled prior to, contact a T3 to do a resetfuel instead. If it resolves then logs need to be pulled and the case escalated to ESG per article Article 15207
Issue | Customer Solution | T1/T2 Solution
Offline | Clearing the pumps on hold and initializing may resolve the issue. Verify that the pumps have power by verifying the PPU's are lit and have prices, and the dispenser card readers are lit and have their normal idle message. | Assist site with Customer Solution
Make sure all pumps are clear | If the pumps are still offline assist them in verifying that the FCI has power and light activity, if it does not then reboot it or move to a different power source. Check communicaiton lights on front of commander to see if TX is blinking to indicate channel is selected corectly.  Slow blinking light on board in FCI means that ocmmuncation to board is OK but no connectuion from board to Dispneser
Go to CSR Functions -> Fuel Manager -> Clear Pumps on Hold -> Select Pump | If they have Tokheim (and high enough  base) then reset controller via topaz fuel menu.
Csr Functions -> Fuel Manager -> Initialize Fuel | CSR Functions -> Fuel Menu -> Reset Controller
Attempt a resetfuel and reboot and if no change then refer to pump tech
Are all pumps effected? If so check the Fourcourt Interface (see picture) and verify that each board has led lights that are lit and what activity they are performing; blinking, solid, no light activity. If there are no LED’s lighted, then verify the power cable is connected to an outlet that has power, and move it to a new power source if possible. If all looks ok unplug it for 30 seconds and plug it back in to power cycle it.
Contact the helpdesk with this information.
Fueling/Calling/Authorizing/Credit Card Activity | A status of fueling means when no one at the pump is actually fueling can mean the dispenser is having a communication issue. This is usually accompanied by a message on the Dispenser Card Reader that is different from the normal idle message. “Thank you for shopping”, “Please see cashier inside”, "E01 error" are some examples | Assist or verify that site has performed the Customer Solution steps
In many cases this is a coms issue so assist site with attempting to clear this with normal methods
If no response at the pump, customer can try to lift the handle or press a payment key or swipe a card to try to "kickstart" the pump out of the mode it is in. | If the LED lights on the fuel board in the FCI are blinking very rapidly without any pause then if could be getting flooded with communication. Rebooting the FCI is needed. Verify normal activity after it’s reboot.
If a pump is authorized then there is a prepay on it and this needs to  be stopped. Pump # -> Stop to unauthorized it. | Clear any stuck fuel sales if any, if a pumpt is authorized then this pump needs to be stopped (pump # stop) to unauthorize it
** Resetfuel if no change
Config Manager -> Forecourt -> Initialize -> Initialize Fuel Driver
Config Manager -> Forecourt -> Initialize -> Initialize Fuel
Config Manager -> Forecourt -> Initialize -> Initialize DCR Driver
Config Manager -> Forecourt -> Initialize -> Initialize DCR -> Choose DCR # if isolated to one DCR or press 0 for All DCRS if all are effected
If the issue persists contact the helpdesk for additional troubleshooting
Reserved | Reserves can be cleared by hitting the following key/softkey sequence. Pump # -> Prepay -> Stop
If you do not have the Prepay and Stop keys programmed they can programmed Config Manager -> Store Operations -> Touch Screen | Assist or verify that site has performed the Customer Solution steps
Reserves can also be cleared by initializing fuel. | Check previous cases to see if any reserve patches were applied. If they have not been then set up the appropriate patch.
Verify nobody is fueling and pumps are clear and all fuel sales are completed. | Contact T3 if site has been patched and issue is still occurring
CSR Functions -> Fuel Menu -> Initialize Fuel
If the issue persists or is a reoccurring problem, contact the helpdesk for additional troubleshooting
Error Processing Prepay  | This can be caused by the fuel site pause being enabled. Verify that it is currently set to false, if it is not then change it to false | Assist or verify that site has performed the Customer Solution steps
CSR Functions -> Fuel Menu -> Toggle Fuel Site Pause | Verify pumps have communication by checking lights in the FCI
o   It should be set to Disabled or False, not Enabled or True | Verify fuel config allows prepay that is being attempted.
o   If need be change it to Disabled or False | Initialize fuel drivers and fuel (reset controller if Tokheim)
Contact the helpdesk with this information | Resetfuel may be need
Not Dispensing | What errors are on the register? | Assist or verify that site has performed the Customer Solution steps
Is sale amount rolling back to $0 before grade is selected? | Validate that the issue is with dispensing, and not pumps frozen after price init, or dispenser not usable after an FP move, both of which have patches for those issues
Is it just one grade of gas not dispensing or all grades?
Are you needing/waiting for a fuel delivery? | Power down Commander and FCI
If there are any messages indicating offline pumps, or you cannot prepay a pump successfully then contact the helpdesk | Reseat fuel cable on Commander and FCI
Initialize the fuel driver and the fuel system | Wait 2 minutes and then power

DEALER CONFIGURATION (BACKDOOR)

****If error "Invalid card" when trying to activate or accept, get ISO from card and verify NW has flagged to allow also check CC config has both SVC listings enabled.

### Sheet: ADS
ADSPAK
Network: | ADS |  (Alliance Data Systems)
Applications: | ADSPAK
NW HD Phone: | (800) 378-1205
Hours: | 24 hours / 7 days a week
NW Connection: | Dial | ( PRI 950-1696  SEC  950-1696  DWNLD  1-800-221-2455)
VSAT | ( PRI 0000000   SEC  0000000   DWNLD  0000000 - numbers will change after PDL)
ISDN | (varies by geographical area. ..  Contact network for specific settings)
TERMINAL FULL | (see Terminal Full steps listed at end of Troubleshooting section)
TROUBLESHOOTING STEPS
Conduct a test
Pre-auth test for an amount "more than" $50.00    (can use available Test Cards) | TestCards | )
Conduct a debit card transaction for any amount
PDL  (A PDL can be requested at any time with this application, without contacting the network first)
Print Network Log from Network Manager
Locate the most recent transactions…
 An "X" in the DESC column indicates a "failed" transaction
Identify the error code in the Reference column for this transaction
 (2 digit code following the "-" ex:  123456-68 )
67 / 68 = Modem issue | (reset modem)
57 = No phone line detected | (verfiy dial tone..   reset modem)
40 = Host system failure | (contact network)
27 = Phone line issue | (verfiy dial tone..   reset modem…
refer to phone company for trouble on line)
87 = Network issue | (contact network)
37 / 77 = Phone number issue | (verfiy dial tone..   reset modem…
verify PRI/SEC dial numbers)
Verify network connection type / Print IOCFG from Maintenance
Dial | net1.ldm  /dev/modem  b=2400 p=- d=0 s=0 c=10000010
VSAT | net1.ldm  /dev/com2  b=2400 p=- d=0 s=0 c=10000040
ISDN | net1.ldm  /dev/com2  b=2400 p=- d=0 s=0 c=10000020
If Dial,  reseat phone line in telco (modem) port and reboot ruby/sapphire (controller)
if VSAT or ISDN,  reseat cable in com port 2 (or specified port) and reboot ruby/sapphire (controller)
Disconnecting com 2 (or specified port) should generate an error message automatically.
If no error message is generated, it indicates the Ruby/Sapphire is not attempting to communicate
and a dial in to repair files will probably be required.
Possible Dial / Multi-thread / Modem issues:
"Dialing, connecting, transmitting, communication failed"
This is an indication that the modem is not sending information that is recognizable by the network and a modem reset is in order.
Reset the modem by power cycling ruby for 1 minute (recommended) or restting via Maintenance function
"Dialing, Line Unavailable" or "Dialing, Host Unavailable"
Verify the modem line has a "ial tone" and is firmly connected to Telco port.
Verify modem is dialing correct PRI and SEC numbers for network connection.
The network may provide alternate numbers when there is an issue.
Possible VSAT / WAN / DSL issues
“No Comm DSR”
This message is specific to VSAT, DSL or Cable, this means the system is no longer seeing the connection to the nw device.
This is a good indication that a cable is loose, unplugged or damaged.
Reseat cable connection at com 2 (or port specified in iocfg) and at network device
"Comm Offline"  or  "Network Offline"
Indicates no connectivity with host.  Can be caused by a cable issue or a network device.
Receiving these messages do indicate that the Ruby/Sapphire is attempting to communicate with Host but getting no response.
Other possible issues:
"Network Not Initialized" message indicates that the network is NOT enabled in Network Manager/Network Configuration, or the
Merchant ID is missing.
Verify required NETWORK files: | Other required MISC files:
jcp.ldm
netinfo.dat | gdp.ldm
netccard.Dat | sdp.ldm
netmgr.ldm | mdp.ldm
ndp.ldm
comm.ldm | (if Dial)
Isdn.ldm | (if VSAT or ISDN)
Escalate to DIAL IN CENTER to replace any corrupted files
Conference network to verfiy if they see a signal
If issue is a network issue, refer to network for resolution
If issue appears to be Verifone issue, and has not been resolved, refer to an Escalation Agent
TERMINAL FULL | A Termianl Full is a condition that exists when the terminal has been storing transactions instead of forwarding them
to the network, but is unable to continue doing so because there is no available memory left in which to store the
transactions.  The terminal is now "full", and can no longer accept transactions until the issue iw resolved, and it
the terminal is able to forward transactions to the network again.  | This issue can be caused by a batch not closing
properly, a loss of network communications, or by an error in a credit card batch.
Have site Close Day first.  This will force the batch closed, and may resolve issue.
Troubleshoot network communications next.   | Utilize the troubleshooting steps listed above.
If the Terminal Full is caused by a lack of communication it will self correct itself as soon as communication has been restored.
If the network communication has been verified, then the issue is an error in a network batch.  Have the site print all network batches, and
escalate to the DIAL IN CENTER for a "Batch Edit" dial in by a Tier 3 agent.
