BUYPASS
Network: | Buypass
Applications: |

entries.title: RUBY MESSAGES — general and numbered operator messages
entries.problem: Comprehensive Ruby/Topaz operator-display message reference: alphabetical general messages plus numbered message ranges, each with description/action.
entries.solution: Look up the specific code or message in the table below for its description and corrective action.
entries.table:
### Sheet: General Messages
GENERAL MESSAGES
This section contains general messages that may appear on the Operator Display but are not numbered.                                  If more than one message occurs at a time, the messages take turns displaying briefly.                                                       Messages are listed in alphabetical order.  Information contained in parentheses is variable: it changes depending on the circumstances.                                                                                                                                                               Not all messages apply to every application.  Some are seen in specific applications only.
MESSAGE | DESCRIPTION/ACTION
(#) RECEIPT(S) PENDING | The indicated number of transactions (#) are suspended.
Abort printing not allowed | This happens if you try to exit the Close Period 4 function after you have started processing the report.  The Close Period 4 report is not saved for later printing and must be printed when it is processed.
ACCEPTED | Indicates that the entry made for either the Set store Telephone number or Set Store Postal Code function was accepted into the system.
ACCOUNT CLOSED | Indicates that the stored value card no longer has any value.
ACCOUNT NUMBER ERROR | The credit card number has not been correctly manually entered.
APPLICATION PENDING - NOTIFY BY MAIL | Quick Credit entries may include a duplicate database information, for example a duplicate street number and last name.  (Citgo)
BAD TOTALS DATE | The Host Totals for the date entered are not available.  "Failed to receive xxxx totals" prints on the report.  The EFS network maintains online totals for five days and settlement totals for 45 days.  (EFS National Bank)
BATCH FULL, CLOSE BATCH | One of two things occurred:  (1) The number of transactions in the batch has reached its limit: close the batch.  (2) The network was not initialized: download parameters from the network using a PDL or TDR function in Network Manager mode.
BATCH NOT FOUND | The batch does not exist at the SPS host.  (Conoco)
CALL HELPDESK # | (Customer sees "PLEASE SEE CASHIER" on the DCR display.)                               Call Mobil Network and request a download of the MOCC (card/prompt) table to the terminal.   (Mobil)
CALL HELPDESK * | Call Mobil Network to request a TID download to the terminal.   (Mobil)
CALL HELPDESK $1 | (Customer sees "DEBIT NOT AVAILABLE" on the DCR display.)                               Terminal cannot communicate with DCR keypad; check physical connections (cables, connectors, dip switches, etc.).      (Mobil)
CALL HELPDESK $2 | (Customer sees "DEBIT NOT AVAILABLE" on the DCR display.)                               Terminal does not have a working key; call Mobil Network to request a working key download.      (Mobil)
CALL HELPDESK $3 | (Customer sees "DEBIT NOT AVAILABLE" on the DCR display.)                               Terminal does not have a master key.  Keypad or security device is not properly injected.  Replace keypad, GSM, MSM, or SSM.      (Mobil)
CALL HELPDESK 3A | Call the Mobil Network help desk.
CALL HELPDESK $4 | (Customer sees "DEBIT NOT AVAILABLE" on the DCR display.)                               Gilbarco only.  DCR did not properly initialize.  Reinitialize fuel and DCRs.    (Mobil)
CALL HELPDESK $5 | (Customer sees "PLEASE SEE CASHIER" on the DCR display.)                               Gilbarco only.  DCR received an improper encryption response.  Reinitialize fuel and DCRs.   (Mobil)
CANNOT PROCESS GEMKEY! | The Gemkey entry was not correct in the Registration Information function.
CANNOT PROCESS | Host cannot verify data.
CANNOT PROCESS                    INVALID SS# | Invalid Social Security number.
CANNOT READ/SWIPE ANOTHER CARD | Information cannot be read; the terminal prompts for an alternative credit card to be swiped.
CAR WASH COMM ERROR | The Ruby terminal and car wash controller are not communicating.  The car wash code could not be received from the controller.  If the noncommunication has just started or just ended, it may take a minute for the alarm to turn on or off.
CARD DECLINED | The credit card is currently being used, usually at a fuel dispenser.  Ask the customer to wait and then retry the credit card.  This message appears until the outside sale is complete and the host releases the credit card.
CARD HAS ONE FUEL PRODUCT LIMIT | The card tendered restricts purchase to a single fuel product in each transaction.
CARD IN READER | A customer has left a card in a DCR outside.
CARD IN USE | If a customer purchases fuel with a stored value card and then tries to make a separate purchase inside before the outside sale has processed, this message appears until the host releases the card.
CARDSCAN 0 LOADED | (Schlumberger) Broadcast loading of all DCRs (CARDSCANs) has concluded.  The '0' means 'all'.
CASSETTE FULL | A cash acceptor cassette is full and needs to be emptied before this message will clear.
CHECK COIN DISP | The coin dispenser is unavailable because of a commuication error or because the coins have run out.
CLOSE NOT ALLOWED | The network was not initialized.  Download parameters from the network using a PDL or TDR function in Network Manager mode.
COMBO#nn DISABLED! | The cashier has attempted to sell a combo whose time period has expired.
COMBO#nn INVALID! | The cashier has attempted to sell a combo that does not exist.
COMM FAILURE | Check the phone cord connections (Dial) or PES connections (VSAT); retry contacting the network; if communication failures are consistent and repeated and you have verified the connections, contact VeriFone Customer Support.
DATE/TIME ERROR | The terminal date or time is not within eight hours of the system date or time.
DEBIT NOT AVAILABLE | Debit host is not available.
DROP (method of payment) | The cash drawer contains more than the maximum allowed for the metho of payment indicated.  Make a large enough safe drop to reduce

entries.title: Critical Error 985 (Sapphire Gempro) — corrupt feature card
entries.answer: Critical Error 985 on a Sapphire Gempro means the HPV-20 feature card is corrupt or defective. A VASC reloads the HPV-20 feature at the version currently installed; if that reload doesn't clear it, the card itself gets replaced.
entries.problem: "Critical Error 985" on a Sapphire Gempro.
entries.context: All software applications; Sapphire Gempro. Verifone article 8246.
entries.solution: Have a VASC reload the HPV-20 feature at the installed version. Replace the card if the reload fails.
entries.keywords: critical error 985, Sapphire, Gempro, HPV-20, feature card corrupt, reload, replace card

entries.title: Critical Error Handler 989–999 (HPV-20)
entries.answer: Critical Error Handler codes 989–999 flag a condition on the HPV-20 itself and don't always mean the system is down. Restart the HPV-20 and listen for the version announcement, watching the lights during POST. Lift the printer cover to rule out an alarm-line problem like a stuck error message. Give it a few minutes and the Sapphire/Ruby screen may offer "1. REBOOT, 2. START APP" — choose 2 so the application loads. A successful ping from the HPV-20 to the Sapphire rules out router and connectivity faults; if it won't ping, recheck the VFI-approved router setup.
entries.problem: Critical Error Handler messages (#989–999) on the HPV-20.
entries.context: HPV-20; Verifone article 8866. Code definitions are in article 1850 (HPV-20 Error Codes — see [087]).
entries.solution: Restart the HPV-20; watch POST lights; lift printer cover to clear a stuck alarm/error; choose "2. START APP" when offered; ping Sapphire to confirm connectivity (if it fails, verify the VFI-approved router setup). CSS: escalate to a 2nd-level Clearwater agent for T-3 access.
entries.keywords: critical error handler, 989, 999, HPV-20, restart, POST lights, printer cover, start app, ping Sapphire, router

entries.title: Cash sales printing receipts despite "force cash receipt" off
entries.answer: If every cash sale prints a receipt even though forced cash receipts are supposedly off, the setting actually in control is "Force Cash Receipt." In Config Client → Payment Controller → POS Configuration, open the POS tab and uncheck Force Cash Receipt, Save, then Tools → Refresh Configurations and test a cash sale. With more than one register, log out of Sales and back in on each so the change applies.
entries.problem: All cash transactions print receipts even though force ticket print for cash is disabled.
entries.context: Commander/Topaz, Commander/Ruby2, RubyCi/Ruby2; Shell. Verifone article 15078.
entries.solution: Config Client → Payment Controller → POS Configuration → POS tab → uncheck "Force Cash Receipt" → Save → Tools → Refresh Configurations → test. On multi-register sites, log out of Sales and back in.
entries.keywords: cash receipts printing, force cash receipt, POS configuration, refresh configurations, Shell, Commander, force ticket print

entries.title: Customer/pole display scrolls copyright or shows garbage (Ruby/Topaz)
entries.answer: A customer or pole display that scrolls the copyright line or shows garbage characters is usually a loose cable or a corrupt display file. On a Ruby, reseat the Gembus cable (Gembus 1 on CPU pins 4,5) on the back of the terminal — and on newer displays, reseat the two connectors under the display base ("To Ruby Gembus 1" and "To Pole Display"). Exit and re-enter Sales. If it's still wrong, check Maint → File Info → List under I/O Manager for CDSP.LDM: if that file is missing, the Help Desk has to dial in and replace the corrupt file; if it's present, warm-boot the register.
entries.problem: Customer/pole display scrolls the copyright line or presents bogus characters.
entries.context: Ruby SuperSystem, Topaz workstation, customer display; Verifone article 6073.
entries.solution: Reseat Gembus cable (and the two base connectors on newer displays). Exit/re-enter Sales. Check for CDSP.LDM via Maint → File Info → List (I/O Manager): missing → Help Desk dial-in to replace; present → warm boot.
entries.keywords: customer display, pole display, scrolling copyright, bad characters, gembus cable, CDSP.LDM, warm boot, Ruby, Topaz

entries.title: Topaz customer display works in diag but stays dark — wrong port
entries.answer: A Topaz customer display that works in Diagnostics but stays dark in Sales — with the 12V in Device Control flipping back off no matter how many times you save it — comes down to the port. The pole/customer display only runs on Port 3. Move it there (put the scanner on another port, e.g. 8) and the 12V holds. While you're behind the unit, check that the Topaz power supply isn't accidentally plugged into the printer power connector, which produces the same dead-display symptom.
entries.problem: Topaz customer display works in Diagnostics but not in Sales; 12V in Device Control turns itself back off.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 3/11/2016 (Shell, Marietta GA). Swapping displays and ports didn't help until moved to Port 3; case 160311-24412.
entries.solution: Put the customer/pole display on Port 3 (move the scanner elsewhere). Verify the Topaz power supply is not plugged into the printer power connection.
entries.keywords: Topaz customer display, 12V off, Device Control, port 3, pole display, power supply, printer connector, dark display

entries.title: Lost EZR / tunnel IP after adding a Mako box (Cybera, bridge mode)
entries.answer: After connecting a Mako box to a RubyCI you may lose access to the EZR and can't read the tunnel IPs. The usual cause is the site's own modem/router (here a Comcast box) running in bridge mode, which disables NAT and passes the ISP's IP straight to the first device that connects — creating a conflict. The site has to call their ISP to take the modem out of bridge mode. To recover the tunnel IP itself, call Cybera with the unit's WAN1 MAC address; they can read it back and help resolve the conflicting IPs.
entries.problem: Can't get into the EZR to check tunnel IP addresses after hooking a Mako box to a RubyCI.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 6/21/2016 (Chevron, Lawrenceville GA). Comcast modem suspected to be in bridge mode; case 160621-41020.
entries.solution: Have the site's ISP take the modem/router out of bridge mode (so NAT works). Get the tunnel IP from Cybera using the WAN1 MAC address.
entries.keywords: Cybera, Mako box, tunnel IP, EZR, bridge mode, NAT, Comcast, WAN1 MAC, RubyCI, conflicting IP

entries.title: Ruby demands "Close Batch" after one credit sale (Chevron/Texaco)
entries.answer: When a Chevron/Texaco Ruby demands "Close Batch" after just one credit transaction, the network logs are jammed. Run Close Daily (option 5 under Reports), then enter Network Manager with the backdoor password, choose 15. Clear Network Logs, then 5. Clear Logs, and answer Y with the Total key. Finish by rebooting the Ruby — pull the square plug on the power brick, wait 30 seconds, and plug it back in.
entries.problem: Ruby asks for "Close Batch" after processing only one credit transaction.
entries.context: Chevron/Texaco (CVXPAK) software.
entries.solution: Close Daily (Reports #5) → Network Manager (backdoor) → 15. Clear Network Logs → 5. Clear Logs → Y (Total). Reboot the Ruby (pull power-brick plug, 30 sec).
entries.keywords: close batch, Chevron, Texaco, CVXPAK, clear network logs, close daily, network manager backdoor, reboot Ruby

entries.title: Put Chevron cards on cash price — Topaz Price Tier menu (EPSPAK)
entries.answer: On EPSPAK, card price tiers aren't set in Network Manager — every card defaults to the credit price at install, and you change them in the Price Tier menu on the Topaz. A few quirks: to move through the card entries you must type 1 (Cash) or 2 (Credit) at the prompt — the arrow keys do nothing; to leave a card unchanged, just re-enter its current value to advance. Cards in the same group (e.g. all "GC" gift cards) share one value, so changing one changes all of them, and you won't see the others update until you exit and re-enter. Press Exit to save. To put Chevron cards on cash pricing, set them to Cash (1) here.
entries.problem: Chevron cards not getting the cash price under cash/credit pricing; the usual Network Manager tab isn't there.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 2/15/2016 (Chevron, Atlanta). EPSPAK/Sapphire — price tiers are controlled on the Topaz, not the network.
entries.solution: Topaz → Price Tier menu. Enter 1 (Cash) or 2 (Credit) to traverse; re-enter current value to skip; group members share a value; Exit saves. Set Chevron cards to Cash.
entries.keywords: Chevron cards, cash price, price tier menu, Topaz, EPSPAK, Sapphire, cash credit pricing, card grouping

entries.title: DCR poll errors — methodical troubleshooting
entries.answer: DCR poll errors deserve a methodical pass before you blame the hardware. Confirm the DCRs are enabled (Fuel Manager → 8. DCR Config → 5, DCR Enable/Disable — not every application has it) and that dcrdrv.ldm is on the terminal; if a port is suspect, run an upgrade to swap the DCR comport to a known-good port. Then narrow it down: one pump, a group, or all of them? A single pump → check that reader's outside display (blank = restore power; otherwise initialize just that DCR through Fuel Manager, and check the dispenser isn't low on receipt paper, which alone can throw a poll error). All pumps scrolling poll errors → verify power to the pumps, the circuit breakers, and the Emergency Shutoff Switch (ESS). If poll errors are blocking prepays with "pump in use" while you wait on a pump tech, a temporary per-DCR workaround lets prepays through: set DCR in dispenser = NO, DCR out of service = YES, pay at the pump = NO, handle-up calling = YES — do each DCR individually and tell the pump tech they were disabled this way.
entries.problem: DCR poll errors (single pump, group, or all pumps).
entries.context: VASC troubleshooting guide, 2/25/2014.
entries.solution: Verify DCR enabled + dcrdrv.ldm present; swap comport via upgrade if needed. Isolate single vs. all pumps; restore power / init single DCR / check receipt paper; for all pumps check power, breakers, ESS. Prepay workaround: per DCR set in-dispenser=NO, out-of-service=YES, pay-at-pump=NO, handle-up calling=YES.
entries.keywords: DCR poll error, poll errors, dcrdrv.ldm, DCR enable, comport swap, ESS, circuit breaker, pump in use, prepay workaround, initialize DCR

entries.title: Commander "dropped the fuel driver" — fuel won't dispense
entries.answer: This was mostly a case-history chat, but the underlying fault is worth keeping: a freshly installed Commander "dropped the fuel driver," so fuel wouldn't dispense. Reinitializing the fuel driver restores dispensing. (Here the site got frustrated with remote help and disconnected before it was confirmed, but the issue was ultimately resolved.)
entries.problem: Fuel not dispensing after install because the Commander dropped the fuel driver.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 9/14/2015 (Shell, Northside Dr, Atlanta); Commander installed days earlier. Case 150914-3314.
entries.solution: Reinitialize the fuel driver (init fuel / fuel driver) to restore dispensing.
entries.keywords: dropped fuel driver, fuel not dispensing, Commander, init fuel driver, after install

entries.title: Debit key on wrong DUKPT engine — fixable on Petro Pay, not Viper Pay (MX915)
entries.answer: If the debit key landed on the wrong DUKPT engine on an MX915, whether you can fix it in software depends on the payment app. On Petro Pay you can: Configure → Editor, find Encrypttype (0=MS, 1=DUKPT, 3=Default); if the POS is requesting DUKPT and you're getting "Invalid PIN," edit dkidx (0=1DES, 1=3DES) to select the right engine. On Viper Pay that option doesn't exist — there's no way to switch engines in the editor, so you have to order a new debit key.
entries.problem: Debit key loaded on the wrong engine (Engine 1); "Invalid PIN" / failure; how to change the DUKPT engine on an MX915.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 6/13/2016 (Shell). Editor path is Petro Pay only; this site was Viper Pay; case 160609-21781.
entries.solution: Petro Pay: Editor → Encrypttype (1=DUKPT) → dkidx (0=1DES, 1=3DES) → Edit Value. Viper Pay: not possible — order a new debit key.
entries.keywords: debit engine, DUKPT, dkidx, encrypttype, MX915, Viper Pay, Petro Pay, invalid PIN, 3DES, new debit key

entries.title: "Debit network not initialized" (Citpak) — clean re-init
entries.answer: "Debit network not initialized" on Citpak while credit runs fine clears with a clean re-init, done strictly in order: pull a PDL from the network and run no transactions after it, run a Close Day, reboot the controller terminal, then leave it alone a full 5 minutes before testing a debit card. If it still fails after that sequence, escalate to T3.
entries.problem: "Debit network not initialized" on debit cards; credit cards process fine.
entries.context: Citpak, debit transactions; Verifone article 9253.
entries.solution: PDL (no transactions after) → Close Day → reboot controller → wait 5 minutes → test debit. If unresolved, see T3.
entries.keywords: debit network not initialized, Citpak, PDL, close day, reboot controller, wait 5 minutes, debit

entries.title: Debit declines after a Chevron→Marathon rebrand — old POP table
entries.answer: After a rebrand from Chevron to Marathon, debit started declining. The culprit was the leftover Chevron POP table — it carried over and broke Marathon debit. Clearing the old Chevron POP table fixes the decline.
entries.problem: Debit declines after a brand change.
entries.context: 12/1/22 — Shiraz; 156 Hwy 27 Bypass, Bremen GA; rebrand Chevron → Marathon.
entries.solution: Remove/clear the carried-over Chevron POP table so Marathon debit can process.
entries.keywords: debit decline, rebrand, Chevron, Marathon, POP table, leftover configuration

entries.title: Debit prompt appears but keypad is dead (new dispenser) — rebuild keypad
entries.answer: On a new dispenser install where the debit prompt comes up ("Debit Y/N?") but the keypad won't respond — even though the programming, ZIP code, and WEX odometer prompts all work — the fix is to completely delete the existing keypad from the configuration and re-input it. A fresh keypad entry restores the key response.
entries.problem: At a DCR, debit asks "Debit Y/N?" but the keys are unresponsive.
entries.context: 3/29/17 — new dispenser install at 540 Concord; Richard. Programming otherwise correct (ZIP, WEX odometer fine).
entries.solution: Completely delete the existing keypad and re-input it.
entries.keywords: debit PIN not taking, keypad unresponsive, new dispenser, delete keypad, re-input, WEX odometer, DCR

entries.title: Under cash/credit pricing, debit follows the credit price
entries.answer: A recurring observation: with cash/credit pricing on, debit cards tend to ring at the credit price unless the price-tier table is set to put debit on cash. At one site there was no FEP table at all for credit cards, yet debit still got the credit price; at another, the FEP table showed the price tier not greyed out, and debit again followed credit pricing. The takeaway is to check the FEP/price-tier table and explicitly assign debit to the cash tier when the site wants debit at the cash price.
entries.problem: Debit gets the credit fuel price under cash/credit pricing.
entries.context: 4/12/21 Shiraz (Citgo, Marietta) — no FEP table for credit, debit=credit price; 4/14/21 Josh (Buena Vista) — FEP price tier not greyed out, debit=credit pricing. Confirmed 4/15/21.
entries.solution: Inspect the FEP / price-tier table; explicitly set debit to the cash price tier if cash pricing is wanted on debit.
entries.keywords: debit cash pricing, credit price, FEP table, price tier, cash credit pricing, greyed out

entries.title: "Department not found" when scanning PLUs — reload the register
entries.answer: When scanning PLUs throws "Department not found," the register's item data is out of sync. Reloading the register restores the departments and the PLUs scan normally again.
entries.problem: Scanning PLUs returns "Department not found."
entries.context: 12/3/20; per Arsalaan.
entries.solution: Reload the register.
entries.keywords: department not found, PLU scan, reload register, item data, sync

entries.title: Period/decimal key dead in Config Client on RubyCI (known bug)
entries.answer: If the period/decimal key does nothing in Config Client on a RubyCI — so you can't key prices or program POP — that's a known RubyCI bug, not a broken keyboard. Until a future release fixes it, use a separate PC running Config Client to enter the prices.
entries.problem: The "." (decimal) key doesn't work in Config Client on a RubyCI; can't update prices or program POP.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 9/16/2015; reported at two RubyCI sites right after install. Confirmed a known issue; case 150916-12474.
entries.solution: Use a PC to access Config Client and update prices, pending a fix in a future release.
entries.keywords: decimal key, period not working, Config Client, RubyCI, known issue, update prices, POP, use PC

entries.title: Fuel sale comes due inside — pump state-bounce patch (Base 39.09–39.13)
entries.answer: There's a specific patch for one cause of fuel sales wrongly coming due inside: a pump "state bounce" on Commander Base 39.09–39.13 (Shell 2.00.04 / 2.00.05, Worldpay 1.02.13). If a site on those versions sees due-inside sales and the usual approval settings (see [048]) are already correct, this patch — Verifone article 15539 — is the fix, applied by the Helpdesk.
entries.problem: Fuel sale erroneously comes due inside (one specific scenario).
entries.context: Commander Base 39.09–39.13; Shell 2.00.04/2.00.05; Worldpay 1.02.13. Verifone article 15539 ("Correct Pump State Bounce That Causes Due Sale" patch).
entries.solution: Apply the SfcCtrl pump-state-bounce patch (article 15539) via the Helpdesk once approval settings are confirmed correct.
entries.keywords: due sale inside, pump state bounce, patch, Base 39.09, 39.13, Shell, Worldpay, article 15539, SfcCtrl

entries.title: Enable the DVR on a Commander/RubyCI (by base version)
entries.answer: To enable the DVR you point it at either the registers or the Commander, and where the setting lives depends on the software base. Below base 30: Config Client → Initial Setup → System Properties, set dvr.multicastEnable — False = DVR on the registers, True = DVR on the Commander. Base 30 and above: Config Client → Tools → Managed Modules → DVR Configuration, set DVR mode to False (registers), True (Commander), or Both. Reboot the affected units after the change.
entries.problem: Enabling/locating the DVR setting on a Commander/RubyCI system.
entries.context: Commander/Topaz, Commander/Ruby2, RubyCI; Verifone article 13385.
entries.solution: < Base 30: System Properties → dvr.multicastEnable (False=registers, True=Commander). Base 30+: Tools → Managed Modules → DVR Configuration → mode False/True/Both. Reboot units.
entries.keywords: DVR enable, dvr.multicastEnable, Managed Modules, DVR Configuration, Commander, base 30, registers, reboot

entries.title: DVR won't run after Commander install — set dvr.multicast.enable = False
entries.answer: When a newly installed Commander won't drive the DVR and the DVR is wired to the Ruby2 terminals (the same connection the legacy Ruby used), the setting to change is dvr.multicast.enable — set it to False so the DVR runs on the Ruby2's, then reboot the Ruby2 terminals for it to take effect. This is the live-chat version of [075].
entries.problem: DVR not working after a Commander install; DVR connected to the Ruby2 terminals.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 4/12/2016 (Texaco, Fairburn GA). VASC tunneled in to change the setting; case 160412-8784.
entries.solution: Set dvr.multicast.enable = False (DVR on the Ruby2's), then reboot the Ruby2 terminals.
entries.keywords: DVR not working, dvr.multicast.enable, false, Ruby2, reboot, Commander install, multicast

entries.title: DVR Specifications — overview & wiring (Topaz/Sapphire)
entries.problem: DVR reference: purpose (cashier-fraud deterrence), requires Ruby Card BRAVO, properties set in Sapphire SMS, LAN vs. serial wiring diagrams, UDP multicast behavior, serial defaults (COM5, 9600, 8-N-1).
entries.solution: Look up the specific code or message in the table below for its description and corrective action.
entries.table:
Digital Video Recorder
The Digital Video Recorder (DVR) is intended to detect and deter cashier fraud by
broadcasting certain transactions, or events, performed on the Topaz workstation and
displaying them alongside the video images recorded at the time events occurred. This
provides video images of the cashier’s actions coupled with a journal of the events as
recorded by the Topaz.
  Note:   To operate properly, the DVR feature requires the Ruby Card BRAVO.
The DVR configuration properties reside on the Sapphire and affect all Topaz systems.
These properties can be accessed through the Sapphire Management Suite (SMS).
See “Property Setup in SMS” for information.

DVR Capabilities
For “DVR on Ethernet,” each Topaz POS system issues UDP multicast datagrams for
DVR capabilities. The UDP multicast is used because an actual destination host need
not be present for the system to send a UDP datagram and multiple hosts can multicast
to the same address and port.
  Note:   The data stream is not buffered. Therefore, if the connection is lost, the data is
          also lost.
 Connectivity
The following configurations are recommended for LAN and Serial DVR setups.
LAN

                                              ROUTER

                    SAPPHPIRE                                     TOPAZ

                        BRAVO
                                               DVR
                        CARD

Serial

                                                   ROUTER         ETHERNET

                 SAPPHPIRE                                         SERIAL

                                                   SWITCH
                   BRAVO
                   CARD

                                TOPAZ              TOPAZ              TOPAZ

                  DVR                   DVR                 DVR

For “DVR on Serial,” the following defaults are used:
    ■   Port: COM5
    ■   Baud: 9600
    ■   Data: 8
    ■   Stop 1
    ■   Parity: none

                                               2
 Property Setup in SMS
In Sapphire Management Suite (SMS), the Sapphire Properties Manager is used to
configure properties for the DVR. In SMS, click Sapphire > System Properties. The
Sapphire Properties Manager appears.

Use the following table as a guide to DVR properties:
  Note:   If you change a value, do not enter spaces before or after the value.
  Note:   Do not change the property: dvr.localIPPort.

        Property          Default Value                    Explanation

 dvr.multicastEnable     true             Set to ‘true’ to enable DVR on ethernet.
                                          Set to ‘false’ to enable DVR on serial.
 dvr.multicastHost       230.0.0.1        IP multicast destination address, network
                                          setup dependent. See the network system
                                          administrator before changing this value.
 dvr.multicastPort       14001            IP multicast destination port number, network
                                          setup dependent. See the network system
                                          administrator before changing this value.

                                           3
 Data Format
Following is the format of the data sent to the serial device or found in the data portion of
the UDP multicast datagram:
        MM/DD/YY HH:MM:SS RRR String from application
        |<--------22-------->|<--- Variable length --->|
        Where:
        MM/DD/YY     - Date
        HH:MM:SS     - Time (24 Hour format)
        RRR          - Register ID (001 - 999)

Broadcast Events
The following events are currently broadcast:
    ■   All Transaction Lines—The following is a typical transaction:
        03/01/06 14:58:49 101                    MILK    1        2.79
        03/01/06 14:58:49 101                    EGGS    1        0.89
        03/01/06 14:58:50 101                   BREAD    1        1.29
        03/01/06 14:58:51 101 T                 BAGEL    1        0.99
        03/01/06 14:58:52 101                     O J    1        0.89
        03/01/06 14:58:52 101              FREE TICKT    1       -1.00
        03/01/06 14:58:52 101                       TOTAL         5.92
        03/01/06 14:58:55 101                         CASH        6.00
        03/01/06 14:58:57 101 ST# 1      TILL XXXX DR# 1 TRAN# 1010020
        03/01/06 14:58:57 101 CSH: CORPORATE          03/01/06 14:58:57
    ■   Safe Drops
        03/01/06 15:18:00 101 Entering Safe Drop --Drawer Opened
        03/01/06 15:18:15 101
        03/01/06 15:18:15 101 Safe Drop
        03/01/06 15:18:15 101       Ref#12345    CASH        50.00
        03/01/06 15:18:15 101
        03/01/06 15:18:15 101 ST# 1       TILL XXXX DR# 1 TRAN# 1010020
        03/01/06 15:18:15 101 CSH: CORPORATE          03/01/06 15:18:15
    ■   ID Checks (Passed, failed or skipped)
        03/01/06 15:01:32 101 Fri Nov 11 15:01:32 EST 1960
        03/01/06 15:01:32 101 TL         CASE PR.BEER   1                 21.99
    ■   User Login (Successful or Failed)—Not currently implemented.

                                                4
     ■   Transaction Suspend/Recall
        Suspend:
        03/01/06 14:59:54 101                    MILK   1         2.79
        03/01/06 14:59:54 101                    EGGS   1         0.89
        03/01/06 14:59:55 101                   BREAD   1         1.29
        03/01/06 14:59:55 101                    EGGS   1         0.89
        03/01/06 14:59:58 101 TRANSACTION SUSPENDED # 1010014
        Recall:
        03/01/06 15:00:54 101                    MILK    1        2.79

entries.title: E01 / Auth Failure when prepaying too soon after a pay-at-pump
entries.answer: Trying to prepay a pump right after stopping a pay-at-pump transaction can throw an E01 / Auth Failure at the dispenser card reader — the pump simply isn't ready yet, the auth fails, and it gets stuck. You'll see the R (reserved) icon on that pump's fuel button, meaning it reserved but never authorized. Clear it with Pump # → Prepay → Stop, or the CLEAR RESERVE function; any pre-auth is automatically refunded/canceled. Worth knowing: you don't have to stop a *calling* pump before ringing a prepay — you can ring the prepay on the Topaz while the pump is still calling.
entries.problem: E01 / Auth Failure on the dispenser card reader after a quick prepay following a pay-at-pump transaction.
entries.context: Pump not ready to take the prepay yet; ends up reserved but unauthorized.
entries.solution: Clear the reserved pump via Pump # → Prepay → Stop or the CLEAR RESERVE function (pre-auth is refunded/canceled). No need to stop a calling pump to ring a prepay.
entries.keywords: E01, auth failure, prepay, pay at pump, reserved, R icon, clear reserve, prepay stop, dispenser card reader

entries.title: "E409 Wrong State" on dispensers — usually pump state or wiring
entries.answer: "E409 Wrong State" means the pump's state doesn't match what the POS expects when you act on it — most often the pump is reserved or on hold. It can also mean the dispenser isn't updating the fuel controller, which points to a fault inside the pump or the fuel controller, or simply a programming mistake. When it's intermittent and limited to a couple of dispensers that share an island (here #3/#4), suspect loose or bad wiring between the dispensers and the FCI — ohm out the wiring.
entries.problem: Intermittent "E409 Wrong State" error on specific dispensers.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 7/20/2015 (Shell, Douglasville GA); intermittent on pumps #3/#4 (shared island); case 150720-3302.
entries.solution: Confirm the pump isn't reserved/on hold; check for a dispenser-to-fuel-controller update problem (pump or FCI fault) or a programming error; if intermittent on one island, ohm out the wiring between dispensers and FCI.
entries.keywords: E409, wrong state, dispenser, pump reserved, on hold, fuel controller, FCI, wiring, ohm out, intermittent

entries.title: EBT / Food Stamp — MOP codes and per-brand support
entries.answer: For EBT/food-stamp questions, two things matter: the MOP code you ring it on, and whether the brand's network even supports EBT. The MOP codes are: 13 Food Stamp (paper only, whole dollars); 16 Manual FS (electronic, dollars & cents, usually a standalone EBT terminal); 20 EBT Food Stamp – Non-Cash Benefit (electronic, through the register/network); 21 EBT Cash Benefit (electronic — note cash benefit can also buy non-food-stamp-eligible items); 22 Manual EBT CB (manual non-paper cash benefit). Whether a given brand supports EBT SNAP and/or EBT Cash Benefit varies by network — see the table below.
entries.problem: Which MOP codes to use for EBT/food stamps, and which oil-brand networks allow electronic EBT.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 4/25/2022. References articles 6508 and 16374 (Petro – EBT support per App). Brand table dated 11/12/2021.
entries.solution: Ring on the correct EBT MOP code (13/16/20/21/22). Confirm the brand supports EBT before troubleshooting a decline:
entries.keywords: EBT, food stamp, SNAP, cash benefit, MOP code, 13, 16, 20, 21, 22, brand support, ExxonMobil, Shell, BP, Chevron, article 6508, 16374

entries.title: EMV "talking points" — internal liability-shift guidance (do not send externally)
entries.answer: This is an internal Verifone alert of EMV talking points, explicitly marked do-not-send-externally — guidance, not a fix. The gist for the October 1 (2015) liability shift: Verifone won't assume liability where a site's EMV solution isn't implemented yet, and the merchant's processor has to advise how the shift is handled in the meantime; delays trace to each processor's own implementation challenges. If a customer pushes for detail, point them to their Sales Representative and their processor — don't read the alert verbatim or forward it.
entries.problem: How to answer customer EMV questions around the liability-shift deadline.
entries.context: Internal Verifone alert ("EMV Talking Points"), ~2015. Marked DO NOT SEND EXTERNALLY.
entries.solution: Refer customers to their Sales Representative and their processor for liability-shift handling; do not forward the alert or read it verbatim.
entries.keywords: EMV, talking points, liability shift, October 1, processor, internal, do not send externally, sales representative

entries.title: Don't check "Enhanced CAT" — it kills the numpad
entries.answer: Leave "Enhanced CAT" unchecked. Checking it killed the numpad — no beeps, no stars, no response to keypresses at all. Noted by Richard on the SPMs, 11/4/16.
entries.problem: Numpad goes completely unresponsive (no beeps, no stars).
entries.context: 11/4/16 — Richard, SPMs (Secure PumpPAY modules).
entries.solution: Do not check "Enhanced CAT."
entries.keywords: enhanced CAT, numpad inop, no beeps, no stars, SPM, secure pumppay, keypad unresponsive

entries.title: Stuck "EPS Comm Offline" — soft reset the NewPro
entries.answer: An "EPS Comm Offline" message that stays up after comms are actually back usually just needs a nudge — do a soft reset on the NewPro and the message clears.
entries.problem: "EPS Comm Offline" message stuck on.
entries.context: NewPro.
entries.solution: Soft reset the NewPro.
entries.keywords: EPS comm offline, stuck message, soft reset, NewPro

entries.title: EPS missing in Config Client — disable SSL
entries.answer: When EPS goes missing in Config Client, disabling SSL brings it back. On the register: CSR Functions → Network Menu → EPS Network Functions → Other Configuration, open Enable/Disable SSL and disable it. Reboot the Commander, then re-test the network and Config Client to confirm EPS is present and everything is working.
entries.problem: EPS missing in Config Client.
entries.context: 4/21/21 — Shiraz; 646 Indian Trail, Mtn Exp Mobil.
entries.solution: CSR Functions → Network Menu → EPS Network Functions → Other Configuration → Enable/Disable SSL → disable. Reboot the Commander; re-test network and Config Client.
entries.keywords: EPS missing, Config Client, disable SSL, EPS network functions, other configuration, reboot commander

entries.title: V910 EPSPAK/CVXPAK can't process credit/debit — check board then comms
entries.answer: When a V910-based EPSPAK/CVXPAK site can't process credit or debit, start at the board and the comms. Check the V910's LEDs — normal is HB (heartbeat) blinking red and PWR solid green (article 9240 covers other states) — and confirm both DIP switches are UP (V920 boards sit DOWN); if you move a DIP switch, press Soft Reset to apply it. Then prove the network path with an Echo Test and host-status check. On a Topaz: CSR → 2. Network Menu → 3. EPS Network Func → 8. Echo Test (look for "Echo Response Successful"), then Network Menu → 8. Diagnostic Check Host Status (look for "Communications Online"); turn the RAN flag on under EPS Configuration. On a Ruby: System Setup → Network Mgr → 3. EPS Network Func → 8. Echo Test. If the echo fails, the fault is the connection or board, not the cards.
entries.problem: Credit/debit not processing on a V910 (EPSPAK/CVXPAK); also covers SSL sign-on failures after a fresh install/upgrade.
entries.context: VASC troubleshooting doc. V910 normal LEDs: HB blinking red, PWR solid green; both DIP switches UP (article 9240). If the Sapphire must be moved, keep cables seated.
entries.solution: Verify V910 LEDs and DIP switches (UP; soft reset after a change). Run Echo Test + Diagnostic Check Host Status (Topaz: CSR → Network Menu → EPS Network Func → Echo Test / Check Host Status; Ruby: System Setup → Network Mgr → EPS Network Func → Echo Test). Set RAN flag on. Echo failure = connection/board issue.
entries.keywords: V910, EPSPAK, CVXPAK, credit debit not processing, echo test, check host status, DIP switch, heartbeat, PWR LED, RAN flag, soft reset, SSL sign-on
entries.source: EPSPAK 300XX V910 Cannot Process Credit Debit - VASC.docx

entries.title: Commander Error 40 / "unable to ping" — storage or whole-unit replacement
entries.answer: Error 40 on a Commander comes with "unable to ping," and the case log shows it's a stubborn hardware fault. The parts to swap, roughly in order, are the SATA DOM, the hard drive, the SD card, the USB DOM, and the internal battery — but across many of these cases none of the storage swaps helped and the fix was replacing the Commander itself (sometimes reusing the existing SATA DOM). Try the SATA DOM and internal battery first, but be ready to replace the unit if Error 40 and the ping failure persist.
entries.problem: Error 40 on a Commander, "unable to ping."
entries.context: Recurring case log 2022–2026 (Shiraz, Mahmoud, et al.). Common outcome: SATA DOM / internal-battery swap, or full Commander replacement.
entries.solution: Swap SATA DOM (and internal battery); try HD/SD/USB DOM. If no change, replace the Commander (the existing SATA DOM can often be reused).
entries.keywords: error 40, error code 40, unable to ping, Commander, SATA DOM, USB DOM, SD, hard drive, internal battery, replace commander

entries.title: "Error communicating fuel server" — reboot the fuel boards, then re-init
entries.answer: "Error communicating fuel server" points at the fuel boards. On Wildco's .43 software it traced to one red/jade board having a problem — reboot the red/jade boards manually (OEMV). If that doesn't settle it, reinitialize the fuel driver in the correct order: Fuel, then Fuel Driver, then DCR Driver, then DCR. (Side note from the same notes: on .43, unchecking the customer-receipt setting stopped the small credit receipt so only the over-$25 receipt prints.)
entries.problem: "Error communicating fuel server."
entries.context: 10/23/20 Wildco .43 software (one red/jade board); 3/17/21 Shiraz, 2155 Buford Dam.
entries.solution: Manually reboot the red/jade (OEMV) boards. If unresolved, reinitialize in order: Fuel → Fuel Driver → DCR Driver → DCR.
entries.keywords: error communicating fuel server, red board, jade board, OEMV, reinitialize fuel driver, initialization order, Wildco

entries.title: "Error retrieving DCR transaction list" — check hose assignments
entries.answer: "Error retrieving DCR transaction list" comes down to the hose setup — check the hose assignments on the dispensers and correct any that are wrong.
entries.problem: "Error retrieving DCR transaction list."
entries.solution: Check (and correct) the hose assignments.
entries.keywords: error retrieving DCR transaction list, hose assignments, DCR, dispenser

entries.title: "Error getting summary report data from Commander" — power-cycle the CI
entries.answer: This one cleared with a power cycle. Verifone advised a reload; the site installed new and reloaded when the dispensers went in, then power-cycled the RubyCI and the summary report data came back. Try power-cycling the CI before reaching for anything heavier.
entries.problem: "Error while getting summary report data from Commander."
entries.context: 8/30/21 — Shiraz, 2646 Falls Road, Toccoa. VF suggested a reload; resolved by power-cycling the CI.
entries.solution: Power-cycle the RubyCI (a reload may also be advised).
entries.keywords: summary report data, error, Commander, RubyCI, power cycle, reload, reports
entries.source: ERROR WHILE GETTING SUMMARY REPORT DATA FROM COMMANDER.xlsx

entries.title: "Failed to decrypt TR34 RKL file" — export the unit's public cert
entries.answer: When Verifone reports a "failed to decrypt TR34 RKL file," they need the unit's public certificate to re-key it. Export it from the device: plug in a USB drive, log in, and go to Security → Key Status → VRK — the screen confirms "Public Key Copied to /tmp/KRD_<serial>." Then Home → Administrator → File Manager, open Root File System → tmp, select the KRD_<serial> file, and Copy it to the USB. Zip the file before sending it to Verifone; a raw .crt attachment gets blocked.
entries.problem: RKL (remote key load) fails — "failed to decrypt TR34 RKL file"; Verifone needs the device public cert.
entries.context: Verifone instructions to Peggy. RKL / TR-34 key exchange on the PIN pad.
entries.solution: On the device: insert USB → Security → Key Status → VRK (copies public key to /tmp/KRD_<serial>) → Home → Administrator → File Manager → Root File System → tmp → select KRD_<serial> → Copy to USB. Zip before emailing (.crt is blocked).
entries.keywords: TR34, RKL, remote key load, failed to decrypt, public cert, VRK, key status, file manager, KRD, USB, zip, PIN pad
entries.source: failed to decrypt TR34 RKL file.xlsx

entries.title: BP EPSPAK 2.03.06 "Fast Credit Pending" surge — enforce matching MOP
entries.answer: After BP's EPSPAK 2.03.06 upgrade, sites saw a jump in "Fast Credit Pending" messages. The cause is credit prepays where no fuel was dispensed — typically when the fuel was rung as CASH but the sale was completed as CREDIT, then cancelled before any fuel pumped. The fix is to change the "Ignore MOP Conflict" setting at BP sites so associates must use the same payment type for the fuel as for the sale; a mismatch now throws a [MOP CONFLICT] error and the prepay must be voided. Use the correct fuel MOP for the tender (table below). The trade-off: associates can no longer complete a prepay with a different tender than was selected for the fuel.
entries.problem: Surge of "Fast Credit Pending" messages after upgrading to BP EPSPAK 2.03.06.
entries.context: Caused by credit prepays with no fuel dispensed (CASH fuel completed as CREDIT then cancelled).
entries.solution: Change "Ignore MOP Conflict" so the fuel MOP must match the completing tender. Correct fuel MOP by tender:
entries.keywords: fast credit pending, BP, EPSPAK 2.03.06, ignore MOP conflict, MOP conflict, prepay, fuel MOP, cash, credit, debit, void

entries.title: "FEP RCNL Failed" — site closes after the network closes
entries.answer: "FEP RCNL Failed" (reconciliation) happens when the site's close runs after the network's close. If Exxon closes at midnight and the site closes at 12:15 AM, the network is already closed and the reconcile fails. Move the site close to fall before the network close to avoid it.
entries.problem: "FEP RCNL Failed" reconciliation error.
entries.context: 9/13/21 — Randall. Example: Exxon network closes at midnight, site closes at 12:15 AM.
entries.solution: Schedule the site close before the network close.
entries.keywords: FEP RCNL failed, reconciliation, close time, network close, site close, Exxon, midnight

  Setting up Fuel and DCR on Commander Site Controller
Configuration
  1. Click Tools > Managed Modules and then select Fuel Driver from the dropdown list.

  2. Click Advanced Settings next to Fuel Channel 01.
         a. Click Enable Channel.
         b. Specify Port to be used.
         c. Select Dispenser type.
         d. Specify Total fueling positions on this channel.
         e. Set Max and Min delay.
         f. Click Save.

                                                                                         1
            Note: Maximum and Minimum Delay are used with Non-Modular Highline pumps only.
3. Set up additional fuel channels using previous steps.
4. From Select Module dropdown list, select DCR Driver.

                                                                                            2
 5. Click Advanced Settings next to DCR Channel 01.
       a. Click Enable Channel.
       b. Specify Port to be used.
       c. Select DCR Family type.
       d. If debit encryption is to be used:
                i. Click Advanced Settings.
               ii. Select Debit Encrypt Type.
                   DSM: Select if using a Dresser/Wayne DUKPT Security Module (DSM).
                   DUKPT: Select for all US sites not using a GSM or DSM.
                   GSM: Select if using a Gilbarco Security Module (GSM).
                   MS: Select only for non-US sites using Master Session encryption.
                   NONE: Select if not configuring for Debit (no encryption).
              iii. Click Save.
       e. Click Save on DCR Channel 01 window.

       Note: In feature Set 19 or higher for Wayne, there is an option to enable Enhanced CAT used
       for Secure CAT DCRs. This setting would only be used at Exxon Mobil sites that have Secure
       CAT DCRs (legacy or Dual CATs with injected secure messages). For both Wayne and SPP, a
       Debit Encrypt Type other than NONE must be selected (failure to do so will result in the
       keypad not functioning properly).

                                                                                                 3
 6. Set up additional DCR channels using previous steps.
7. Click Advanced Settings next to DCR Positions.
       a. Specify the DCR numbers that are going to be on the DCR channel that was previously
           setup.
       b. Click Save.

8. Click Forecourt > Fuel > Fueling Positions.

                                                                                                4
        a. Configure fueling positions as required from the dropdown list. For dispensers with a
          DCR, check the DCR In Dispenser option.
       b. Finish setting up the parameters for the site for the other tabs in Fuel Manager form.

9. Click Forecourt > DCR > DCR Position Attributes.

           Configure the DCR options and graphics.

10. Click Forecourt > DCR Keys.

                                                                                                   5
                 Configure the DCR Keypad layout as required by the site’s physical DCR keypad layout.

    11. After configuration is complete, click Forcourt > Initialization in the following order:
            a. Fuel Driver
            b. Fuel
            c. DCR Driver
            d. DCR

Initialization
Driver Initializations are only required when a change is made in the Fuel and DCR drivers section in
Managed Modules.

                                                                                                        6
 Incase multiple initialization requests are made while the pumps or DCRs are not communicating, the
requests queue and after the communication restarts the initialization requests are completed.

Troubleshooting
No green or red light on 485/Current loop

Reason:

         RS232 Cable unplugged from Forecourt box.
         Wire Polarity wrong at connector on board.
         DCR’s not assigned to channel in managed modules.

DCR shows Loading Complete, then out of service

Reason:

    DCR in Dispenser not enabled in Fuel positions.

Current loop and 485 board light indicators
485 Board:

         Has connectors at the top.
         Port 1 is right most connector.
         Green lights at either end should be solid/blinking very fast.
         Red light under each connector should be solid/blinking very fast.

Disconnecting cable from FCC to Commander Site Controller will turn off all lights and will only light up
when connected to active DCR channel on the Commander Site Controller’s ports.

Current loop board:

         Has connectors at the top.
         A solid green on the right hand side should be solid/blinking very fast. This means it is
          communicating with the DCRs. A slower blink means it is not communicating with DCRs but

entries.title: Fuel comm lost on only one register — workstation, LAN, or power
entries.answer: When fuel comm drops on just one register in a multi-terminal system, the fault is on that workstation, not the whole site. Clear any LAN comm errors first. Then list the file info on both the controller and the affected workstation and check the FUEL SUBSYSTEM for fuelsrv.ldm (on the controller) and fuelcli.ldm (on the workstation); if either is missing, dial in to replace it. If both files are present, look at the LAN side — cabling, termination, or a LAN block — and at the workstation's power supply, especially if that register has been rebooting itself. Worst case, the fix is a reload of that Ruby.
entries.problem: Fuel communication lost on a single register while others are fine.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 6/27/2016. WS1 had been rebooting itself (hinting at the power supply); case 160627-4705.
entries.solution: Resolve LAN comm errors. Check fuelsrv.ldm (controller) and fuelcli.ldm (workstation) via file info — dial in to replace if missing. If present, check LAN cabling/termination/block and the workstation power supply. Last resort: reload the Ruby.
entries.keywords: fuel comm, one register, fuelsrv.ldm, fuelcli.ldm, LAN, cabling, termination, power supply, workstation, reload, multi-terminal
entries.source: FUEL COMM ON ONLY ONE RUBY.pdf

entries.title: Fuel/DCR initialization order
entries.answer: The initialization order for fuel matters: Fuel, then Fuel Driver, then DCR Driver, then DCR. Running them in that exact sequence is what gets fuel and the card readers talking again after a reset or a config change.
entries.problem: Correct order to initialize fuel and DCRs.
entries.context: Per Shiraz, 3/17/21.
entries.solution: Initialize in order: Fuel → Fuel Driver → DCR Driver → DCR.
entries.keywords: initialization order, init fuel, fuel driver, DCR driver, DCR, sequence, reinitialize

entries.title: Sales won't complete / stuck on register — reload and power-cycle
entries.answer: When sales won't complete and stick on the register, a reload plus a power cycle clears it: reload the register and power-cycle the Commander, and sales finish normally again.
entries.problem: Sales would not complete — stuck on the register.
entries.context: 2/4/20 — Ryan; Kimia's Shell, 3639 Flat Shoals Rd, Decatur.
entries.solution: Reload the register and power-cycle the Commander.
entries.keywords: fuel sales sticking, sales won't complete, stuck on register, reload register, power cycle commander

1. The GemCom Extended Error Code (GEEC) values are designed to convey specific
information concerning the nature of the error. Each value consists of three constituents
represented by six decimal digits, seeeff, which are broken down as follows:

                         The severity of the error; this can be one of "record-
               s         level" (0), "data set-level" (1), "session-level" (3),
                         or "(overall) API-level" (4).
              eee        One of the error codes listed below.
                         The number of the API function that encountered
                         the error.
              ff         Note: if this value is even, the "source" of the error
                         is on the PC -- if odd, the "source" of the error is on
                         the POS.

2. These values will fall into the following ranges:

              0          Success
              1          Done with read-all (not an error)
              2          99999 -- "record-level" severity
          100000 –       "data set-level" severity (also for file-mode)
           199999
          200000 –       "session-level" severity
           299999
          300000 –       "API-level" severity
           399999

        EEC_S_REC        "record-level" severity indicates the error most
           (0)           likely affects only the associated record(s)
                         "data set-level" severity indicates the error affected
         EEC_S_DS        the data set or file being transferred and that any
            (1)          further operations on the current data set or file
                         transfer will fail
                         "session-level" severity indicates an error
         EEC_S_SES       prohibiting any further activity during the current
            (2)          transfer session has occurred; to retry, it will be
                         necessary to close this session and start a new one
                         "API-level" severity usually indicates an illogical
         EEC_S_API
                         state has been entered and is most likely due to a
            (3)
                         programming error

entries.table:
﻿

General Messages
This section contains general messages that may appear on the Operator Display but are not numbered.
If more than one message occurs at a time, the messages take turns displaying briefly.
Messages are listed in alphabetical order. Information contained in parentheses is variable: it changes depending on the circumstances.
General Messages 
Message 	Description/Action
(#) RECEIPT(S) PENDING 	The indicated number of transactions (#) are suspended.
ACCEPTED 	The entry made for either the Set Store Telephone Number or Set Store Postal Code function was accepted into the system.
CANNOT PROCESS GEMKEY! 	The Gemkey entry was not correct in the Registration Information function.
CARD IN READER 	A customer left a card in a DCR.
CARDSCAN 0 LOADED 	(Schlumberger) Broadcast loading of all DCRs (CARDSCANs) has concluded. The `0' means `all.'
CASSETTE FULL 	A cash acceptor cassette is full and needs to be emptied before this message will clear.
COMBO#nn INVALID! 	The cashier attempted to sell a combo that does not exist.
COMM FAILURE 	Check the phone cord connections (Dial) or PES connections (VSAT); retry contacting the network; if communication failures are consistent and repeated and you have verified the connections, contact the VeriFone Technical Support Center.
DEBIT MODULE NOT PRESENT 	Debit is not available at a DCR.
DROP (method of payment) 	The cash drawer contains more than the maximum allowed for the method of payment indicated. Make a large enough safe drop to reduce the amount in the drawer to below the maximum allowed.
Entry accepted 	The Gemcom password entered was accepted.
FUEL COMMS ERROR 	The Ruby terminal lost communication with the fuel controller through which it controls the dispensers. Check the connections. If the connections are good and communication cannot be reestablished, contact the VeriFone Technical Support Center.
GSM OFFLINE 	Check the connections to and from the Gilbarco Security Module (GSM). If connections are good but the GSM is still offline, contact the VeriFone Technical Support Center.
HOST UNAVAILABLE 	Check the phone cord connections (Dial) or PES connections (VSAT); retry contacting the network; if communication failures are consistent and repeated and you have verified the connections, contact the VeriFone Technical Support Center.
INVALID CHARACTERS 	Either the telephone number was not entered correctly in the Set Store Telephone Number function or the postal code was not entered correctly in the Set Store Postal Code function. Use 0 - 9 only. Do not use separators.
Invalid input 	The PLU/modifier combination entered for the car wash key does not exist. Define this department in the PLU File first or choose another department.
INVALID TIME OF TRANSACTION 	The time for the original transaction was entered incorrectly for a Fleet card refund.
JOURNAL BLOCKED 	The Ruby terminal cannot communicate with the journal printer. Make sure that the printer is online and that all connections are securely in place. The printer may need to be reset (refer to "Troubleshooting").
JOURNAL PAPER LOW 	The paper in the journal printer is getting low. Add new paper.
KEYPAD ERROR: rp1 	Return PIN Failure: Invalid master key type or range. Call the VeriFone Technical Support Center. The help desk should contact a servicer.
KEYPAD ERROR: rp2 	Return PIN Failure: Key not loaded. Call the VeriFone Technical Support Center. The help desk should contact a servicer.
KEYPAD ERROR: rp3 	Return PIN Termination: [CANCEL] key pressed. No action required. The transaction will time out and return to idle.
KEYPAD ERROR: rp4 	Return PIN Termination: [CLEAR] key pressed twice. No action required. The transaction will time out and return to idle.
KEYPAD ERROR: rp5 	Return PIN Termination: Delay between entering pin numbers is too long. No action required. The transaction will time out, and return to idle.
KEYPAD ERROR: rp6 	Return PIN Failure: Incompatible with encryption mode. Call the VeriFone Technical Support Center. The help desk should contact a servicer.
KEYPAD ERROR: rp7 	Return PIN Termination: First key time out expired. No action required. The transaction will time out and return to idle.
KEYPAD ERROR: rs1 	Return String Failure: Invalid display buffer or display range. Call the VeriFone Technical Support Center. The help desk should contact engineering.
KEYPAD ERROR: rs2 	Return String: Undefined error. Call the VeriFone Technical Support Center. The help desk should contact engineering.
KEYPAD ERROR: rs3 	Return String Termination: [CANCEL] key pressed. No action required. The transaction will time out and return to idle.
KEYPAD ERROR: rs4 	Return String Termination: [CLEAR] key pressed twice. No action required. The transaction will time out and return to idle.
KEYPAD ERROR: rs5 	Return String Termination: Delay between entering pin numbers is too long. No action required. The transaction will time out, and return to idle.
KEYPAD ERROR: rs6 	Return String: Undefined error. Call the VeriFone Technical Support Center. The help desk should contact engineering.
KEYPAD ERROR: rs7 	Return String Termination: First key time out expired. No action required. The transaction will time out and return to idle.
LOADING 0 CARDSCAN 	(Schlumberger) Broadcast loading of all DCRs (CARDSCANs) has begun. The `0' means `all.'
MAIL PENDING or MAIL WAITING 	The network has sent mail to the terminal. Use the Mail function in Network Manager mode to read your mail.
Max number exceeded 	You tried to perform either a pay out or a pay in and have reached the maximum number of times allowed for this period, which is 25.
MONEY ORDER PRINTER UNAVAILABLE. EXITING PAY OUT 	The Ruby terminal is not communicating with the money order printer. Check the communications link between the two machines.
NETWORK SUSPENDED TRANSACTION 	The controller is retrieving a network suspended transaction from a workstation.
NEW GEMKEY ACCEPTED 	The Gemkey entry in the Registration Information function was acce

entries.title: Exxon gift-card reload declines on second recharge in a visit — network rule
entries.answer: An Exxon gift-card reload that declines on the second recharge in a single visit is usually a network rule, not a Commander problem. On certain networks you can't recharge a card twice unless it's been used for a purchase in between — add $20, try to add another $20 with no purchase between, and it's refused. That behavior is set by the network and can surface or change with the app version (this site was fine on base 51 and started failing a week after base 53). Verify the recharge setup first — the recharge department's product code, and Sales → Prepaid Card Recharge Department pointing at the gift-card department — but if that's correct, it's a network matter: have the site call the brand network (Exxon gift card: 866-603-6483).
entries.problem: Gift card recharge/reload declines when done more than once in a visit.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 2/22/2023 (Exxon). Worked on base 51, broke ~a week after base 53; config verified correct (recharge dept product code 0533, recharge dept #5556); case 8464886.
entries.solution: Confirm the recharge department/product code and Sales → Prepaid Card Recharge Department are correct. If correct, it's network-controlled — contact the brand network (Exxon: 866-603-6483).
entries.keywords: gift card reload, recharge, decline, Exxon, prepaid card recharge department, product code, network, base 53, buypass

entries.title: PIN pad stuck on line-item display after ViperPay upgrade — check SSL port
entries.answer: A PIN pad that hangs on the line-item display right after a ViperPay upgrade — noticed here during an ICC config table update — was not fixed by reloading contactless (CTLS). The thing to check is the SSL port: 4000/5000. Getting the SSL port right is what clears the stuck line-item display.
entries.problem: PIN pad stuck on the line-item display after an ICC config table update / ViperPay upgrade.
entries.context: 10/18/19 — Richard; Valero, 428 Cumming, Aaron. Reloading CTLS made no difference.
entries.solution: Check/correct the SSL port (4000/5000). Reloading CTLS alone does not fix it.
entries.keywords: ICC config table, PIN pad stuck, line item display, ViperPay, SSL port, 4000, 5000, CTLS, contactless, Valero
entries.source: ICC CONFIG TABLE UPDATE ON TERMINAL.xlsx

entries.title: Ruby2/Topaz 410 stuck at A5 — load over serial console in diag mode
entries.answer: A Ruby2/Topaz 410 stuck at A5 status and refusing to load with the Petro Suite Installer needs a load over the serial console in diag mode. Flip the diag switch down and reboot the register; once it's in diag mode, connect a laptop to the router and to the register's serial console port; start the Petro Suite Installer and use the default Ruby2/Topaz 410 IP for the Device IP.
entries.problem: Ruby2/Topaz 410 stuck at A5; can't load with the Petro Suite Installer.
entries.context: Commander/Ruby2, Commander/Topaz 410; Verifone article 14006. Routed HD-Commander/RubyCI, Installation Inquiry, P4/S4.
entries.solution: Diag switch down → reboot → connect laptop to router and to the serial console port → run Petro Suite Installer with the default Ruby2/Topaz 410 IP.
entries.keywords: A5, stuck, Ruby2, Topaz 410, Petro Suite Installer, diag mode, serial console, load register, Commander

entries.title: Commander PuTTY "Enter Login Token to Continue" (internal)
entries.answer: When logging into a Commander/RubyCI (base 40+) over PuTTY you may be prompted to "enter a login token." Generating that token is an internal Verifone process (article 15399) — the article itself notes it has no troubleshooting value for a customer or service tech. The token is derived from numbers shown on the POS and is handled by Verifone during a support session, not something the site enters on its own.
entries.problem: PuTTY login prompts for a login token on a Commander/RubyCI.
entries.context: Commander/RubyCI base 40+; Verifone article 15399 (internal solution only).
entries.solution: Internal Verifone process — the token comes from numbers on the POS and is provided by Verifone during a session; no customer-side action.
entries.keywords: PuTTY, login token, enter token, Commander, RubyCI, base 40, internal, article 15399

entries.title: What "Fuel Deposit" means on a department report
entries.answer: "Fuel Deposit" on a department report isn't a real product department — it's a holding bucket for prepaid fuel. As prepaid fuel is dispensed, the money moves out of Fuel Deposit into the actual fuel department set in Fuel Product Department setup, so at a clean shift close it should read 0.00. A positive Fuel Deposit means something didn't clear: an undispensed or non-refunded under-run/stopped prepay at close, a softkey or PLU assigned to department 9999 and used, or a fuel grade reporting as NONE (check the FP Hose report; see article 2045). A negative amount is the flip side — when the leftover from a prior shift cashes out, it still comes out of 9999.
entries.problem: Fuel Deposit total shows a non-zero (positive or negative) amount on reports.
entries.context: Ruby reports / department report, all pump types; Verifone article 1828.
entries.solution: Expect 0.00 at a clean close. Positive = stopped/under-run prepay not cleared, a softkey/PLU on dept 9999, or a fuel grade reporting NONE (check FP Hose report, article 2045). Negative = the prior-shift leftover cashing out of 9999.
entries.keywords: fuel deposit, department report, 9999, prepaid fuel, under-run, FP hose report, out of balance, fuel product department
entries.source: index FUEL DEPOSIT DEPT SHOWING AMOUNT.pdf

entries.answer: There's a Commander patch for Base 43.09–43.30 that handles fuel continuing when a pump fails to stop, along with several related issues across all pump types. It's applied by the Helpdesk — Verifone article 15922.
entries.problem: Fuel transaction continues if the pump fails to stop.
entries.context: Commander/RubyCI, Base 43.09–43.30; Verifone article 15922.

entries.title: "Fueling point not available at this workstation" on Prepay — Zoned Fuel Control
entries.answer: "Fueling point not available at this workstation" on every pump when you press Prepay is a touchscreen setting. In Configuration Client, load the touchscreen configuration and check whether Zoned Fuel Control is ticked. If it's unchecked, check it, Apply, and Save, then log out and back in on the register and test. If it was already checked, that's a deeper issue — call the Petro Help Desk.
entries.problem: "Fueling point not available at this workstation: all" when pressing the Prepay button.
entries.context: Commander, Topaz, Sapphire, Ruby2, RubyCI; Verifone article 13517.
entries.solution: Configuration Client → touchscreen configuration → check "Zoned Fuel Control" → Apply → Save → log out/in and test. If already checked, contact the Petro Help Desk.
entries.keywords: fueling point not available, prepay button, zoned fuel control, touchscreen configuration, workstation, Config Client

entries.title: Gilbarco Highline price won't reset to zero
entries.answer: On sites with older Gilbarco Highline pumps, the price may not reset to zero on prepay or postpay. Verifone treats the fix as an internal process tied to the pump's ballast data messages (article 15210), so it's handled through the Helpdesk rather than a setting the site changes.
entries.problem: Older Gilbarco Highline pump prices don't reset to zero on prepay/postpay.
entries.context: Commander/RubyCI, Gilbarco Highline; Verifone article 15210 (attachment: ballast data messages). Marked internal process.
entries.solution: Internal/Helpdesk process involving the pump's ballast data messages.
entries.keywords: Gilbarco Highline, price not resetting, zero, ballast, prepay, postpay, article 15210, internal
entries.source: index GILBARCO HIGHLINE NO RESET BALLAST.pdf

entries.title: E1115 "Invalid Department" on new PLUs
entries.answer: E1115 "Invalid Department" when you scan or key a newly created PLU means the PLU isn't tied to a valid department. In Configuration Client/Manager: Store Operations → Merchandise → PLUs and confirm each PLU shows a department name (fix as needed); Store Operations → Merchandise → Departments and create any missing departments. Also create a dedicated "PLU NOT FOUND" department, note its number, and set it under Store Operations → Sales → PLU Not Found Department (enter the number without leading zeroes, Save). Log out and back in on all registers. After that, an unrecognized item returns the proper "PLU NOT FOUND – Enter Price" prompt instead of E1115.
entries.problem: New PLUs return E1115 INVALID DEPARTMENT when scanned or keyed.
entries.context: Commander with Topaz/Ruby2, RubyCI; Verifone article 15623.
entries.solution: Confirm PLUs have a department (Merchandise → PLUs); create missing Departments; create a "PLU NOT FOUND" department and set it under Sales → PLU Not Found Department (no leading zeroes); log out/in on all registers.
entries.keywords: E1115, invalid department, PLU, new PLUs, PLU not found department, merchandise, departments, Config Client

entries.title: Multiple card swipes to tender — non-fuel PLU with a fuel product code
entries.answer: If credit/debit needs several swipes to finish a sale, the usual cause is a non-fuel PLU carrying a fuel product code — the system treats it as a fuel sale and re-swipes to pre-authorize each time. The sale still completes and the customer is charged once, but it's annoying. Check that PLUs and their departments have proper product codes, and that non-fuel items don't use fuel product codes. There's also a known quirk: a PLU whose name starts with "HD " (note the trailing space) triggers the same behavior — remove the space, replace it with a hyphen, or move the "HD" off the front.
entries.problem: Credit/debit requires multiple card swipes to complete a transaction.
entries.context: Commander, RubyCI, Topaz/Ruby2; Verifone article 13755. The "HD " prefix bug was flagged to development.
entries.solution: Fix product codes so non-fuel PLUs/departments don't use fuel product codes. For PLUs starting with "HD " (with a space), remove/replace the space or move "HD" off the front.
entries.keywords: multiple card swipes, pre-authorize, fuel product code, non-fuel PLU, HD prefix, tender, credit, debit

entries.title: "Nothing to Commit" — com-port changes must be made in ConfigClient (Base 46+)
entries.answer: On Commander Base 46 and higher, Topaz/Ruby2 com-port changes can no longer be made from the register's backdoor Maintenance section — they have to be done in ConfigClient. Trying to change them on the register throws an error, and you may see "Nothing to Commit." Make com-port changes in ConfigClient. Verifone noted the "Nothing to Commit" register error may be resolved in Base 46.10, 47.03, and 48+.
entries.problem: "Nothing to Commit" error when changing Topaz/Ruby2 com ports from the register.
entries.context: Commander Base 46+; Verifone alert (Chris_L15, 9/29/2017).
entries.solution: Make com-port changes in ConfigClient, not the register backdoor. (Register error may be fixed in Base 46.10 / 47.03 / 48+.)
entries.keywords: nothing to commit, com port, ConfigClient, register backdoor, maintenance, Base 46, Topaz, Ruby2
