entries.title: ExxonMobil "PAY INSIDE NK / 1NK" message
entries.answer: "PAY INSIDE NK" (or "1NK") on an ExxonMobil site is a network response, not a register fault. First ask whether the customer has used the same card several times that day, then try a different card. If multiple different cards all get it, the network side is the problem — have the site's network checked. If the network is fine, call the Helpdesk.
entries.problem: "PAY INSIDE NK / 1NK" message on ExxonMobil.
entries.context: Commander, Ruby2, Topaz, RubyCI, ExxonMobil; Verifone article 15371.
entries.solution: Check if the same card was used repeatedly that day; try a different card; if multiple cards fail, have the network checked; if the network is OK, contact the Helpdesk.
entries.keywords: pay inside NK, 1NK, ExxonMobil, network response, multiple uses, different card, helpdesk
entries.source: index PAY INSIDE NK EXXON ERROR MSG.pdf

entries.title: Troubleshooting out-of-balance reports (Ruby/Sapphire/Topaz)
entries.problem: Guide to troubleshooting period reports that are out of balance / totals not matching: get the actual report first, watch for misreads, missing cashier/shift reports, and stored transactions; how to verify a report is balanced.
entries.solution: Look up the specific code or message in the table below for its description and corrective action.
entries.table:
Troubleshooting Reports - totals not matching - out of balance ...                             Page 1 of 5

  Add to Favorites

                                           Article 8952 Details

                                              Article Title
  Troubleshooting Reports - totals not matching - out of balance ...
                                             Problem/Task
      • Troubleshooting Reports - totals not matching - out of balance ...
      • My report is out of balance
      • My report totals don't match
      • How can I verify my report is balanced?
      • What does this entry on my EOD mean?
                                             Environment
      • Ruby/Sapphire/Topaz Period Reports
      • Out-of-balance
      • Stored Transactions
      • Totals not matching
      • Missing reports
                                                 Cause
  Insufficient User Rights
                                      Customer Solution/Action
      1.      Troubleshooting Report Issues:

              It's rare that real problems exist on a Ruby/Sapphire POS period report. Most
              issues turn out to be misunderstandings or misinterpretations of the data - or
              failure to collect all the relevant data, such as a missing cashier or shift report.

              ◦ Rule number 1 - You can't troubleshoot a report without seeing it.

              If the site is calling about report problems, such as out-of-balance batches, or
              some other issue, get them to fax over the report in question. You can't be
              effective, if you can't verify the integrity of the report data yourself. Plus, the site
              may not refer to certain entries on the report by its correct name and you spend a
              lot of time running down problems that don't exist.

              ◦ Rule number 2 - The corporate "worksheet" the site uses to balance their report
                - isn't a proper POS report.

              The correct procedure for balancing the report is to compare specific data on the
              actual report with other information found elsewhere on the report as detailed in
              the steps, How to balance your Report. Many times the site is comparing
              information on the report from the POS with a worksheet they keep elsewhere ...

              ◦ Rule number 3 - Use the phrase, "As compared to what - specifically?"

              Many times the site will simply tell you their totals don't match, well, what totals,
              and what are they matching them to? You may find that the totals on the End of
              day don't match a figure on a spreadsheet they keep on the Back Office (see rule
              number 2).

              ◦ Rule number 4 - Get all the data necessary to troubleshoot issue.

              Don't settle for partial reports, or blacked-out censored reports (I've had
              customers mark through figures they didn't feel I had any business seeing - I
              sent it back to them and said get me a clear copy or forget it - they may have
              crossed through crucial information) - you need to see the complete picture to be
              able to solve their problem.

http://clientservices.veriorder.com/index.cfm?fuseaction=view.articledetail&id=8952            11/21/2016
 Troubleshooting Reports - totals not matching - out of balance ...                             Page 2 of 5

             ◦ Rule number 5 - If you don't understand clearly what problem the site is
               reporting - you can't solve it

              Make sure you know what's wrong with the report, not simply that it's not in
              balance or the totals don't match. Which totals don't match - and by how much?
              And where on the report are the totals in question? Is this the first time, or is it a
              recurring problem? If network totals are involved, was there an outage that
              might explain it ... stored sales ... and extra period close?

              These five rules should go along way toward putting you on the right path to
              helping your customer.

              Now, let's examine some specifics.

             ◦ What if the totals on my Daily report don't match up to my shift or cashier
               reports?

              In this case, sites are usually keeping a spreadsheet somewhere on a back office,
              and have noted that when they add up all the department sales for their shift
              reports (for example), they don't add up to the total on their Daily report.

              Usually this is cleared up by asking the site to compare the beginning and ending
              transaction number in the header portion of the report. What they'll find (usually)
              is that there is a "gap" in transactions accounted for - meaning there's a report
              they haven't accounted for - it may have been for a partial shift or something like
              that. They can also add up the starting and ending times on the shift reports to
              determine the timeframe of the missing report.

              If they're lucky, the missing report is still in the terminal under the Print shift, or
              print cashier report option - but if not they'll have to go to the journal tape for
              details - but at least they'll have their answer.

              That last example illustrates the importance of clarifying what totals -
              specifically ... Not knowing the site was merely attempting to compare daily with
              shift could've sent you off in the wrong direction - adding unnecessary time and
              wasted effort to the call.

              Report issues, such as concerns about being out of balance or “short” on some
              aspect, can be clarified and cleared up for the most part by passing the report
              data through a sound balancing method. What

entries.title: Ruby2 screens going blank/dark and unresponsive — capture status before reboot
entries.answer: For Ruby2 screens going blank or dark and unresponsive, document the status code on the little 2-digit 7-segment display and notify your T3 before doing anything — the OS team wants specific maint-command output captured while the screen is blank, before any reboot. The issue is tied to the screensaver: T3 runs a maint command that pops the screensaver control panel on the Ruby2, checks whether the end user can work the up/down buttons, and tries changing the screensaver timeout. Don't reboot until the requested output is captured.
entries.problem: Ruby2 screen goes blank/dark and becomes unresponsive.
entries.context: Verifone alert; OS team wants data captured before reboot. Linked to the screensaver.
entries.solution: Record the 2-digit 7-seg status code; notify T3 first. T3 runs maint commands to show the screensaver control panel, verify up/down buttons, and change the screensaver timeout — capture output before rebooting.
entries.keywords: Ruby2, blank screen, dark screen, unresponsive, touchscreen, screensaver, status code, 7-segment, T3, maint command

entries.title: Inside receipts not printing 2 copies (or printing too many)
entries.answer: Whether inside sales print a customer copy (the second receipt) comes down to one setting: Payment Controller → POS Configuration → POS Tab → Misc → Print Customer Copy. Check it to print the customer copy on the original sale; uncheck it if the site doesn't want a second copy. It affects all payment types on most applications. Note that a reprinted receipt always prints both customer and merchant copies — that's hard-coded for duplicates.
entries.problem: Inside sale receipts print only one copy (want two), or print too many.
entries.context: Commander/RubyCI, Topaz/Ruby2; Verifone article 15548.
entries.solution: Payment Controller → POS Configuration → POS Tab → Misc → "Print Customer Copy": check to print the second/customer copy, uncheck to stop it. Reprints always show both copies (hard-coded).
entries.keywords: two receipts, customer copy, print customer copy, POS configuration, misc, second receipt, duplicate receipt

entries.title: E9040 "Unable to store fuel prepay trans" — duplicate of [035]
entries.answer: Same E9040 "Unable to store fuel prepay trans" issue already covered at [035] (here as Verifone article 13559). The fix is unchanged: after a load/reload/upgrade, make sure all registers are on the same application version and share the same time/date; if that doesn't resolve it, contact the Helpdesk.
entries.problem: "Unable to store fuel prepay trans" (E9040) when prepaying (duplicate of [035]).
entries.context: Commander/RubyCI; Verifone article 13559 (Tier-3 conversation attachment).
entries.solution: See [035] — align register versions and time/date; else Helpdesk.
entries.keywords: E9040, unable to store fuel prepay, duplicate, article 13559, version, time date, Commander
entries.source: index UNABLE TO STORE FUEL PREPAY TRANSACTION.pdf

entries.title: "Unsettled Suspends" on shift/day reports
entries.answer: "Unsettled Suspends" piling up on shift/day reports usually trace to a sale that was suspended, recalled, and added to more than once. The guidance for the cashier is simple: if you have to claim a suspended sale and add items more than once, void it instead and ring everything in one transaction. That avoids leaving the unsettled-suspend artifact on the report.
entries.problem: Unsettled suspends showing on shift/day reports.
entries.context: Commander, RubyCI, possibly Sapphire; Verifone article 15532.
entries.solution: Advise cashiers not to claim/add to a suspended sale more than once — void it and tender all items at once.
entries.keywords: unsettled suspends, suspended sale, shift report, day report, void, claim, tender at once

entries.title: Restrict credit on lottery (Shell, network product restriction)
entries.answer: To stop credit paying for lottery on a Shell site, set it on the POS network side: CSR Functions → Network → EPS Network Manager Function → Wholesaler → Product Restrictions → Lottery → Restrict Credit → Yes. Then wait five minutes and confirm a Yes shows an asterisk (*) next to it — that asterisk means the restriction took. (This is the network-level approach; [045] does the same job with Rule Manager on a Commander.)
entries.problem: Restrict credit as a payment type on lottery (Shell).
entries.context: Per Shiraz, 11/14/24.
entries.solution: CSR Functions → Network → EPS Network Manager Function → Wholesaler → Product Restrictions → Lottery → Restrict Credit → Yes. Wait 5 min; verify the asterisk (*) appears.
entries.keywords: restrict credit, lottery, Shell, product restrictions, EPS network manager, wholesaler, asterisk, network restriction

entries.title: Marpak/Marathon Fast Credit prepays — disable Fast Credit
entries.answer: On a Marpak/Marathon site, Fast Credit with fuel prepays gets ugly because Marathon doesn't allow partial refunds — when a prepay under-runs, the difference can't be returned to the credit card — and Fast Credit with Merchandise isn't allowed either. Verifone's advice is to disable Fast Credit on Marpak to avoid the stuck prepays. (Note: the Sales-manual section is incorrect for Marathon, and a "Beat Software" build for Commander doesn't have this limitation.)
entries.problem: Fast Credit and fuel prepays behaving badly after a rebrand to Marathon (Marpak).
entries.context: Verifone chat, 5/21/2015 (Marathon, Buford Hwy, Atlanta). Marathon disallows partial refunds and Fast-Credit-with-merchandise; case 150521-18535.
entries.solution: Disable Fast Credit on Marpak. Consider the Commander "Beat Software" build, which avoids the issue.
entries.keywords: Marpak, Marathon, fast credit, fuel prepay, partial refund not allowed, under-run, disable fast credit, rebrand

entries.title: Marpak stuck prepays from under-runs — set Fast Credit to "fuel only"
entries.answer: When Marathon's no-partial-refund rule leaves fuel prepays stuck from under-runs, set Fast Credit to fuel-only. Test it first: with Fast Credit on you should be able to press Pump # → Prepay → Credit without entering an amount; if the enter-amount screen doesn't clear, Fast Credit is off. If you do get an amount prompt, go to SMS → Network Manager → Network Configuration → Miscellaneous tab — Fast Credit has two options, one for credit prepay and one for fuel only — and select fuel only. After that, Pump # → Prepay → Credit goes through with no amount screen.
entries.problem: Stuck fuel prepay sales from under-runs because Marathon doesn't allow partial refunds.
entries.context: Marpak, Fast Credit; Verifone article 12725.
entries.solution: SMS → Network Manager → Network Configuration → Miscellaneous → Fast Credit → select "fuel only." Verify Pump # → Prepay → Credit completes with no amount screen.
entries.keywords: Marpak, stuck fuel prepay, partial refund, under-run, fast credit fuel only, network configuration, miscellaneous, Marathon
entries.source: MARPAK Partial Refunds Not Allowed Causing Stuck Fuel Prepay Sales.pdf

entries.title: Marpak E1214 "Only Fuel Allowed on Prepay Credit"
entries.answer: On Marpak 3.05–3.06, E1214 "Only Fuel Allowed on Prepay Credit" means you can't put fuel and merchandise on the same Fast Credit prepay. Two ways around it: enable Credit Prepay with No Fast Credit (SMS → Network Manager → Network Config) so mixed fuel+merchandise sales work, or keep Fast Credit on and ring the fuel and merchandise as separate transactions.
entries.problem: Cannot complete a fuel + merchandise sale; E1214 "Only Fuel Allowed on Prepay Credit"; can't enable Fast Credit with Merchandise.
entries.context: Sapphire, Marpak 3.05.xx–3.06.xx; Verifone article 13522.
entries.solution: Option 1: enable Credit Prepay, No Fast Credit (SMS → Network Manager → Network Config). Option 2: keep Fast Credit, ring fuel and merchandise separately.
entries.keywords: E1214, only fuel allowed on prepay credit, Marpak, fast credit, merchandise, credit prepay, Sapphire

entries.title: Menu keys slow to respond (Sapphire) — bad menu data or power
entries.answer: Menu keys taking up to ten seconds to respond in Sales usually start with bad menu data, then point at power. First verify there are no invalid PLUs in the menus, and check whether department keys and other PLUs respond normally. If everything is slow — not just menu keys — treat it as a possible power problem: check the power brick, make sure the vents are clean and unobstructed, consider bypassing the UPS, and power-cycle the unit. If it's still slow, call the Helpdesk.
entries.problem: Menu keys take up to 10 seconds to respond in Sales mode.
entries.context: Ruby/Sapphire, all applications; Verifone article 8865.
entries.solution: Verify no invalid PLUs in menus; confirm department/PLU keys respond. If all keys are slow, check power (power brick, clear vents, bypass UPS, power-cycle). Then Helpdesk.
entries.keywords: menu keys slow, 10 seconds, Sapphire, invalid PLU, power brick, UPS, vents, power cycle, slow response

entries.title: "Unit Price Exceeded" selling merchandise (Citgo) — product codes
entries.answer: "Unit Price Exceeded" when selling merchandise on a Citgo Commander is a product-code problem. Check that the departments and PLUs are assigned to the correct product codes — an item on the wrong product code can trip the over-the-limit unit-price check. Correct the codes; call the Helpdesk if you need a hand.
entries.problem: "Unit Price Exceeded" / over the limit when selling merchandise.
entries.context: Citgo Commander/RubyCI; Verifone article 15543.
entries.solution: Verify departments and PLUs are on the correct product codes; correct any that are wrong.
entries.keywords: unit price exceeded, merchandise over limit, Citgo, product code, department, PLU, Commander

entries.title: Program a Metrologic scanner for RubyFocus (2D)
entries.answer: To set up a Metrologic scanner for RubyFocus (2D certification), scan the configuration barcodes in order: Enter Program Mode (999999), Novram (512) Config, Extended Data Parsing, then Exit Program Mode (999999). Scanning them in that numbered order programs the scanner for the Ruby.
entries.problem: Configuring a Metrologic scanner for the Ruby (RubyFocus 2D certification).
entries.context: MetroSet 2.4.1 sheet, "RubyFocus_Certification_Finalized_2D," 2007.
entries.solution: Scan in order: Enter Program Mode (999999) → Novram (512) Config → Extended Data Parsing → Exit Program Mode (999999).
entries.keywords: Metrologic, scanner, RubyFocus, 2D, program mode, 999999, novram 512, extended data parsing, MetroSet

entries.title: "Mobil OOB Report Available" on the Topaz
entries.answer: "Mobil OOB Report Available" on the Topaz is a settlement report waiting to be printed, and only a manager-level login can clear it — a cashier ID won't see the option. Log in with a Manager ID/password (or via Backdoor) → Network Menu → Mobil Reports, and print all previous reports before the current one. That clears the message, though it can return after the daily close. The printout is a settlement report — terminal totals, payment-table totals, and exception/pending/discounted transactions — and it's good practice to print it daily to confirm the site balances with the network.
entries.problem: Topaz displays "Mobil OOB Report Available."
entries.context: ExxonMobil Commander; Verifone article 13897. Requires manager role.
entries.solution: Manager ID/password (or Backdoor) → Network Menu → Mobil Reports → print all previous reports before current. Message may reappear after daily close; print daily.
entries.keywords: Mobil OOB report, out of balance, settlement report, ExxonMobil, manager ID, network menu, Mobil reports, Topaz

entries.title: Money-order fee moves only on "Void Line"
entries.answer: A behavior note on Base 47.16 (Chevron 1.10.19): voiding a money-order line item adds the fee to the Money Order Fee department and subtracts it from the Money Order department — but only when you Void Line, not when you Void Ticket. Worth knowing when money-order totals look off after a void.
entries.problem: Money-order fee handling on a void.
entries.context: Base 47.16, Chevron 1.10.19; per Jeremy, 6/4/19.
entries.solution: Use Void Line (not Void Ticket) to move the fee correctly between the Money Order Fee and Money Order departments.
entries.keywords: money order fee, void line, void ticket, money order department, Base 47.16, Chevron

entries.title: Set up NAXML deals (item list, mix & match, combo)
entries.answer: NAXML deals on the Sapphire are built in three pieces, each ending with "save and send to Sapphire." Item list: Item List Maintenance → Add New → description → add the PLUs or departments. Mix & Match: Mix&Match Maintenance → Add New → description, start/end dates and times, weekday availability → pick the item list, a package pricing type, the quantity, and the price (you can stack package tiers, e.g. list 1 = 2 for $2.00, list 2 = 4 for $3.75; note you can't delete an added tier, only modify it — if you don't need it, set it equal to another or delete the whole mix&match). Combo: Combo Maintenance → Add New → description, dates/times, weekday availability → price-by/pricing scheme, price and priority → move the relevant item lists over with the >> button and set each one's quantity. Save and send to Sapphire.
entries.problem: How to set up NAXML item lists, mix & match deals, and combos.
entries.context: NAXML deal setup procedure (Sapphire).
entries.solution: Build item list → mix & match → combo in their maintenance tabs; save and send to Sapphire after each.
entries.keywords: NAXML, deal setup, item list, mix and match, combo, package pricing, Sapphire, maintenance tab, save and send

entries.title: Mix & Match won't import after CITPAK 6.00.04 upgrade
entries.answer: After upgrading to CITPAK 6.00.04, a Mix and Match deal won't import. The old deal can still be used on the Ruby, but only if you first remove the data that was entered in the NAXML Deal — leaving both in place is what blocks the import.
entries.problem: Mix and Match will not import into upgraded CITPAK 6.00.04.
entries.context: CITPAK, NAXML, back office deal; Verifone article 12978 (attachment: Gemcom 1.08.09 RubyCommOptions).
entries.solution: Use the old deal on the Ruby, but remove the data entered in the NAXML Deal first to avoid the conflict.
entries.keywords: NAXML, mix and match, CITPAK 6.00.04, won't import, deal, back office, remove NAXML data, article 12978

entries.title: Network Troubleshooting Guide — duplicate of [054]
entries.answer: The same Network Troubleshooting Guide already embedded at [054] (this is the original; [054] is the "Copy of"). See [054] for the per-application network reference — help-desk numbers, connection settings, test cards, terminal-full steps, and network error codes.
entries.problem: Per-application network troubleshooting reference (duplicate).
entries.context: Same workbook as [054].
entries.keywords: network troubleshooting, duplicate, per application, error codes, test cards, terminal full

entries.title: "Card Not Accepted / Use Chip" on Shell after upgrade — EMV rollout
entries.answer: After a Shell upgrade to 4.05.03, cards came back "Card Not Accepted" with the swipe telling customers to use chip. This was a known EMV-rollout issue, not a site misconfiguration — the site needs to call the Shell EMV Rollout Help Desk at 1-844-893-8916.
entries.problem: "Card Not Accepted," swipe says use chip, after a Shell upgrade.
entries.context: TJ, 10/7/19; Shell after upgrade to 4.05.03.
entries.solution: Call the Shell EMV Rollout Help Desk: 1-844-893-8916.
entries.keywords: card not accepted, use chip, Shell, EMV rollout, 4.05.03, help desk, 1-844-893-8916, upgrade

entries.title: Epspak E9068 "Notify Helpdesk" — Auto Recovery ran
entries.answer: "Epspak / Error E9068: Notify Helpdesk" means the software ran its Auto Recovery feature. It won't stop sales, but the alarm stays up until it's cleared. Reboot the register showing the message; if it's the main Ruby, the Sapphire may need a reboot too. If the message is still there after the reboot, call the Verifone Helpdesk.
entries.problem: "Epspak / Error E9068: Notify Helpdesk" alarm on the register.
entries.context: Epspak, V910; Conoco, Chevron, BUYPAK 6.xx, CITPAK 6.xx, HPSPAK 4.xx; application base 166+. Verifone article 9287. Caused by the software's Auto Recovery.
entries.solution: Reboot the register showing it (reboot the Sapphire too if it's the main Ruby). If still showing after reboot, call the Verifone Helpdesk.
entries.keywords: E9068, notify helpdesk, Epspak, auto recovery, V910, reboot register, sapphire, alarm, base 166

entries.title: Panasonic BR vs CR lithium battery selection (source PDF unreadable)
entries.answer: The source PDF wouldn't extract (it returns a PostScript error), so this is a pointer rather than a transcription. It's Panasonic's guide to choosing between BR and CR lithium coin cells — the kind used for memory/clock backup on these POS boards (see the internal-battery swaps in [086]). In general: BR-series cells (lithium-carbon monofluoride) tolerate higher operating temperatures and have very low self-discharge, which suits long-life backup; CR-series cells (lithium-manganese dioxide) offer higher capacity and stronger pulse current. Match the series and part number the board already specifies rather than substituting across series.
entries.problem: Choosing the correct replacement lithium coin cell (BR vs CR).
entries.context: Panasonic reference; the PDF did not extract (PostScript error) under pdftotext or OCR.
entries.solution: Use the BR/CR series and part number the board specifies. BR = higher temp tolerance, low self-discharge (long-life backup); CR = higher capacity / pulse current.
entries.keywords: Panasonic, lithium battery, BR series, CR series, coin cell, internal battery, memory backup, replacement, unreadable PDF

entries.title: Clearing a paper jam at the cutter (RP-300 / RP310)
entries.answer: On an RP-300/RP310 printer, a paper jam at the cutter can leave the top cover stuck. First just power the printer off and on a few times — that often frees it. If the cover is still stuck, turn the printer off, remove the cutter cover, and turn the cutter screw with a screwdriver until the paper releases, then clear it.
entries.problem: Paper jammed at the cutter; top cover stuck.
entries.context: RP-300 User's Manual (text recovered via OCR).
entries.solution: Power off/on several times. If still stuck: power off, remove the cutter cover, turn the cutter screw with a driver until the paper releases.
entries.keywords: paper jam, cutter, RP-300, RP310, top cover stuck, printer, turn screw, power cycle

entries.title: "Parse error" logging into Network Manager in SMS
entries.answer: A parse error logging into Network Manager in SMS usually shows up after you've logged into two different site brands in a row — SMS is still expecting the previous brand's network configs (often Shell's) while you're on a different brand's site. It's a client-side hiccup, not the site: clear the Java cache on your PC and retry, or use a different SMS version. The "Shell" in the message refers to the network brand, not a Unix shell.
entries.problem: Parse error when logging into Network Manager in SMS.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 8/10/2015 (Chevron site). Seen after logging into two different site brands; case 150810-3642.
entries.solution: Clear the PC's Java cache and retry, or install/use a different SMS version.
entries.keywords: parse error, SMS, network manager, java cache, sapphire, two brands, Shell configs, log in

entries.title: BP chip-card "partial failure" after VRSD 3.10.35 — downgrade
entries.answer: A "partial failure" on chip cards right after the BP VRSD upgrade to 3.10.35 was resolved by downgrading to the earlier version — the .35 build was the problem. Separately, when BP EPS won't take the card table on a startup, the recovery that worked: call Buypass for a new PDL, reboot all equipment and wait 10 minutes, do a trigger pull, wait 3 minutes, then apply the table (it succeeds) and download the EMV key; the URL shouldn't need changing.
entries.problem: Chip card "partial failure" after a BP VRSD upgrade; BP EPS table won't apply on startup.
entries.context: 3/31/20 Damian (VRSD 3.10.35); 10/13/20 startup notes (Buypass PDL, EPS table reload).
entries.solution: Downgrade from VRSD 3.10.35. For a stuck table: new PDL from Buypass → reboot all, wait 10 min → trigger pull → wait 3 min → apply table → EMV key download (URL unchanged).
entries.keywords: partial failure, BP, VRSD 3.10.35, chip card, downgrade, Buypass, PDL, trigger pull, apply table, EMV key, startup

entries.title: Named Commander Base 43.09 forecourt patches (with KB numbers)
entries.answer: A short list of named Commander Base 43.09 patches for specific forecourt problems — handy when you know the symptom and need the exact patch/KB to request from the Helpdesk. The notable ones: Gilbarco "fuel bad iRC" line-noise fix (KB 15770, mandatory at Gilbarco sites); double-book transactions from AUX fuel positions/PIC (KB 15835, mandatory with Aux Fuel Points/PIC); DCR screen going blank after a card swipe so "Debit Y/N" or "Debit or Credit" never shows (KB 15702); DRW fuel-point move, stuck reserve, and FP-offline issues (KB 15838); and discount price not returning to street price after fueling on Tokheim/Bennett DCRs (KB 15836, mandatory at Tokheim/Bennett sites). All are applied by the Helpdesk.
entries.problem: Finding the right Commander Base 43.09 patch for a specific forecourt symptom.
entries.context: Verifone patch sheet (text recovered via OCR). All patches need T3/supervisor approval.
entries.solution: Match the symptom to the KB: Gilbarco fuel bad iRC → 15770; AUX/PIC double-book → 15835; DCR screen blank after swipe → 15702; DRW stop/move/stuck reserve/FP offline → 15838; Tokheim/Bennett discount-not-returning → 15836.
entries.keywords: Commander patch, Base 43.09, Gilbarco, fuel bad iRC, double book, AUX fuel, PIC, DCR screen blank, DRW, stuck reserve, Tokheim, Bennett, KB 15770, 15835, 15702, 15838, 15836

entries.title: Can't close shift or day — "Waiting" / "Time Out" on a register
entries.answer: When a shift or daily close won't finish — errors, a timeout, or you get bounced straight back to "Close Shift/Daily" — the message tells you where to look. "WAITING on register X" means a workstation is still in Sales; make sure every register has exited Sales. "TIME OUT on register X" means that register isn't responding: reset comm 3 on all registers, check the workstation for printer lockups or other hangs, and power-cycle it. If it then comes up to "Unable to download — continue Y/N?", call the Verifone Petro Helpdesk.
entries.problem: Unable to close shift/day; errors, timeout, or bounced back to the close prompt.
entries.context: Ruby/Sapphire, Topaz/Sapphire; Verifone article 6368 (Ruby or Ruby/Sapphire).
entries.solution: "Waiting on register X" → exit Sales on all workstations. "Time out on register X" → reset comm 3 on all registers, clear printer/lockups, power-cycle the workstation. If "Unable to download continue Y/N?" → call the Petro Helpdesk.
entries.keywords: can't close shift, close day failed, waiting on register, time out, reset comm 3, power cycle, unable to download, period close

entries.title: New cash drawer adapter (22839-01) — never plug in hot
entries.answer: The big gotcha with cash drawers: a newer drawer built for Topaz/Ruby2 uses adapter P/N 22839-01 to work on a Ruby, and that adapter must never be plugged in "hot" — connecting it while the Ruby is powered on will blow the port. (A blown adapter isn't from using the wrong port.) Two versions of the adapter share the same part number; the troublesome one is identified by its revision (22839-01 rev A). Power the Ruby down before connecting, and check the adapter revision. The article also carries general cash-drawer troubleshooting and part numbers.
entries.problem: Replacement cash drawer not working / blown port.
entries.context: Commander, RubyCI, Topaz, Sapphire, Ruby; Verifone article 000003978. Adapter 22839-01 (rev A is the problem version).
entries.solution: Power the Ruby OFF before connecting the 22839-01 adapter — never hot-plug it (it blows the port). Check the adapter revision.
entries.keywords: cash drawer, adapter, 22839-01, hot plug, blown port, Ruby, Topaz, Ruby2, revision A, part number
entries.source: Petro - Cash Drawer - General Information and Troubleshooting.pdf

      Customer Solution
      UPDATE: Per Anna H. in ESG, POP Discounts go in order of how they appear in the POP
      Discount file. So if you have two discounts for the same PLU entered, for example, it will
      only use the first discount listed.

https://verifone.force.com/vk/s/article/PetroFuelDiscountsPOPDiscountCashCreditPricing              4/28/2020
 Petro - Fuel Discounts - POP Discount - Cash Credit Pricing                                        Page 2 of 4

       1. Note: Always use your Manager Manual for the specific software version the site has when
          setting up ANY of these discounts.
       2. A: If site wants to discount fuel per gallon, determine "qualifier" the customer needs.
       3. Cash/Credit pricing vs. Pop Discount
       4. CASH/CREDIT PRICING SETUP

        • The most basic Fuel Discount- (Cashier presses a Fuel Discount Key during POSTPAY
          sale):In Manager ->Configuration -> Sales Configuration: Option 7 (typically) is "Fuel
          Discount". This can be set up on a Soft Key (it's under the "Other" section). Option 7 is the
          ONLY setting in Sales Configuration for the fuel discount.
        • All other Discount settings there are for another Discount key. The fuel discount key is set
          up per gallon.
        • If a site wants to do a different kind of discount for fuel, ask these questions.
             ◦ Do you want to discount the fuel itself (per gallon) or discount merchandise when they
              buy fuel?
        • If they want to discount fuel per gallon, go to Step A. If they want to discount merchandise,
          go to Step B.
        • If Site is running VIPER and they want pop discount based on card type, then the option
          "POP BY CARD TYPE ENABLED" must be set to Yes

        • For example, do they want to discount:
        • By using Cash to buy the fuel? (If so, Cash/Credit pricing or Pop Discount can be set up)
        • By using a certain type of card (Gift card, certain credit card, etc.)? (Pop Discount would
          need to be set up for this)By buying a quantity of a PLU or Department item? (Pop
          Discount)

        • Cash/Credit pricing is typcially only recommended for sites with dual PPU's (The ability to
          show 2 separate prices at the pump for the same grade of fuel) but can be used for single
          PPU sites as well
        • In a Cash/Credit pricing setup, Debit cards and Gift Cards will automatically use the cash
          price.
        • Pop Discount is used for all other cash/credit pricing scenarios.

      Go To Fuel Manager -> Fuel Product Prices

       1. Type in the different Cash and Credit prices for each grade of fuel
       2. Go to Fuel Manager -> Site Parameters -> Fuel Site Parameters
       3. Set "Ignore MOP Conflict" to No
       4. Go To Fuel Manager -> Initialize Fuel -> Full Initialization
       5. Go To Fuel Manager -> Initialize DCRs -> Download to ALL DCRs
       6. OPTIONAL: Site can set up a Soft Key (set up as an "Other Key") for CONVERT which can
          convert the price between cash and credit on a postpay sale.

      SEE ATTACHED for the Pop Discount setup sheet.

       1. B: If site wants to discount merchandise after buying fuel, they can set up a PLU
          Promotion:

        • Each PLU itself needs to be enabled for PLU Promotion within PLU File
        • Go to Manager -> PLU Promotion File and follow the steps for the setup.

        • NOTE: For BP POP Discount by card type (card types and initials from the POP card

        • Card Type Card Type Initials

        • BP FUELING BL

      With split tender the POP discount is not given unless the second MOP/card type is also

entries.title: "License Not Supported" scanning a driver's license (2D / ID check)
entries.answer: "License Not Supported" when scanning a driver's license for an ID check usually means the 2D barcode isn't being read. Confirm the site actually has a Verifone-configured 2D scanner, then make sure you're scanning the 2D barcode and not the linear one — covering the linear barcode with a finger forces the scanner onto the 2D code. It can be state-specific: some states' IDs read fine while others don't. If it still won't read, call the Helpdesk.
entries.problem: 2D scanner won't read a driver's license for ID check; "License Not Supported" (varies by issuing state).
entries.context: Topaz, 2D scanner, Ruby2, Commander, Sapphire, RubyCI; Verifone article 000005267.
entries.solution: Verify a Verifone-configured 2D scanner; scan the 2D barcode (cover the linear barcode to force it); call the Helpdesk if it persists.
entries.keywords: license not supported, 2D scanner, driver license, ID check, linear barcode, age verification, state, Topaz

entries.title: Apple Pay / contactless setup on MX8xx and MX915
entries.answer: Getting Apple Pay (contactless) working depends on the pinpad. On 8-series pinpads the contactless attachment has to be ordered with the unit — it can't be added in the field. On an MX915, contactless must be enabled in the Card Table; if it isn't, the network has to enable it (it only needs to be supported on the network end — no download required). Then confirm the NFC reader actually works: Diagnostics → Card → Contactless Reader, and test with a phone that has Apple Pay (the NFC antenna sits directly beneath the screen, so lay the phone on the screen). Make sure Contactless is enabled/checked in the config.
entries.problem: Apple Pay / contactless not working on the pinpad.
entries.context: MX8XX, MX9XX; Verifone article 000006697.
entries.solution: 8-series: contactless attachment must be factory-ordered (no field install). MX915: enable contactless in the Card Table (or have the network enable it — network-side support only, no download). Verify the NFC reader via Diagnostics → Card → Contactless Reader (test with an Apple Pay phone on the screen).
entries.keywords: Apple Pay, contactless, NFC, MX915, MX8xx, card table, contactless reader, diagnostics, network enable, attachment

entries.title: Topaz 410 "System Failed to Load FPGA" (solid yellow screen)
entries.answer: A Topaz 410 that boots to a solid yellow screen reading "System Failed to Load FPGA …! Contact VeriFone support" needs a full power-down first: disconnect power and wait for every light — including STATUS and HEART BEAT — to go out completely, then power back up. If it comes back, a VASC attempts a software reload; if it persists, the unit likely needs hardware replacement.
entries.problem: Topaz 410 boots to a solid yellow screen: "System Failed to Load FPGA …! Contact VeriFone support."
entries.context: Topaz model 410, possibly Ruby2; Verifone article 000006900.
entries.solution: Disconnect power; wait for all lights (incl. STATUS, HEART BEAT) to go out; power back up. VASC software reload; hardware replacement if it persists.
entries.keywords: Topaz 410, FPGA, system failed to load, yellow screen, power down, status, heartbeat, VASC, reload, hardware

entries.title: Chevron pinpad field notes — prompts, Apple Pay, AID table
entries.answer: A cluster of Chevron pinpad oddities worth recognizing. One: a flow where insert → enter PIN → remove card, then a few seconds later it prompts "Correct Amount Y/N?" after the customer has already walked away (look at the PIN_SELECT / prompt setup). Two: Apple Pay not being taken as debit, with no PIN prompt. Three: "AID Table Update Failed" after swapping a pp102 pinpad. Treat them as separate items — AID Table Update Failed points at re-running the table/key update on the replaced pinpad, and the Apple-Pay-as-debit behavior ties back to card-table and PIN-select configuration.
entries.problem: Assorted Chevron pinpad issues (late amount prompt, Apple Pay not as debit, AID Table Update Failed).
entries.context: 4/30/20 Ryan (1023 Sandy Plains); 5/14–5/18/20 Justin (1727 Mars Hill, after replacing pp102).
entries.solution: AID Table Update Failed after a pp102 swap → re-run the AID/table update on the new pinpad. Late "Correct Amount Y/N?" / Apple-Pay-as-debit → review PIN_SELECT and card-table config.
entries.keywords: Chevron, pinpad, correct amount, PIN_SELECT, Apple Pay debit, AID table update failed, pp102, card table

entries.problem: Comprehensive PIN pad reference: types (1000SE, MX8xx, MX915), functionality, and troubleshooting, plus attachments (MX config, PetroPay MX8, MX915 TCP port edit via 159+pw, MX900 smart-card reader test).
entries.solution: Look up the specific code or message in the table below for its description and corrective action.

Petro - PINPad - Troubleshooting and General Info                                              Page 1 of 7

  Add to Favorites

                                          Article 7182 Attachments

                                   8 Documents are attached to this article.

    MX900 Smart Card Reader Test PICTURE - Download                                   Tier 1

                                              Article 7182 Details

                                                Article Title
  Petro - PINPad - Troubleshooting and General Info
                                               Problem/Task
      • Types of PINPads
      • Functionality
      • Troubleshooting Steps
                                                Environment
      • PINpads
      • 1000SE
      • MX 800 series (MX 8XX)
      • MX830
      • MX850
      • MX860
      • MX870
      • MX915
      • Ruby
      • Sapphire
      • Topaz
      • Commander
      • RubyCI
      • Ruby2
      • All Software Applications

                                                    Cause
  Insufficient User Rights
                                       Customer Solution/Action
      • PIN pads are a small number entry device attached to the register. These devices are
        used to enter encrypted information such as pin numbers for debit cards. They also
        can be used for other data entry, like driver id numbers and odometer readings for
        fleet card users.
      • Newer PIN pads also are designed for the customer to swipe their own card on the
        PIN pad itself, without handing the card to the cashier.
      • PIN pads require a powered port setup on the register, unless they use a separate
        power connection.
      • Topaz sites require the PIN pad to be enabled in CSR Functions -> Network Manager
        -> PIN pad Configuration -> Select PIN pad.
      • There are a few standard encryption types, Master Session, DUKPT and Triple DES.
        Each network/software has its own separate encryption types. For Example, a

http://clientservices.veriorder.com/index.cfm?fuseaction=view.articledetail&id=7182             4/18/2018
 Petro - PINPad - Troubleshooting and General Info                                            Page 2 of 7

        BUYPAK site with a DUKPT Encrypted PIN pad will NOT work with an XOMPAK site,
        even if it is also set up to use DUKPT injection.
      • The Injected Petro PIN pad article contains the correct encryption types. PIN pads will
        be encrypted (also known as injected) from the factory, which means it cannot be
        changed at the site or with a VASC. The PIN pads that come from the factory will have
        an injection sticker that starts with "A-KEY-PCS-______" where the part after PCS is
        what the network and encryption is.

  NOTE : Before proceeding with the following troubleshooting steps, contact the Helpdesk if
  your location has an Onsite contract or if further assistance is needed.

  COMMON PROBLEMS AND TROUBLESHOOTING

  (only replace pinpads if site has on-site contract, otherwise refer the site to their local
  VASC)

  Locations that have on-site contracts will have a pinpad request done by ERS (MRA)
  Request

  INVALID MEMORY/ INVALID MKEY

     1. Unplug the Power supply from the Terminal.
     2. Unplug the PIN Pad cable .
     3. Check if all the contacts on the cable and Pin Pad are clean and in good condition.
     4. Replug the PIN Pad to the Terminal making sure the arrow is on Top of the
       extension.
     5. Replug the Power supply.
     6. Perform a test transaction.
     7. If problem persists, replace PIN Pad with cable.

  PINPAD NOT RESPONDING OR NO RESPONSE FROM PIN

  On MX915, you can press 1,5,9 at the same time. It will prompt for a password, type in
  166832, Enter. Once it comes up, press 5 then press 2, it will restart the app. If that fails.....

     1. Unplug the Power supply from the Terminal.
     2. Unplug the PIN Pad cable.
     3. Check if all the contacts on the cable and Pin Pad are clean and in good condition.
     4. Ensure that the buttons on the Pin Pad are clean and not sticking. A stuck key will
       cause the Pin pad to become unresponsive.
     5. Replug the PIN Pad to the Terminal making sure the arrow is on Top of the
       extension.
     6. Replug the Power supply.
     7. Perform a test transaction.
     8. If problem persists, replace PIN Pad with cable.

  TRANSACTION NOT ALLOWED

     1. Unplug the Power supply from the Terminal.
     2. Unplug the PIN Pad cable. Check if all the contacts on the cable and Pin Pad are
       clean and in good condition.
     3. Replug the PIN Pad to the Terminal making sure the arrow is on Top of the
       extension.
     4. Replug the Power supply.
     5. Perform a test transaction.
     6. If problem persists, replace PIN Pad with cable.
     7. In case, problem is not resolved, replace terminal.

  PINPAD REQUIRED TO CONTINUE/ NO POWER TO PINPAD

http://clientservices.veriorder.com/index.cfm?fuseaction=view.articledetail&id=7182             4/18/2018
 Petro - PINPad - Troubleshooting and General Info                                         Page 3 of 7

     1.    Unplug the Power supply from the Terminal.

entries.title: "Upgrade Available / PMC Not Ready" during Commander Auto-Upgrade
entries.answer: "Upgrade Available / PMC Not Ready" during a Commander Auto-Upgrade is, per the article, a Helpdesk call (article 000006233). The practical note on this one: make sure INCOM is disabled — an active INCOM can leave PMC not ready going into the upgrade.
entries.problem: "PMC Not Ready" returned during a Commander Auto-Upgrade.
entries.context: Commander Auto-Upgrade; Verifone article 000006233. Filename note: disable INCOM.
entries.solution: Disable INCOM, then retry the upgrade; contact the Helpdesk if it persists.
entries.keywords: PMC not ready, upgrade available, Commander auto-upgrade, INCOM disabled, helpdesk
entries.source: PMC NOT READY MAKE SURE INCOM IS DISABLED.pdf

entries.title: "PMC Not Ready" — make sure the new MNSP router is fully functional first
entries.answer: Another "PMC Not Ready" cause is doing the software upgrade before the network gear is ready. When you do an MNSP upgrade or router swap, make sure the new router (MNSP) is fully functional before starting the software upgrade. Shiraz hit this at a BP and again at an Exxon.
entries.problem: "PMC Not Ready" when upgrading after an MNSP/router change.
entries.context: 3/2/21 Shiraz (749 S Marietta Pkwy, Green Oil BP); recurred at an Exxon.
entries.solution: Confirm the new MNSP router is fully functional before starting the software upgrade.
entries.keywords: PMC not ready, MNSP, router swap, upgrade, fully functional, Commander, network gear
entries.source: PMC NOT READY MAKE SURE MNSP IS FULLY FUNCTIONAL.xlsx

entries.title: "POP Comm Error 102" / blank MX915 — payment NIC dropping FEP comm
entries.answer: "POP Comm Error 102" with a blue/blank MX915 screen (seen on Chase Paymentech base 53.40) traces to the payment NIC dropping FEP comm rather than an actual network outage — the Cradlepoint showed no drops over 11 days, yet EPS saw FEP comm dropping from the payment NIC. The immediate recovery is to reboot the pinpad, which clears it each time. Things that did NOT solve it: swapping the serial cable for Ethernet, moving the reboot time to 3:00/3:30 AM, and reloading ViperPay/OS. Verifone noted a lot of pinpad reboots on Chase 53.40, so on that version treat recurring POP Comm Error 102 as the known payment-NIC drop and reboot the pinpad.
entries.problem: "POP Comm Error 102," blue/blank MX915 pinpad screen.
entries.context: 2/20/23 Sal/Mehdi, Circle K #246 (Covington); Chase Paymentech base 53.40. Per Erin: many pinpad reboots with Chase 53.40.
entries.solution: Reboot the pinpad to recover. (Serial→Ethernet, reboot-time changes, and ViperPay/OS reloads did not fix it; root cause is the payment NIC dropping FEP comm.)
entries.keywords: POP comm error 102, MX915, blank screen, Chase Paymentech, 53.40, payment NIC, FEP comm, Cradlepoint, pinpad reboot, ViperPay

entries.title: "Preset not allowed" — full-fuel init with all pumps idle (and Fuel Site Pause)
entries.answer: "Preset not allowed" is cleared by a clean full-fuel initialization — but every pump has to be idle first. Make sure no prepay, postpay, or pay-at-pump sale is set, running, or finishing while you initialize, then clear all fueling positions to idle and run the full-fuel init. One catch around close time: Automatic End Of Day (AEOD) can be configured to disable fuel for the duration of the close, so "preset not allowed" during AEOD is expected. If it continues past the AEOD window, check Fuel Site Pause (CSR FUNC → 3. Fuel Manager Menu → 9. Fuel Site Pause) — it must be set to disabled/false for fuel to work. If none of that fixes it, call the Helpdesk.
entries.problem: "Preset not allowed" on fuel.
entries.context: AEOD can disable fuel during close; Fuel Site Pause can leave fuel paused.
entries.solution: Clear all fueling positions to idle (no active prepay/postpay/PAP) → full-fuel init. If it persists past AEOD, set CSR FUNC → 3. Fuel Manager → 9. Fuel Site Pause to disabled/false. Else Helpdesk.
entries.keywords: preset not allowed, full fuel initialization, idle pumps, AEOD, automatic end of day, fuel site pause, fuel manager

entries.title: Receipt printing on .43 software — uncheck customer receipt
entries.answer: A receipt-printing quirk on .43 software (same notes as [088]): one receipt prints a small credit receipt and a second prints on sales over $25. Unchecking the customer-receipt setting stops the small one — you then get no small receipt and a single receipt over $25.
entries.problem: Extra small credit receipt printing on .43 software.
entries.context: 10/23/20, Wildco .43 software. Overlaps [088].
entries.solution: Uncheck the customer-receipt setting (no small receipt; one receipt over $25).
entries.keywords: receipt copy, customer receipt, small credit receipt, .43 software, over $25, Wildco

entries.title: Registering a RubyCI / getting a gemkey — call Registration
entries.answer: Registering a RubyCI (and getting it to generate a gemkey) is handled by Verifone's Registration department, not the VASC line or the standard Helpdesk — and the web portal frequently fails to produce a gemkey, returning only a case number. Call Registration at 1-800-519-7225, option 2. (1-888-297-7604 is Sales, not registration.)
entries.problem: RubyCI registration; portal won't generate a gemkey (only a case number).
entries.context: Verifone chat, 9/8/2015; case 150908-7937.
entries.solution: Contact Verifone Registration: 1-800-519-7225, option 2.
entries.keywords: registration, RubyCI, gemkey, portal, case number, 1-800-519-7225, registration department, sales number

entries.title: "Remote Service Is Down" on Topaz/Ruby2 — checks then ordered power-up
entries.answer: "Remote Service Is Down" on a Topaz or Ruby2 is worked through a power-up sequence after some quick checks. First confirm the date/time on the Sapphire (via SMS) and try to ping the Topaz/Ruby2. Reseat the Ethernet at the Sapphire/Commander, the router, and every Topaz/Ruby2, and try logging out and back in. If it's still down, power everything off — router, Sapphire/Commander, and all terminals — then bring it back in order: router first, then the Sapphire/Commander, and once the Sapphire reaches A5, power the Topaz terminals up one at a time. Log out and back in; the error should be gone. Check for stuck transactions and JLogs on the terminals and the Sapphire/Commander. If the terminal is up but the pumps are grey, run a full fuel initialization. Still down? Call the Helpdesk.
entries.problem: "Remote Service Is Down" / "Remote Services Down" on Topaz or Ruby2.
entries.context: Topaz/Ruby2, Sapphire/HPV-20, router, Commander; Verifone article 000004472.
entries.solution: Check Sapphire time (SMS) and ping; reseat Ethernet everywhere; log out/in. If still down, power off all → power up router → Sapphire/Commander → (at A5) Topaz one at a time → log out/in. Check stuck transactions/JLogs. Grey pumps → full fuel init. Else Helpdesk.
entries.keywords: remote service is down, Topaz, Ruby2, Sapphire, A5, reseat Ethernet, power up sequence, ping, stuck transactions, JLogs, full fuel init
entries.source: REMOTE SERVICE IS DOWN - Error Message.pdf

entries.title: Block credit on lottery the simple way — change the product code
entries.answer: The cleanest way to keep credit off lottery is the product code, not a rule. Change the Lottery department's product code from "General Merchandise" to "Lottery" — that alone blocks credit cards on lottery automatically (Verifone's Chris Lane did exactly this on site #4405888 and confirmed credit was refused on lottery afterward). If the straight product-code change doesn't hold for a particular site, fall back to Rule Manager ([045]).
entries.problem: Restrict credit cards on lottery purchases.
entries.context: Email thread, 2/20/2019, Verifone site #4405888 (Chris Lane, T2). Third method alongside [045] (Rule Manager) and [130] (network product restriction).
entries.solution: Set the Lottery department's product code to "Lottery" (not "General Merchandise"); this blocks credit automatically. Fall back to Rule Manager if needed.
entries.keywords: restrict credit, lottery, product code, general merchandise, department, rule manager, debit cash only

entries.title: Reset a site's secure/manager password — VASC tunnels in
entries.answer: To reset a site's secure/manager password, have a VASC tunnel in and reset it — you'll need to enable the login on the unit first so they can connect. They set a new manager password and read it back to you. (Passwords are site-specific; treat any value from a chat as one-time.)
entries.problem: Need to reset a site's secure/manager password.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 9/25/2015 (Exxon, Rex GA). VASC tunneled in after login was enabled.
entries.solution: Enable the login on the unit; have a VASC tunnel in and reset the manager/secure password.
entries.keywords: reset password, secure password, manager password, VASC tunnel, enable login, Exxon

entries.title: "Roll Up DB Reports" setting (Period Configuration)
entries.answer: "Roll Up DB Reports" lives in Reports Manager → Report Configuration → Period Configuration, and it controls whether a period's database totals carry forward into the next period. Shift always rolls up into Day, so the option isn't offered for Shift; for the other periods, set Roll Up DB Reports to Yes to roll that period's data into the next one. The same Period Configuration screen also sets the delay between closes, the unit of measure, and the close-security level.
entries.problem: Understanding/using the Roll Up DB Reports option.
entries.context: Reports Manager → Report Configuration → Period Configuration (text recovered via OCR).
entries.solution: Set Roll Up DB Reports = Yes for a period to roll its totals into the next period (Shift→Day is automatic).
entries.keywords: roll up DB, reports, period configuration, report configuration, reports manager, close security, delay between close

entries.title: Ruby2 screens blank/unresponsive — duplicate of [126]
entries.answer: Another copy of the "Ruby 2 screens going blank/dark and unresponsive" alert. See [126]: document the 2-digit 7-seg status code and notify T3 before rebooting; the issue ties to the screensaver.
entries.problem: Ruby2 screen blank/dark/unresponsive (duplicate alert).
entries.context: Same alert as [126]/[177].
entries.keywords: Ruby2, blank screen, unresponsive, screensaver, status code, T3, duplicate

entries.title: Lockup cases now include Topaz 410
entries.answer: This is the lockup-cases alert ([037]) with one important addition: Topaz 410 units (part number M050-02-410-NAA) share the same SATA-DOM and similar internals as the Ruby2, so they can show the same lockups — and unlike Ruby2/CI, Verifone is still requesting logs for Topaz 410 lockups. Everything else holds: the genuine lockup has the heartbeat solid or fully off (a flashing heartbeat points to sunlight, the screensaver bug, version mismatch, or loyalty auth), and the serial for the ERS step comes from running status on the RubyCI/unit.
entries.problem: Commander/Ruby2/CI/Topaz410 lockups (updated alert including Topaz 410).
entries.context: Verifone alert; references article 15207. Topaz 410 = M050-02-410-NAA. Logs still requested for Topaz 410.
entries.solution: Same as [037]; for Topaz 410, still pull logs. Genuine lockup = heartbeat solid/off; flashing → sunlight/screensaver/version/loyalty.
entries.keywords: lockup, Topaz 410, M050-02-410-NAA, Ruby2, RubyCI, SATA DOM, heartbeat, logs, article 15207, ESG

entries.title: Ruby2 screens blank/unresponsive — duplicate of [126]
entries.answer: Another copy of the "Ruby 2 screens going blank/dark and unresponsive" alert. See [126].
entries.problem: Ruby2 screen blank/dark/unresponsive (duplicate alert).
entries.context: Same alert as [126]/[175].
entries.keywords: Ruby2, blank screen, unresponsive, screensaver, status code, T3, duplicate

entries.title: Handling customer follow-ups on open Ruby2 lockup cases
entries.answer: This is the approved way to handle a customer following up on an open Ruby2 lockup case in ESG escalation: don't ping ESG for a status — read the standard reassurance (engineering is working to find root cause and will make contact once it's known; senior management is monitoring) and don't comment further. New cases still escalate normally. The symptom set that defines the issue: the Ruby2 heartbeat is solid or off, you can't remotely connect, the screen doesn't respond or make any sound to button presses, and the soft-reset button doesn't reset.
entries.problem: What to tell a customer chasing an open Ruby2 lockup case.
entries.context: Verifone alert (customer-communication script). Cases sit in ESG escalation.
entries.solution: Use the standard reassurance statement; don't ask ESG for updates; escalate new cases normally. Symptoms: heartbeat solid/off, no remote connect, no response/sound to buttons, soft reset fails.
entries.keywords: Ruby2 lockup, ESG, escalation, customer update, heartbeat, remote connect, soft reset, no response, script

entries.title: "Screensaver issue" message after reboot — known bug
entries.answer: The "screensaver issue" message — seen after rebooting a Shell RubyCI on 2.00.05, where it popped up once — is a known bug Verifone was working on, and it shows up on ExxonMobil software too. There wasn't a fix at the time, only a hoped-for patch. It belongs to the broader Ruby2 screensaver/lockup family ([037], [126]); if a site keeps hitting it, that's the context.
entries.problem: "Screensaver issue" message appears (often after a reboot).
entries.context: Verifone chat, 6/2/2016 (Shell RubyCI 2.00.05); confirmed known issue, also on ExxonMobil; case 160602-19305.
entries.solution: Known bug awaiting a patch — no config fix. Relate to the Ruby2 screensaver/lockup issue ([037], [126]).
entries.keywords: screensaver issue, known bug, RubyCI, Shell 2.00.05, ExxonMobil, reboot, patch, lockup

entries.title: "One-To-One Portal application error" on Verifone Premier
entries.answer: A "One-To-One Portal application error" on the Verifone Premier portal (here under Serial Inquiry) is a portal-side fault, not your account or the device. Use Go Back and retry, try a different Premier function, or use Contact Us if it keeps failing — there's nothing to fix on the site end.
entries.problem: "One-To-One Portal application error" using Serial Inquiry on Verifone Premier.
entries.context: Verifone Premier portal, 10/23/2017.
entries.solution: Portal-side error — Go Back and retry, try another function, or use Contact Us. No site-side fix.
entries.keywords: Premier portal, one-to-one portal application error, serial inquiry, Verifone, web portal, retry

entries.title: Shell Vantage pinpad stuck on "Swipe Payment Card" (15-min EPS timeout)
entries.answer: On Shell Vantage (Wincor EPS), the pinpad can appear stuck on "Swipe Payment Card" for what feels like forever — it's actually a 15-minute EPS timeout. It triggers when the cashier rings items, presses tender, selects "No" to loyalty, gets the "Swipe Payment Card" prompt, and then the card isn't swiped within 3 minutes. To avoid the long lock, void the transaction to cancel the EPS request before the 3-minute mark, and re-ring it when it can be finished promptly.
entries.problem: Pinpad locked on "Swipe Payment Card," ~15 minutes to time out.
entries.context: Commander/RubyCI, Shell Vantage, Wincor EPS; Verifone article 15268. 3-minute no-swipe triggers a 15-minute timeout.
entries.solution: Void the transaction before the 3-minute mark to cancel the EPS request; re-ring when it can be completed quickly.
entries.keywords: swipe payment card, stuck pinpad, 15 minute timeout, Shell Vantage, Wincor EPS, loyalty no, void transaction, 3 minutes
entries.source: SHELL COMMANDER PIN PAD GETTING STUCK.pdf

entries.title: Shell pinpad won't communicate / US Debit — reload files, refresh EPS config
entries.answer: A Shell pinpad that won't communicate with the register — VHQ throwing a "post pay load" error — came back after reloading all the Shell pinpad files. For the related US Debit issue, push an EPS network config update: Network Manager → Refresh EPS Configuration (option #9 in 2.09.21). This was resolved without escalating to Shell Level 2.
entries.problem: Shell pinpad won't communicate with the register ("post pay load" in VHQ); US Debit issue.
entries.context: 5/19/20 Justin, 2489 Dallas Hwy. Software 2.09.21.
entries.solution: Reload all Shell pinpad files. For US Debit: Network Manager → Refresh EPS Configuration (#9 in 2.09.21).
entries.keywords: Shell pinpad, won't communicate, post pay load, VHQ, reload pinpad files, US debit, refresh EPS configuration, network manager

entries.title: Wayne printers offline every morning (pattern)
entries.answer: A recurring pattern worth recognizing: Wayne receipt printers going offline every morning, across every printer type and firmware (DW12, DW14, clamshell, Zebra) and at multiple sites. The note logs it as an open pattern without a settled fix — and it has the fingerprint of an overnight event (close/reboot or comm timing) rather than a bad printer, since type and firmware don't change it. The morning recovery is to reinitialize the DCRs/printers; if it's chronic, look at what runs overnight (AEOD/reboot timing) rather than swapping printers.
entries.problem: All Wayne printers offline every morning, regardless of type/firmware.
entries.context: 4/7/20 (Kimia's, Flat Shoals, DW12); 5/4/20 (Premier Chevron, Reinhardt); 5/14/20 — recurring at multiple sites.
entries.solution: Reinitialize the DCRs/printers in the morning. Investigate overnight processes (AEOD/reboot/comm timing) rather than the printer hardware (type/firmware don't matter).
entries.keywords: Wayne printers, offline every morning, DW12, DW14, clamshell, Zebra, DCR, reinitialize, overnight, AEOD

entries.title: Shell Commander install — Vantage box handles comms (minimal network config)
entries.answer: On a Shell Commander/RubyCI install there's almost no network configuration to do on the Verifone side, because Shell sites use the Wincor Vantage box for communications. The real work is physical: configure the cabling and set up the Zone Router accordingly. The "Shell Commander Network Configuration Guide" and similar KB docs that show as "document restricted" are an access/profile matter — Verifone's training group controls that access and may need to adjust your administrator profile.
entries.problem: What network configuration is needed for a Shell Commander install; restricted KB docs.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 9/1/2015 (first Shell RubyCI). Shell uses the Wincor Vantage box for comms.
entries.solution: Minimal POS-side network config — set up cabling and the Zone Router. For restricted KB docs, have Verifone training adjust your admin profile.
entries.keywords: Shell Commander, Vantage box, Wincor, network configuration, zone router, cabling, restricted document, RubyCI

entries.title: Shell Commander install field tips (PEI forum)
entries.answer: Field tips from techs doing Shell Commander installs: bring a monitor with a DVI connector and a USB mouse for the Wincor Vantage box — showing up without the DVI monitor is a bad surprise. The upgrade itself is quick across the common combos (Ruby2CI with Gilbarco, Ruby2-with-Commander with Gilbarco, Ruby2CI with Tokheim Premier B / VxDHC). One open question raised: single-line card reader loyalty prompting that was automatic on Sapphire may need keys programmed on Commander.
entries.problem: Practical gotchas for Shell Commander installs.
entries.context: Petroleum Equipment Institute forum thread, Aug 2015.
entries.solution: Bring a DVI-connector monitor and a USB mouse for the Wincor Vantage box. Expect to program loyalty keys for single-line card readers on Commander (was automatic on Sapphire).
entries.keywords: Shell Commander, install, DVI monitor, USB mouse, Wincor Vantage, Gilbarco, Tokheim, single line card reader, loyalty, PEI forum

entries.title: Credit prepay won't reset Tokheim dispenser (debit fine) — site profile
entries.answer: A Tokheim site where you can prepay with debit but not credit — credit won't let the dispenser reset, so the customer lifts the handle, it won't reset, they hang it up and it comes due inside as $0 — is not a pump problem. Because it resets fine for debit, the trouble is on the Shell/Vantage side: a site profile issue. It gets escalated to Shell Vantage Level 2 (Verifone won't make update callbacks; if Shell takes the case they contact the site).
entries.problem: Credit prepay won't reset the dispenser (comes due inside at $0); debit prepay works.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 12/10/2015 (Shell, Doraville, Tokheim dispensers); case 151210-18893.
entries.solution: Not a pump issue (debit resets fine) — it's a Shell site profile problem; escalate to Shell Vantage Level 2.
entries.keywords: credit won't reset, Tokheim, prepay, due inside $0, debit works, site profile, Shell Vantage Level 2, dispenser

entries.title: Ruby locking up in System Setup (v950 dial-in) — software corruption
entries.answer: A Ruby locking up in System Setup, paired with trouble dialing into the v950, usually means software corruption — and it can be on either the Sapphire or the Ruby. The way forward is to dial in and determine which unit is corrupt, then reload that one.
entries.problem: Ruby locks up in System Setup; trouble dialing into the v950.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 8/13/2015 (Shell, Roosevelt Hwy); case 150812-11784.
entries.solution: Dial in to identify whether the Sapphire or the Ruby has the corruption, then reload the affected unit.
entries.keywords: Ruby lockup, system setup, v950, dial in, corruption, sapphire, reload

entries.title: "Site Settlement Failed: BP Mobile… Report Available" — bounce the mobile host
entries.answer: "Site Settlement Failed: BP Mobile… Report Available" clears by bouncing the mobile-payment host. Go to Payment Controller → Mobile Payment Configuration, open the Host Configuration tab, uncheck Enabled Host and Save, then re-check Enable Host and Save. Have the site log out and back in on the POS. The message itself goes away after the next day close. If it keeps coming back, call in for deeper troubleshooting.
entries.problem: "Site Settlement Failed: BP Mobile... Report Available."
entries.context: Verifone chat, 2/22/2023 (Amoco, Summerville GA; Base 53.40, Buypass 3.12.40); case 08464056.
entries.solution: Payment Controller → Mobile Payment Configuration → Host Configuration: uncheck Enabled Host → Save → re-check Enable Host → Save. Log out/in on POS. Message clears after the next day close.
entries.keywords: site settlement failed, BP mobile, report available, mobile payment configuration, host configuration, enable host, day close, Amoco

entries.title: "No billto address found" on Verifone Premier (Site Finder)
entries.answer: "No billto address found for the account" on the Verifone Premier portal (here under Site Finder) is an account-data problem, not a device fault — the account is missing a bill-to address. There's nothing to fix on the site; use Contact Us to have the account's billing address added.
entries.problem: "No billto address found for the account" on Premier Site Finder.
entries.context: Verifone Premier portal, 2017.
entries.solution: Account-side issue — contact Verifone (Contact Us) to add a bill-to address to the account.
entries.keywords: Premier portal, no billto address, site finder, account, billing address, Verifone, contact us

entries.title: "Site ID Not Set" after a PDL (Sapphire upgrade / V910)
entries.answer: "Site ID Not Set" on network/credit transactions after pulling a PDL — seen while upgrading a Sapphire and adding a V910 — is a Helpdesk item (Verifone article 9029). It shows up on EPSPAK Conoco/Chevron (Viper) after the PDL; call the Helpdesk to get the Site ID set.
entries.problem: "Site ID Not Set" on network/credit transactions after a PDL.
entries.context: EPSPAK, Conoco/Chevron, Viper, V910; while upgrading a Sapphire/adding a V910. Verifone article 9029.
entries.solution: Call the Helpdesk to set the Site ID.
entries.keywords: site ID not set, PDL, Sapphire upgrade, V910, EPSPAK, Conoco, Chevron, Viper, helpdesk

entries.title: Configuring SkuPos — and it can drop the network
entries.answer: To configure SkuPos, log into Config Client on the back-office computer and call SkuPos to set it up. One gotcha: SkuPos can knock the network offline — if that happens, disconnecting the back-office computer cleared it.
entries.problem: SkuPos configuration; SkuPos taking the network offline.
entries.context: 2/9/21, Jeremy.
entries.solution: Log into Config Client on the back-office PC; call SkuPos to configure. If the network goes offline, disconnect the back-office computer.
entries.keywords: SkuPos, Config Client, back office computer, network offline, disconnect, configure

entries.title: After SPSPAK rebrand, outside card reads fail (inside works) — network response
entries.answer: After rebranding to SPSPAK with new Wayne dispensers (the site had no card readers before), inside card prepays work but every outside (DCR) card attempt fails. The outside failure is a response code generated by the network, not a POS setting — nothing on the POS would cause it. Pull another parameter download, and contact the SPS network; they may need to adjust something on their end.
entries.problem: Outside (DCR) card transactions fail every time after an SPSPAK rebrand; inside works.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 3/11/2016 (rebranded Marina, SPSPAK 3.01.01, single Ruby + Sapphire, new Wayne dispensers); case 160311-26224.
entries.solution: Pull another parameter download; contact the SPS network to adjust on their end (it's a network response code, no POS fix).
entries.keywords: SPS network, SPSPAK, outside card fail, DCR, parameter download, network response code, Wayne, rebrand, inside works

entries.title: "SSL Sign-On Unsuccessful" on EPSPAK / V910
entries.answer: "SSL Sign-On Unsuccessful" on EPSPAK 3.00.xx / V910 (often right after a fresh install or upgrade) is part of the same V910 "cannot process credit/debit" article (8341). Work it like [085]: check the V910 LEDs and DIP switches, then run an Echo Test and Check Host Status. Important: get Tier-3 approval before doing a hard reset on a V910 or a Sapphire Newpro. V920 is now supported, and the same article also applies to COPPAK/EPSPAK.
entries.problem: "SSL Sign-On Unsuccessful" / can't process credit/debit on EPSPAK 3.00.xx / V910 after a fresh install or upgrade.
entries.context: CVXPAK/EPSPAK (also COPPAK), V910/V920, Ruby/Topaz; Verifone article 8341 (the VASC doc is [085]).
entries.solution: Follow [085] (LEDs, DIP switches, Echo Test, Check Host Status). Get T3 approval before any hard reset of a V910 or Sapphire Newpro.
entries.keywords: SSL sign on unsuccessful, EPSPAK 3.00, V910, V920, CVXPAK, COPPAK, echo test, hard reset, Tier 3, fresh install, upgrade

entries.title: Commander stuck on A8 after a soft reset — hard power cycle
entries.answer: A Commander stuck on A8 after someone hit the soft-reset button on the front recovers with a hard power cycle — pull power and bring it back up — so you don't necessarily need a reload. (A8 means the OS is installed but no application is running; see [167].)
entries.problem: Commander stuck on A8 after the site pressed the front soft-reset.
entries.context: Email, 3/14/2023, per Shiraz Tharani (475 Windy Hill Rd, Next Level Chevron).
entries.solution: Hard power cycle the Commander (often recovers without a reload).
entries.keywords: stuck on A8, Commander, soft reset, hard power cycle, reload, A8 status, OS no application

entries.title: "Synchronizing API" hang — reload ViperPay, check pinpad IP/port
entries.answer: For a "Synchronizing API" hang, reload ViperPay and check the pinpad networking: confirm the pinpad IP and that it's on port 5000, then run a POP Init. Loading the latest ViperPay (4.06.02.03 here, for Exxon/Buypass) ran without problems.
entries.problem: Stuck "Synchronizing API."
entries.context: 9/20/19, Randall; ViperPay 4.06.02.03 for Exxon/Buypass.
entries.solution: Reload ViperPay; verify pinpad IP and port 5000; run POP Init.
entries.keywords: synchronizing API, ViperPay, reload, pinpad IP, port 5000, POP init, Exxon, Buypass

entries.title: Tank Reconciliation Report shows no tanks / all zeros
entries.answer: A Tank Reconciliation Report that shows no tanks and all zeros on the EOD — common right after a Commander install — has two things to check. First the obvious: tank rec needs actual fuel transactions to have run; with no sales it's zeros by design. Second, if it lists no tanks at all, the tank gauge (ATG/TLS) isn't communicating — verify the Zone Router is up and running so the controller can reach the gauge.
entries.problem: Tank Reconciliation Report shows no tanks and zeros on the End of Day report.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 9/18/2015 (Shell, Northside Dr, Atlanta; Commander newly installed); case 150918-23911.
entries.solution: Confirm fuel transactions actually ran (no sales = zeros). If no tanks are listed, check that the Zone Router is up so the tank gauge (ATG/TLS) can communicate.
entries.keywords: tank reconciliation, zeros, no tanks, ATG, TLS, tank gauge, zone router, end of day, Commander install

entries.title: "Terminal Full" on secure user apps — the VASC procedure
entries.answer: "Terminal Full" on secure user-based apps (CITPAK 5.00.00+, MARPAK, SPSPAK, ADSPAK) is a settlement backlog, and the VASC procedure is methodical — note each step you do for the Helpdesk. Print the current-date network log: "Ts" and rtb-restarts mean a true terminal-full; an "X" means a comms/modem problem instead. Confirm batches sitting in Pending (Network Manager → Batch Detail). Pull a parameter download (Network Manager → Configuration) — it should succeed; if it fails, treat it as a network-comms problem. Conference the network processor and check what they're seeing — complete transactions, pre-auths only, or nothing — and grab timestamps of the last complete transaction and pre-auth that reached them. Reboot the register (give it two minutes), then run a credit card; if it still says terminal full, have the site close the day. If it persists after close, the site uses their secure ID to go backdoor into Dealer Configuration, set Enable Report Masking to No, then Store and Forward → Print Oldest Batch (you must Print — Delete won't print the batch info), verify all card data printed, and note the batch number before deleting.
entries.problem: "Terminal Full" on secure user-based applications.
entries.context: CITPAK 5.00.00+, MARPAK, SPSPAK, ADSPAK. VASC procedure.
entries.solution: Network log (Ts/rtb-restart = true full; X = comms). Check Pending batches; pull a parameter download (fail = network issue). Conference the processor (note last complete/preauth timestamps). Reboot register 2 min; run a card; close day if needed. Persisting: secure-ID backdoor → Dealer Config → Enable Report Masking = No → Store and Forward → Print Oldest Batch (print, don't delete) → verify card data printed → note batch number.
entries.keywords: terminal full, CITPAK, MARPAK, SPSPAK, ADSPAK, network log, Ts, rtb restart, pending batch, parameter download, store and forward, print oldest batch, report masking, close day

entries.title: Test a Topaz cash drawer (and check for a blown port)
entries.answer: To test a Topaz cash drawer, first make sure the cash-drawer comport connection is secure, and if you have a spare drawer, swap it to tell whether the fault is the drawer or the Topaz. To test whether the Topaz's drawer ports are blown, put the Topaz in Diag mode (flip dipswitch 1 down and reboot), touch the screen when prompted, choose Dolphin — the two cash-drawer selections are greyed out — and type 92768 ("Topaz" backwards). The cash-drawer selections become visible; pick the port the drawer is plugged into and run the test. A good port opens the drawer.
entries.problem: Testing a Topaz cash drawer / checking if a drawer port is blown.
entries.context: VASC instructions.
entries.solution: Secure the comport; swap a known-good drawer to isolate. To test ports: Diag mode (dipswitch 1 down, reboot) → touch screen → Dolphin → type 92768 → select the drawer's port → run test (success opens the drawer).
entries.keywords: Topaz cash drawer, test, blown port, diag mode, dipswitch 1, Dolphin, 92768, comport, drawer opens
entries.source: Testinig a Topaz Cash Drawer - VASC Instructions.docx

entries.title: Gemcom Extended Error Code (GEEC) reference
entries.problem: Structure and value ranges of Gemcom Extended Error Codes (seeeff format; even=PC source, odd=POS source), severity levels, and the EEC_* code list (e.g. EEC_I_OK=0, EEC_E_NOTFND_RECORD=30, EEC_E_FULL_POS_DSTFIL=31).
entries.solution: Look up the specific code or message in the table below for its description and corrective action.
entries.table:
The Gemcom Extended Error Code (GEEC) values are designed to convey specific information concerning the nature of the error.  Each value consists of three constituents:
seeeff
where:
Note:  if this value is even, the "source" of the error is a function on the PC -- if odd, the "source" of the error is a function on the POS.
These values will fall into the following ranges:
The calling program should act accordingly.
