entries.title: POS time keeps reverting after you fix it — change it on the network
entries.answer: When you correct the POS time and it snaps back — here it kept returning to September 9, off 14 hours — the network is pushing the wrong time, so fixing it locally won't hold. Confirm whether it's wrong on all the Topazes, enable the login (CSR Func → Maintenance Menu → #16 Enable Login → Yes) so support can get in, and check the date after a credit/debit transaction. If it still reverts, the time has to be changed on the network side — conference the network to correct it.
entries.problem: Site time is wrong and reverts shortly after being corrected (off 14 hours).
entries.context: Verifone chat, 9/10/2015 (Shell, Lithia Springs); case 150910-17531. See also [001] (VIPER time/zone).
entries.solution: Enable Login (CSR Func → Maintenance Menu → #16). If the time reverts after a correction/transaction, conference the network to change it on their side.
entries.keywords: time wrong, time reverts, date wrong, off 14 hours, enable login, conference network, Topaz, network time

entries.title: Full DOM reload of a Base 53+ Ruby2 / Topaz 410
entries.answer: To do a full DOM reload on a Base 53+ Ruby2 or Topaz 410, you downgrade the OS first, then load the app. Boot into Diag mode and put the diag switch back up. Launch PuTTY and log in as user "installer," password RFSmaintXL. Run sudo /etc/install_tools/install_appfs and select "yes" to downgrade the operating system — but DO NOT REBOOT. Launch PSI for any pre-Base-53 application, set the install address to 192.168.31.193, and complete that install. Boot back into Diag mode to set the register ID as needed, flip the diag switch up, then open PSI for the Base 53 app you actually want and load it normally.
entries.problem: Full DOM reload procedure for a Base 53+ Ruby2 or Topaz 410.
entries.context: Requires PuTTY (installer / RFSmaintXL) and PSI; install address 192.168.31.193.
entries.solution: Diag mode (switch up) → PuTTY → sudo /etc/install_tools/install_appfs → yes to downgrade OS → DO NOT REBOOT → PSI install a pre-53 app (IP 192.168.31.193) → diag mode to set register ID → PSI load the Base 53 app normally.
entries.keywords: DOM reload, Ruby2, Topaz 410, Base 53, diag mode, PuTTY, installer, RFSmaintXL, install_appfs, downgrade OS, PSI, 192.168.31.193, register ID
entries.source: Topaz Ruby2 dom FULL reload process (1).docx

entries.title: EID reads all zeroes — reseat the System ID board
entries.answer: An EID reading all zeroes on a Commander/CI can't be fixed remotely — it lives on the System ID board. Power the Commander down and open the case; the System ID board is on the side of the CI unit where the A9 displays — a small board roughly 1"×2" with two screws and one wired connector. Reseat that cable connector, close it up, power on, and check. Verifone has corrected the EID via dial-in in the past, but the field fix is the reseat. Don't swap parts between Commander units: each CI carries one dedicated serial for both the CI and Ruby2 portions, so if the System ID board itself is bad the whole unit gets replaced (replacing just the RubyCI would terminate the warranty).
entries.problem: EID shows all zeroes (should be the unit's real EID).
entries.context: Verifone chat, 11/15/2023 (Amoco, Rex GA); brand-new install; case 09774532.
entries.solution: Power down, open case, reseat the System ID board's cable connector (board is on the CI side near the A9 display). If the board is bad, replace the whole CI unit (don't interchange parts — warranty). Verifone can sometimes correct the EID via dial-in.
entries.keywords: EID all zeroes, System ID board, Commander, CI, A9, reseat cable, dedicated serial, replace unit, warranty, dial in

entries.title: EID doesn't match the serial — on-site VASC with an OTP, Verifone remotes in
entries.answer: When a Commander's EID doesn't match the physical serial — here the unit carried a Ruby2's EID and the site couldn't on-board — it can't be corrected by dial-in. Verifone needs a VASC on site with an Enable Helpdesk Login OTP so they can remote in, verify the serial number, and correct the EID. Get the OTP generated on the unit, then have Verifone remote in to fix it. This is the same System-ID/EID territory as [202].
entries.problem: EID doesn't match the physical serial; site can't on-board.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 6/19/2024 (Chevron, Griffin GA); physical serial 216-345-897 vs Ruby2 EID 216-239-591; case 10651118. Not fixable via dial-in.
entries.solution: VASC on site generates an Enable Helpdesk Login OTP; Verifone remotes in to verify the serial and correct the EID.
entries.keywords: EID doesn't match, serial number, on-board, OTP, enable helpdesk login, VASC on site, remote in, Ruby2, Commander

entries.title: No "downgrade" document for an M400 — reload to the correct software
entries.answer: There's no document to downgrade an M400 (here from EVPay 2.06.00, loaded by mistake on Base 54) because you don't downgrade it — when an M400 gets the wrong version, the fix per ESG is to reload the PINpad to the correct software, not roll it back. That's why no downgrade doc exists on the portal.
entries.problem: Need to "downgrade" an M400 loaded with the wrong version; no portal document exists.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 6/25/2025; EVPay 2.06.00 mistakenly loaded on Base 54; case 12156025 (per ESG/supervisor).
entries.solution: Don't downgrade — reload the M400 PINpad with the correct software version.
entries.keywords: M400, downgrade, EVPay 2.06.00, Base 54, reload pinpad, correct software, no document, ESG
entries.source: transcript NO DOWNGRADE DOCUMENT M400 rev.txt

entries.answer: The full chat for the M400 downgrade question; resolution at [207] — reload the pinpad to the correct software rather than downgrading.
entries.problem: M400 wrong version (duplicate transcript of [207]).
entries.keywords: M400, downgrade, reload, duplicate, transcript

entries.title: "Printer Unavailable" on a C18 — COM swap, then replace the printer
entries.answer: "Printer Unavailable" on a C18 is worked as a comms problem, and a COM swap is the usual fix. The full checklist: power-cycle, reload, verify/replace the printer and cable, confirm the printer prints a test receipt and is set to 38400 baud, check for an Error light on the printer, and do a COM swap both on the back of the POS and in Config Client. If you've done all that — COM already swapped, no error light, cables and printer replaced — and it still won't talk to the register, the printer is the remaining suspect; replace it.
entries.problem: "Printer Unavailable" on a C18 (RP330 printer).
entries.context: Verifone chat, 6/25/2025 (BP, Newnan GA); RP330 at 38400 baud; case 12156149.
entries.solution: Power-cycle, reload, check/replace cable, verify test receipt + 38400 baud, check the printer error light, COM swap on the back of the POS and in Config Client. If still unavailable, replace the printer.
entries.keywords: printer unavailable, C18, RP330, 38400 baud, COM swap, Config Client, error light, replace printer, cable

entries.title: PWR435 (P400) vs PWR445 (M400) power supply — not interchangeable
entries.answer: The P400 power supply (PWR435-001-01-A) and the M400 power supply (PWR445-001-01-A) are not documented as interchangeable — so if a distributor ships the wrong one, get the correct PWR445 for the M400. Verifone had no cases either way and informally suggested trying it and monitoring for a day, but there's no official sign-off that a PWR435 is safe on an M400. Use the correct part.
entries.problem: Distributor shipped a PWR435 (P400 PSU) instead of a PWR445 (M400 PSU); are they interchangeable?
entries.context: Verifone chat, 6/10/2025; case 12087259. No documentation either way.
entries.solution: Use the correct PWR445-001-01-A on an M400. Not documented as interchangeable with the PWR435 (P400).
entries.keywords: PWR435, PWR445, P400, M400, power supply, interchangeable, part number, distributor

entries.title: Ruby2 "Status Error 25" — undocumented, call VFHD
entries.answer: Status error 25 on a Ruby2 isn't in the usual references — not the Ruby2 hardware guide, and the Base 53 Commander User Reference lists only a handful of status error codes (not this one). Verifone confirmed there's no comprehensive status-error-code guide for Commander/CI/Ruby2/Topaz, so error 25 has to be worked by calling VFHD with the account so they can look at the specific unit. For the codes that *are* documented, see the GemCom/HPV-20/GEEC references at [101], [087], and [199].
entries.problem: Ruby2 shows "status error 25"; not listed in the standard guides.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 8/31/2022 (Texaco, Stockbridge GA); case 07090303. Commander User Reference (Base 53) lists only ~5 status error codes.
entries.solution: No comprehensive status-error-code guide exists — call VFHD with the account to troubleshoot the specific unit.
entries.keywords: Ruby2, status error 25, undocumented, Commander user reference, status codes, VFHD, no guide

entries.title: Replaced pinpad won't take chip (forces swipe) — check the "Contact" box
entries.answer: A replaced pinpad that makes customers swipe instead of insert — won't take chip — often just has the "Contact" box unchecked. Check Contact in the card config, run Init EMV, log out and back in on the POS, and test. (A pinpad that tested good in the lab still needs this enabled on site.) Note the mirror image of [051]/[082]: you want Contact checked, but leave Contactless unchecked.
entries.problem: Newly replaced pinpad won't take chip cards — forces swipe.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 8/2/2021 (unbranded Buypass, Jasper GA); case 04401790. "Contact" was unchecked.
entries.solution: Check the "Contact" box, run Init EMV, log out/in on the POS, and test.
entries.keywords: won't take chip, swipe instead of chip, pinpad, contact checkbox, init EMV, log out in, replaced pinpad, EMV

entries.title: "Unable to load Ruby2/Topaz 410" stuck at A5/A8 — duplicate of [109]
entries.answer: Another "unable to load Ruby2/Topaz 410, stuck at A5" article (000006131) — same fix as [109]: load over the serial console in diag mode. Flip the diag switch down, reboot, connect a laptop to the router and the register's serial console port, and run the Petro Suite Installer with the default Ruby2/Topaz 410 IP. (A8 = OS installed, no app yet; see the status codes at [167].)

entries.title: "Unread block data" after an upgrade — reload with AutoUpgrade
entries.answer: "Unread block data" after an upgrade — blocking you from getting into Configuration Manager — cleared by reloading the register with AutoUpgrade. Reload via AutoUpgrade and Configuration Manager comes back.
entries.problem: "Unread block data" after an upgrade; can't get into Configuration Manager.
entries.solution: Reload the register with AutoUpgrade.
entries.keywords: unread block data, after upgrade, configuration manager, reload register, autoupgrade

entries.title: "Signal 11 caught" (segmentation fault)
entries.answer: "Signal 11 caught" is a segmentation fault — a program tried to read memory that wasn't assigned to it. On a Verifone POS it points one of two ways: a software bug (often surfacing during an install or upgrade) or faulty hardware, typically RAM or the DOM. Retry/reload the software, and if it keeps faulting, reseat and then replace the suspect memory/DOM. It's not a single-setting fix — treat it as "bad code path or bad hardware."
entries.problem: "Signal 11 caught" error, often during an install.
entries.context: Signal 11 = segmentation fault (invalid memory access). Generic reference article.
entries.solution: Reload/retry the software; if it persists, reseat then replace the RAM/DOM (faulty hardware).
entries.keywords: signal 11, segmentation fault, segfault, memory error, install, RAM, DOM, faulty hardware, software bug

entries.title: PLU promo end date won't move in Config Client — click the day, don't arrow
entries.answer: If a PLU promo's end date won't move past the current year in Config Client, it's the date-picker, not the software — the trick is how you pick the day. Change the year first, then click directly on the day number (the "31" box) to set it; that locks in, say, December 31, 2030. Pressing the arrow at the bottom of the calendar (or Enter) cancels the box instead of selecting, which is why the date never takes. Click the date, don't arrow to it.
entries.problem: PLU promo end date won't go past 12/31 of the current year in Config Client.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 4/4/2019 (Texaco, Suwanee GA; Ryko car wash, Base 47); case 190404-11023.
entries.solution: In the date picker, change the year then click the day box (e.g. "31") to select it. The arrow button / Enter cancels the selection.
entries.keywords: PLU promo, end date, Config Client, date picker, calendar, car wash, Ryko, won't change date, December 31
entries.source: vfi chat PLU PROMO END DATE 7110 McGINNIS FERRY.pdf

entries.title: Clear a stuck fuel sale / poll errors at the register
entries.answer: To clear a stuck fuel sale at the register, use the quick prepay-stop sequence: press the Pump #, press Prepay, and when the enter-amount window pops up, drag it out of the way and press Stop Fuel — all within a few seconds. If a pump throws E01 / "Error Processing Prepay," power-cycle the Commander to clear it. But if you're seeing poll errors across all the pumps at once and the RS485 board in the FCI has no lights, that's hardware on the forecourt side — the FCI/board needs a Commander tech on site, not a register fix.
entries.problem: Stuck fuel sales on specific pumps; then site-wide poll errors.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 3/20/2018 (Shell, N Druid Hills); case 180320-13105. RS485 board in the FCI had no lights.
entries.solution: Stuck sale: Pump # → Prepay → drag the amount window aside → Stop Fuel (within seconds). E01 → power-cycle the Commander. All-pumps poll errors + dark FCI RS485 board → FCI hardware, send a Commander tech.
entries.keywords: poll errors, stuck fuel sale, prepay stop, drag window, E01, error processing prepay, power cycle commander, FCI, RS485 board, no lights
entries.source: vfi chat POLL ERRORS 2080 NORTH DRUID HILLS.pdf

entries.title: Ruby2 stuck on the splash screen (A9) after reboot — reload
entries.answer: A Ruby2 register stuck on the Ruby2 splash screen after a reboot — won't ping, and a power-cycle plus a database rebuild and reboot didn't move it, status sitting at A9 — has one step left short of hardware: reload the register. When a database rebuild doesn't clear a stuck splash, the reload is the next move.
entries.problem: Ruby2 register stuck on the Ruby2 splash screen after a reboot (status A9).
entries.context: Verifone chat, 1/15/2019 (Mobil, Smyrna GA; register 102); case 190115-8609. Ping failed; power-cycle and database rebuild didn't help.
entries.solution: Reload the register (next step after a failed database rebuild).
entries.keywords: Ruby2, stuck on splash, Ruby2 screen, A9, reboot, can't ping, database rebuild, reload register
entries.source: vfi chat RUBY2 STUCK ON SPLASH SCREEN 3100 HIGHLANDS.pdf

entries.title: E3 on Topaz 410 after a SATA DOM swap — check the DOM's DIP switch
entries.answer: E3 on a Topaz 410 (or Ruby2) status after swapping the SATA DOM — with the heartbeat not flashing and no beep — points at the SATA DOM board, specifically its DIP switch. Power down, disconnect all cables, remove the two screws under the back near the COM ports, and tilt the top of the unit (with the screen) forward to get inside. Find the blue SATA DOM board and check the small DIP switch facing the front of the unit: it should sit toward the middle of the board; nudge it there with a non-conductive tool if it's off. If the switch is already correct and the board still won't read, suspect a bad SATA DOM (they can arrive DOA).
entries.problem: E3 on Topaz 410 status after replacing the SATA DOM; HB not flashing, no beep.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 8/15/2017 (Chevron, Cornelia GA, brand-new Topaz 410); case 170814-37081. VASC-only internal access.
entries.solution: Open the unit (2 screws under back near COM ports, tilt screen forward); on the blue SATA DOM board, set the front-facing DIP switch toward the middle. If correct and still no read, the SATA DOM is likely bad.
entries.keywords: E3, Topaz 410, SATA DOM, DIP switch, blue board, heartbeat, no beep, bad DOM, VASC, Ruby2

entries.title: DOA EZR (Cybera router) — call Verifone first, never factory-reset
entries.answer: When an EZR (Cybera router) looks dead on arrival, loop Verifone in before you replace it — they work with Cybera to troubleshoot and have Cybera confirm the DOA, which can often be done on the spot. One hard rule: never do the 3-second factory reset on an EZR unless Verifone tells you to. Doing the factory reset on three routers here stopped them from ever pulling their software update.
entries.problem: Dead-on-arrival EZR / Cybera routers; routers not updating software.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 7/19/2016 (6 sites with DOA EZRs); case 160719-8366. The 3-second factory reset left three routers unable to update.
entries.solution: Contact Verifone before replacing a DOA EZR (they confirm DOA with Cybera). Never perform the 3-second factory reset unless Verifone instructs.
entries.keywords: DOA, EZR, Cybera, router, factory reset, 3 second reset, software update, Verifone, replace

entries.title: "Must be 7 characters" on a password reset — fill all three fields first
entries.answer: A password reset that keeps rejecting a correct 7-character entry with "must be 7 characters" usually isn't the password — it's the site pressing OK/Enter before filling in all three fields. Make sure the original password, new password, and confirm password are all entered before hitting Enter, and the error clears.
entries.problem: Password reset rejects a valid 7-character password with "must be 7 characters."
entries.context: Verifone chat, 5/24/2017 (BP, Marietta GA). The site was pressing OK before filling all fields.
entries.solution: Enter original / new / confirm password in all three fields before pressing Enter.
entries.keywords: must be 7 characters, password reset, original new confirm, press enter, OK, BP

entries.title: MX915 hangs on the ViperPay splash — reload version, then suspect the pinpad
entries.answer: An MX915 that hangs on the ViperPay splash for minutes is worked as a software-vs-hardware split. If it eventually reboots normally on a second try, load a different ViperPay version to rule out a corrupt file for the version you have. If a fresh version still hangs, you're most likely looking at a bad pinpad — even the frustrating ones that come back from repair marked "No Trouble Found."
entries.problem: MX915 hangs on the ViperPay splash screen for minutes.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 2/21/2019 (Buypass, Commerce GA); case 190221-11138.
entries.solution: If it reboots OK on a retry, load a different ViperPay version to rule out a bad file. If it still hangs, the pinpad is likely bad.
entries.keywords: MX915, hangs, ViperPay, splash, reload version, bad pinpad, no trouble found, reboot

entries.title: Scanner stops after a VRSD upgrade (Base 46+) — set the port in Managed Modules
entries.answer: After a VRSD upgrade on Base 46+, a scanner (or other device) that quit working usually needs its port reassigned in Config Manager — the register backdoor route (Maintenance → Device Control) no longer works for this, which is also where the "Nothing to Commit" error comes from. Set it under Tools → Managed Modules: select the Register, select the Device, then Enable and Set the Config (the Host Names / device entries live here). It's easy to miss because the section isn't obvious at first glance.
entries.problem: Scanner quit after a VRSD upgrade; can't set the port via the register backdoor ("Nothing to Commit").
entries.context: Verifone chat, 10/12/2017 (Shell, Lilburn GA; Shell 2.04.08, Base 46.08); case 171012-23124. Same site/issue as [235].
entries.solution: Config Manager → Tools → Managed Modules → select the Register → select the Device → Enable and Set the Config (Host Names). See [120] and [235].
entries.keywords: scanner stopped, VRSD upgrade, Base 46, nothing to commit, managed modules, device control, port assignment, host names, Config Manager

entries.title: Fuel prepay drops to "0.00" and sticks — clear it, then chase the cause
entries.answer: A fuel prepay that drops to "0.00" and sticks (the amount frozen in black) gets cleared by a Tier-level clear of the stuck sale, after which the pump is operational again. When it keeps happening across different dispensers, it's not one bad pump — the usual causes are improper prepaying technique, incorrect fuel configuration, ISP/network problems making sales come due inside and stick, or a pump running under-spec firmware. There are patches for sales coming due inside, though not always for the version a site is on, so check fuel config and pump firmware alongside clearing the stuck sale.
entries.problem: Fuel prepay drops to "0.00" and sticks; recurs on different dispensers.
entries.solution: Tier-level clear of the stuck 0.00 sale. Then chase the recurring cause: prepay technique, fuel configuration, ISP/network (sales due inside), or under-spec pump firmware. Check for a "sales coming due inside" patch for the version.
entries.keywords: prepay dropping to 0.00, stuck fuel sale, black amount, VAPS 1.06.17, clear stuck sale, fuel config, pump firmware, due inside

entries.title: "EC" status on a Topaz 410 — loose components or hardware
entries.answer: "EC" on a Topaz 410 status generally means the same thing it does on a Commander — loose internal components or a hardware fault — even though it's unusual to see it on a Topaz. Reseat the internal components; if that doesn't change it (as here, on a unit only three months old), the unit needs to be replaced. There isn't a published list of these status codes beyond the common b1–b3 and A8/A9.
entries.problem: Topaz 410 shows "EC" on status and won't go into diagnostics.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 12/5/2017 (Shell, Atlanta; Topaz 410, 3 months old).
entries.solution: Reseat internal components; if no change, replace the unit. (No published status-code list beyond b1–b3, A8/A9 — see [167].)
entries.keywords: EC status, Topaz 410, loose components, hardware, reseat, won't enter diagnostics, replace, status codes

entries.title: Swapping the CI main board changes the EID — can't match the case serial
entries.answer: The EID lives on the CI main board, so swapping the main board (the one with the ports) changes the EID — and there's no way to make the new EID match the serial printed on the case. That mismatch is expected after a board swap and isn't itself a fault. What you can fix: the Service ID can be corrected by Verifone tunneling in, and VRSD connectivity re-verified (it passed here after the swap). Don't chase matching the EID to the case serial — it can't be done.
entries.problem: After swapping the CI main board, the EID changed and no longer matches the case serial.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 1/15/2020 (Buypass, Hogansville GA); case 00236481. Service ID corrected; VRSD verified.
entries.solution: Accept the new EID (it follows the main board). Have Verifone correct the Service ID and confirm VRSD check-in. The EID cannot be re-matched to the case serial.
entries.keywords: main board swap, EID changed, CI, case serial, service ID, VRSD, can't match, Commander, RubyCI
entries.source: vfichat SWAPPED MAIN BD CI SVC ID EID CHANGED.pdf

entries.title: "US Debit" prompt after a pinpad upgrade — update the EMV config
entries.answer: Getting a "US Debit" prompt after upgrading the pinpads is a card-config issue — the EMV/card configuration needs updating. Verifone updated the EMV config (you can have them tunnel in if no one has the Config Manager password) and then had the registers logged off and back on. Update the EMV config and log the registers out and in, and the US Debit prompt resolves.
entries.problem: "US Debit" prompt appears after upgrading the pinpads.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 11/3/2017 (Exxon, Tucker GA); case 171101-17943. EMV config updated via tunnel-in.
entries.solution: Update the EMV/card config (have Verifone tunnel in if the Config Manager password is unknown); log the registers off and back on.
entries.keywords: US debit, pinpad upgrade, EMV config, card configuration, log off on, Config Manager, tunnel in

entries.title: Offsite/remote access to Commander tools via the Cybera router
entries.answer: You can reach a Commander's back-office tools — Configuration Manager, Report Navigator, Transaction Viewer — from offsite by setting up remote access through the site's Cybera router (port-forwarding to the Commander). Techs on the PEI forum strongly recommend doing this at install time: it saves return trips when something needs a config tweak later or the owner changes their mind. The priority on the day is still getting the pumps/DCRs talking and the network online — but the offsite access pays for itself afterward.
entries.problem: Reaching Commander Config Manager / Report Navigator / Transaction Viewer from offsite.
entries.context: Petroleum Equipment Institute forum thread, 2016.
entries.solution: Set up remote access through the Cybera router (port-forward to the Commander); configure it during the install to avoid return visits.
entries.keywords: offsite connection, remote access, Cybera, Commander, Configuration Manager, Report Navigator, Transaction Viewer, port forward, install

entries.title: Tank Rec stays zeros with correct fuel setup — upgrade the Veeder-Root TLS
entries.answer: A Tank Reconciliation Report that stays zeros even though the fuel setup checks out (no NONE products) often comes down to the tank gauge, not the POS. Here Tier-3/Engineering concluded the Veeder-Root TLS needed upgrading because it wasn't communicating correctly with the Commander to hand over report data — the unit was on version 19 while current was around 33/34. Verifone doesn't track TLS versions, so get the version off the gauge and check with Veeder-Root support (1-888-561-7942) about the firmware upgrade. Continues the tank-rec case from [196].
entries.problem: Tank Reconciliation Report still zeros after fuel setup verified correct.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 9/25/2015 (Shell, Northside Dr; case 150924-45722); follows [196]. Veeder-Root TLS on version 19.
entries.solution: Upgrade the Veeder-Root TLS firmware (it wasn't passing report data to the Commander). Get the version from the gauge; contact Veeder-Root support (1-888-561-7942).
entries.keywords: tank reconciliation, zeros, Veeder-Root, TLS, ATG, firmware upgrade, version 19, Commander, report data, 1-888-561-7942

entries.title: Commander won't check in over VRSD — toggle the Service ID
entries.answer: A Commander that won't check in over VRSD after an install can be jogged loose by toggling the Service ID: change the Service ID on the Commander to something else and then change it back to the correct one. That refresh got the Commander checking in here without a full escalation.
entries.problem: Commander not checking in over VRSD after an install.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 5/10/2016 (Texaco, Dahlonega; Commander newly installed).
entries.solution: Change the Service ID on the Commander, then change it back to the correct one (a refresh) — it then checks in.
entries.keywords: VRSD, not checking in, Commander, service ID, change and change back, refresh, install

entries.title: VRSD failure with a PayMedia socket timeout — comms are blocked
entries.answer: A VRSD failure where the log shows the PayMedia request timing out — "COMMUNICATIONS ERROR… SocketTimeoutException: connect timed out" — with all other settings correct means the comms are being blocked, not misconfigured. Here it was the Prysm device blocking it. Confirm the router's port setup is correct; if the ports are right and it still times out, it's a network issue to chase on the router/firewall (Prysm) side.
entries.problem: VRSD won't connect; PayMedia connection fails with a socket timeout.
entries.context: Verifone chat, 5/24/2016 (Sunoco, Gillsville GA; RubyCI install); case 160524-12065. Comms blocked by the Prysm device.
entries.solution: Verify the router port setup. If ports are correct and PayMedia still times out, it's a network block (Prysm/firewall) to resolve on the network side.
entries.keywords: VRSD failure, PayMedia, socket timeout, connection failed, comms blocked, Prysm, router ports, network issue, Sunoco

entries.problem: Wayne dispenser Mode 30 authorization error codes (0-27+) explaining why a fueling-point authorization failed — e.g. 1 disqualified due to unit prices, 16 pump stopped, 17 authorize reserved, 18 pump offline, 26 preset/prepay invalid or 0 amount.

entries.title: Commander "EC / Please Check System WDT" (Article 13556) — duplicate of [038]/[046]
entries.answer: Another copy of the Commander "EC / Please Check System WDT" article (13556) — same fault and fix as [038] and [046]: power-cycle the Commander until the lights are fully off, reseat the SATA DOM, USB DOM, and SBC RAM, try a diag-mode reload, and return the unit for repair if it persists.
entries.problem: Commander won't boot — "EC / Please Check System WDT!!" (duplicate article).
entries.context: Commander Site Controller; Verifone article 13556 (attachments: sample EC message, Commander showing EC1). Same as [038]/[046].
entries.solution: See [038]/[046]: power-cycle; reseat SATA DOM, USB DOM, SBC RAM; diag-mode reload; repair if it persists.
entries.keywords: EC, check system WDT, watchdog, Commander, won't boot, SATA DOM, USB DOM, RAM, duplicate, article 13556

entries.title: Prepay vanishes when the handle is lifted (works on approval) — pump firmware
entries.answer: A prepay that vanishes from the register the moment the customer lifts the handle — no dispense, as if the sale never existed — while the same setup works fine on Approval points at the dispenser, not the POS. On the forum, an identical SHOPAK / Gilbarco Highline diesel case turned out to be pump firmware: the dispenser firmware was out of date (and possibly conflicting with the Verifone gear / PAM), and upgrading the pump to current firmware stopped it. Since it reports fine on approval and worked on an earlier setup, check and update the dispenser firmware.
entries.problem: Prepay disappears from the register when the handle is lifted; no dispense (works on Approval).
entries.context: Petroleum Equipment Institute forum, 2010. SHOPAK 5.00.09, Gilbarco Highline single-product diesel, PAM. Worked fine on an earlier setup (4.04.06).
entries.solution: Update the dispenser/pump firmware to current (the prior version conflicted/under-spec). It reports fine on Approval, so the POS side is OK.
entries.keywords: weird prepay, prepay disappears, handle lifted, no dispense, Gilbarco Highline, SHOPAK, pump firmware, PAM, approval, diesel

entries.title: Where to set scanner/device port assignments after a Base 46 VRSD upgrade
entries.answer: After a VRSD/Shell 2.04.08 upgrade (Base 46.08), a scanner that stopped working needs its port reassigned in Config Manager, because port assignments no longer live under Maintenance → Device Control with the backdoor passwords. The exact path: Tools → Manage Modules → select the register → select the device → Enable and Set the Config. (Same fix as [223]; related to the Base 46 com-port change at [120].)
entries.problem: Scanner stopped after a VRSD upgrade; can't find where to set port assignments.
entries.context: Verifone chat (Shell, Five Forks Trickum, Lilburn GA; Shell 2.04.08, Base 46.08); agent Berry Baldford. Same issue as [223].
entries.solution: Config Manager → Tools → Manage Modules → select the register → select the device → Enable and Set the Config. (Port assignments moved here from Maintenance → Device Control in Base 46.)
entries.keywords: scanner, port assignments, VRSD upgrade, Shell 2.04.08, Base 46.08, manage modules, device control, Config Manager, commit, enable set config
entries.source: Welcome to Verifone Chat Support COMMIT 5414 FIVE FORKS TRICKUM.docx
